symptom: Drive redirection cannot be disabled when using Context Based Redirection authentication context in AVD/Windows 365
rootCause: Known product limitation - drive redirection is not yet controllable via authentication context claims. Hotfix is pending
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Context Based Redirection

solution: Use alternative methods to restrict USB redirection on Windows 365 Cloud PC (e.g., Intune device configuration policies)
symptom: USB redirection cannot be disabled on Windows 365 Cloud PC when using Context Based Redirection
rootCause: Known product limitation - USB redirection control via authentication context is not supported on Windows 365 Cloud PC
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Context Based Redirection

symptom: Windows 365 Admin Insights returns 401 Unauthorized or 403 Forbidden errors - user cannot view insights data
rootCause: OBO (On-Behalf-Of) token acquisition failed; user lacks CloudPC.Read or CloudPC.ReadWrite permissions; tenant missing Microsoft Graph API consent
solution: Verify user has CloudPC.Read or CloudPC.ReadWrite permissions. Check Graph API consent for the tenant. In Kusto, query CloudPCEvent with ComponentName='ServiceHealthClient' and TraceLevel<=2 to confirm 401/403 errors.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Admin Highlights/Troubleshooting

symptom: High error rate on Windows 365 Service Health API - 5xx errors from downstream Graph API calls
rootCause: Microsoft Graph Service Health API outage, network connectivity issues, or tenant lacking Graph permissions
solution: Check Microsoft Graph service status. Verify tenant has Graph permissions. Query CloudPCEvent with ComponentName='ServiceHealthClient' and filter for 5xx status codes. Check for HttpRequestException or TaskCanceledException in error messages.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Admin Highlights/Troubleshooting

symptom: Intermittent timeout errors (TaskCanceledException) in Windows 365 Admin Insights API
rootCause: Downstream service (DFE or Service Health API) degradation, large data volume for tenant, or network latency
solution: Check DFE and Service Health API performance. Query CloudPCEvent for TaskCanceledException messages. Review error trend with daily aggregation query to identify time-based patterns. Consider increasing timeout if issue persists.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Admin Highlights/Troubleshooting

symptom: Specific subcategory not showing data in Windows 365 Admin Highlights/Insights
rootCause: Subcategory not enabled in EnabledHighlightSubcategories configuration, subcategory mapping missing in code, or DFE report not configured for the subcategory
solution: Check if subcategory is in EnabledHighlightSubcategories config. Verify subcategory mapping exists in code. Check DFE report availability for that subcategory. Also check if permission check is failing for the specific subcategory.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Admin Highlights/Troubleshooting

symptom: Windows 365 Cloud PCs suddenly deprovisioned or deallocated; existing Cloud PCs cannot be started by customers
rootCause: Tenant flagged as abuse (IsAbuse=true) or HighlySuspicious by fraud/abuse detection system; Cloud PCs deallocated when TrustLevel is HighlySuspicious or Fraud
solution: Check CPCD for IsAbuse flag and TrustLevel. For abuse: raise DFM Collab with FROG/ICE (Ops PE OneVet MPN T3). For fraud: engage CRCE (RiskOps). If decision reverted, ensure TrustLevel resets to Normal. If Cloud PCs were deprovisioned, coordinate recovery with SaaF/Engineering via ICM
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/DO NOT USE : Anti-fraud and Abuse Process

symptom: Windows 365 Cloud PC provisioning takes much longer than usual without failures; slow provisioning lane
rootCause: Tenant TrustLevel set to Suspicious or HighlySuspicious, moving provisioning to slow/super-slow lane with reduced daily limits (30 or 20 respectively)
solution: Verify TrustLevel in CPCD. If Suspicious: provisioning throttled but no deallocation. If HighlySuspicious with IsAbuse=false: Cloud PCs deallocated. Engage CRCE or FROG/ICE based on IsAbuse flag for adjudication. After clearing, TrustLevel should reset to Normal automatically
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/DO NOT USE : Anti-fraud and Abuse Process

symptom: Windows 365 Power Platform connector action or trigger fails with HTTP 401 Unauthorized
rootCause: User not properly authenticated, authentication token expired, or invalid credentials/connection for the Windows 365 connector
solution: 1. Verify user is signed in with valid credentials. 2. Refresh authentication token or re-authenticate. 3. Re-establish the connector connection. 4. Ensure connection uses the correct identity/account
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Power Platform Connector/Troubleshooting

symptom: Windows 365 Power Platform connector action fails with HTTP 403 Forbidden - access denied executing connector actions
rootCause: User lacks CloudPC.ReadWrite.All permission or has insufficient role assignments in Azure AD/Entra ID
solution: 1. Verify user's Entra ID role assignments. 2. Check CloudPC.ReadWrite.All permission: Azure Portal → Enterprise Applications → Windows 365 → API Permissions. 3. Assign Global Administrator, Cloud PC Administrator, or custom role with CloudPC.ReadWrite.All. 4. Allow up to 15 minutes for permission propagation
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Power Platform Connector/Troubleshooting

symptom: Windows 365 Power Platform connector trigger fails with HTTP 403 Forbidden - access denied configuring or using triggers
rootCause: User lacks Microsoft.CloudPC/Webhooks/Create and Microsoft.CloudPC/Webhooks/Delete permissions; custom role missing webhook permissions
solution: 1. Verify user's role and permissions. 2. For Global Admin/Cloud PC Admin: permissions should be default - verify role is active and not expired. 3. For custom roles: review role definition, ensure Microsoft.CloudPC/Webhooks/Create and Webhooks/Delete are included. 4. Allow up to 15 minutes for permission propagation
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Power Platform Connector/Troubleshooting

symptom: HTTP 403 Forbidden when executing Windows 365 Power Platform connector actions
rootCause: User lacks CloudPC.ReadWrite.All permission or insufficient role assignments
solution: 1) Verify Azure AD/Entra ID role assignments. 2) Check CloudPC.ReadWrite.All permission via Azure Portal > Enterprise Applications > Windows 365. 3) Assign Global Admin, Cloud PC Admin, or custom role with CloudPC.ReadWrite.All. 4) Wait up to 15 min for propagation.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Power Platform Connector/Troubleshooting
Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Reserve - User Provisioning/Troubleshooting
symptom: Windows 365 Reserve self-provisioning option (Set up my Cloud PC) not visible in Windows App - user cannot initiate Reserve Cloud PC provisioning
solution: 1) Try web client and app versions to isolate. 2) Wait a few minutes and refresh/restart client. 3) Verify user has self-provisioning enabled and check priority order of multiple settings. 4) Check CPCD > Settings > User Settings Policy Overview to validate. 5) Verify user has license + provisioning policy + remaining time. 6) If none resolve, file ICM - customers can still provision from Intune as workaround.
rootCause: User not included in scoped Entra ID group for self-provisioning setting; conflicting Windows App settings across multiple Entra groups; Windows App settings not yet delivered to client; user missing active Reserve license or provisioning policy

sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Reserve - User Provisioning/Troubleshooting
symptom: Windows 365 Reserve Cloud PC entry unexpectedly removed from Windows App - user loses access to Cloud PC without initiating deprovisioning
solution: 1) Check if user received a notification in Windows App. 2) Verify self-provisioning setting is still enabled and user is in correct group. 3) Check priority order of multiple user groups/settings. 4) Verify license + provisioning policy + remaining time via Reserve License Report. 5) If none explain it, file ICM for setting-to-Windows App communication issue. Customers can still provision from Intune.
rootCause: Self-provisioning setting was disabled or Entra group scope changed; usage period has expired and access was automatically revoked

symptom: HTTP 403 Forbidden error when executing Windows 365 Power Platform connector actions - access denied on CloudPC operations
rootCause: User lacks CloudPC.ReadWrite.All permission or has insufficient role assignments in Azure AD/Entra ID
solution: 1) Verify user Azure AD/Entra ID role assignments. 2) Check CloudPC.ReadWrite.All permission via Azure Portal > Enterprise Applications > Windows 365 > API Permissions. 3) Assign Global Administrator, Cloud PC Administrator, or custom role with CloudPC.ReadWrite.All. 4) Allow up to 15 minutes for permission propagation.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Power Platform Connector/Troubleshooting

symptom: HTTP 5xx server error when using Windows 365 Power Platform connector actions or triggers - internalServerError or serviceException
rootCause: Backend service issues, service deployment/configuration problems, or transient infrastructure failures
solution: 1) Retry operation after a few minutes for transient issues. 2) If persists, create ICM to CloudPC Service/Windows 365 IT Pro Experience with: full error response, request-id, client-request-id, timestamp, connector type, operation, tenant ID. 3) Use Power Automate flow checker to view error codes.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Power Platform Connector/Troubleshooting

symptom: Windows 365 Cloud PC resize fails early before processing begins
rootCause: Cloud PC not in Provisioned state before resize, or VM is Gen1 (Gen2 required)
solution: 1) Confirm VM is Gen2. 2) Confirm Cloud PC was in Provisioned state. 3) Retry resize (single retry).
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Resize V2/Troubleshooting

symptom: Windows 365 Cloud PC resize fails mid-process during snapshot or VM extension phase
rootCause: Snapshot creation failure or VM extension did not complete successfully during resize V2
solution: 1) Check snapshot creation status. 2) Validate VM extension completion. 3) Retry resize.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Resize V2/Troubleshooting

symptom: Windows 365 Cloud PC resize with group-based licensing shows license/VM spec mismatch after resize
rootCause: Group-based licensing assignment state inconsistency during resize V2 process
solution: 1) Validate license assignment state. 2) Trigger retry from MEM portal. 3) Escalate if license/VM spec mismatch persists.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Resize V2/Troubleshooting

symptom: Capture Screen option greyed out in Windows App on macOS when connecting to Cloud PC via Teams
rootCause: Windows App version not 2926, Screen Capture Redirection not enabled in Experimental settings, Screen Capture Protection or Teams screenshot restriction blocking, or RDPVCHost process not running on Cloud PC
solution: 1) Confirm Windows App version is 2926. 2) Enable Experimental > Screen Capture Redirection. 3) Check if Screen Capture Protection or Teams screenshot restriction is enabled. 4) Open Task Manager, end Windows Remote Desktop background process, launch beta RDPVCHost.exe. 5) Retest capture.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Screenshot Capture in Microsoft Teams via Windows App on macOS/Troubleshooting

symptom: Windows 365 Resize V2 fails with invalid disk size error when attempting to downsize Cloud PC
rootCause: Downsizing (reducing disk size) is not supported in Resize V2. Only equal or larger disk size SKU is allowed
solution: Resize only to equal or larger disk size SKU. Inform customer that downsizing is by-design not supported
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Resize V2/Troubleshooting

symptom: Mouse pairs successfully but cursor does not move inside remote session - Bluetooth authorization issue on iOS
rootCause: Bluetooth permission denied or revoked for Windows App, commonly after OS upgrades
solution: Navigate to iOS Privacy/Bluetooth settings, confirm Windows App has Bluetooth access enabled, toggle Bluetooth off and on, fully close and relaunch Windows App. Reinstalling app may reset permissions.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Surface Mouse Support on iOS/Troubleshooting

symptom: Cursor moves but clicks or scrolling do not work in Windows App on iOS
rootCause: Mouse profile not fully supported, partial HID compatibility, or custom mouse button mapping
solution: Test basic inputs (left-click, right-click, scroll wheel), confirm whether issue reproduces across sessions. Escalate if cursor works but input actions fail consistently.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Surface Mouse Support on iOS/Troubleshooting

symptom: Mouse disconnects randomly during Windows App session on iOS
rootCause: Bluetooth power management, low mouse battery, or iOS background restrictions killing connection
solution: Check mouse battery level, keep Windows App in foreground during testing, re-pair mouse and restart session. Collect disconnect frequency and whether it occurs only in Windows App.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Surface Mouse Support on iOS/Troubleshooting

symptom: UES Automatic Cleanup - customer reports missing user storage/profile data after cleanup ran unexpectedly
rootCause: Cleanup ran on unattached storage that exceeded the configured inactivity threshold (days), or forced deletion triggered after storage quota exceeded for prolonged period
solution: 1) Confirm cleanup is enabled on the policy. 2) Verify inactivity threshold and conditional flags. 3) Check if storage was unattached longer than configured threshold. 4) Validate whether forced deletion occurred due to prolonged exceeded usage. 5) Confirm customer acknowledged permanent deletion behavior. Note: cleanup runs every 24 hours, only deletes unattached storage.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/UES Automatic Cleanup/Troubleshooting

symptom: UES Automatic Cleanup not occurring when expected - unattached storage not being cleaned up despite cleanup being enabled
rootCause: Cleanup toggle may be disabled, storage may still be attached, conditional 'only when exceeded' flag blocking cleanup, or policy storage not being evaluated daily
solution: 1) Verify cleanup toggle is enabled in policy. 2) Confirm storage is unattached. 3) Validate whether conditional 'only when exceeded' flag is blocking cleanup. 4) Confirm policy storage is being evaluated daily.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/UES Automatic Cleanup/Troubleshooting

symptom: UES auto-expanding disk - user runs out of storage before expansion completes, especially at initial 4 GB size with large downloads/OneDrive sync/git clones
rootCause: Edge case where rapid large file operations (ISO downloads, OneDrive sync, git clones) fill disk faster than real-time expansion can keep up, more likely at initial 4 GB disk size
solution: This is a known edge case. Workaround: pre-expand disk by downloading smaller files first, or increase initial storage allocation. Admin can switch user to fixed-size storage temporarily if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/UES Disks automatically expand/FAQ

symptom: UES auto-expanding disk does not expand despite high disk usage - disk stays at current size
rootCause: Possible causes: Cloud Device Agent not healthy, policy/metadata settings misconfigured, expansion events not being triggered, or max size already reached
solution: 1) Confirm user has existing profile disk. 2) Validate storage type and max size config. 3) Check current disk size vs usage. 4) Review agent logs for expansion attempts. 5) Re-apply policy or metadata settings. 6) Validate Cloud Device Agent health. 7) Escalate to platform/storage engineering if expansion events missing.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/UES Disks automatically expand/Troubleshooting

symptom: UES auto-expanding disk expansion stops before reaching configured maximum size
rootCause: Potential issue with expansion logic, Cloud Device Agent health, or storage platform limitation
solution: 1) Validate max size configuration. 2) Check agent logs for expansion errors. 3) Re-apply policy or metadata settings. 4) Validate Cloud Device Agent health. 5) Escalate to platform or storage engineering if expansion events are missing.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/UES Disks automatically expand/Troubleshooting

symptom: Windows 365 Business Cloud PC does not resume after hibernation - session lost or requires reprovisioning after >1 hour inactivity
rootCause: Cloud PC fails to transition out of hibernation state, session state not preserved during hibernate cycle. This is part of the Business SKU revamp with integrated hibernation (Private Preview).
solution: 1) Confirm Cloud PC is Business SKU. 2) Reproduce by allowing >1 hour inactivity. 3) Validate reconnect behavior via browser. 4) Check if session resumes with prior state intact. 5) If session lost or requires reprovisioning, escalate. Reference existing W365 Business TSG for additional troubleshooting.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/W365 Business Revamp & Hibernation/Troubleshooting

symptom: W365 Monitoring (Radar) shows OS build version as Windows 10 instead of Windows 11
rootCause: Known display issue in Radar monitoring configuration page
solution: Known issue to be addressed in future update. Actual OS is correct, display-only problem.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/W365 Monitoring (Radar)/Troubleshooting

symptom: W365 Monitoring (Radar) filters lost when switching tabs, time range change resets data series and filter values
rootCause: Known UI issue - filters and data series not persisted across tab navigation
solution: Known issue. Workaround: re-apply filters after switching tabs.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/W365 Monitoring (Radar)/Troubleshooting

symptom: GCC customer reports loss of access to Windows 365 Portal (IWP)
rootCause: Windows 365 Portal deprecated for GCC, GCC support now built into Windows App
solution: Expected planned deprecation. Use Windows App instead. No migration required.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Windows App Support Updates for Government Clouds (GCC, GCCH, Fairfax, Mooncake)

symptom: W365 Business customer deleted CloudPCBPRT (Windows 365 BPRT Permanent User) account from AAD, causing provisioning failure for licensed users
rootCause: The BPRT account is required for W365 Business provisioning. When deleted, the bulk token becomes orphaned and blocks re-provisioning even if the account is recreated.
solution: 1) Request CMDCPCSupport eligibility in OSP portal (24hr activation). 2) Find scale unit via Kusto: CloudPCEvent | where env_cloud_environment=="PROD" | where AccountId=="{tenantId}" | project env_cloud_name. 3) Use DMS client with YubiKey to request elevation: Request-AzureActiveDirectoryElevation.ps1 -GroupName CMDCPCSupport-JIT-PE-PlatformServiceOperator. 4) In Geneva Actions portal (Torus debug account), find AadProxy Service Actions > delete existing bulk token of given tenant, select PROD-{scaleunit} endpoint. 5) Verify deletion in Bulk Token Operations portal. 6) Customer must wait 24-48 hours before reassigning license.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/WCX Specific Content/OCE APIs/Bulk Token Deletion Process

symptom: Cloud PC deprovisioned after license expired; re-assigning license creates new Cloud PC instead of recovering old one
rootCause: License expiration triggers automatic deprovisioning; re-assignment provisions new workspace instead of recovering deprovisioned one
solution: Use Legacy Recovery OCE API: 1) Confirm license re-enabled (UserLicenseEntity_MV CapabilityStatus=Enabled). 2) Get old+new workspace IDs (WorkspaceEntity_MV). 3) Deprovision new workspace via Scheduler Service. 4) Wait for Lockbox approval. 5) Recover old workspace with RecoverReason=LicenseExpired. 6) Verify DisplayStatus=provisioned.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/WCX Specific Content/OCE APIs/Cloud PC Recovery from License Expiration/Legacy Recovery Steps

symptom: Cloud PC persistent RDP connection or app access issues unresolvable by standard troubleshooting
rootCause: Underlying Azure VM host/node issue
solution: Redeploy via OCE API: 1) Get TenantId/DeviceId from Kusto (RDAgentMetadata join DeviceEntity_MV). 2) CloudPC OCE > ResourceMgmt > Trigger Device Action, Type=16 (Redeploy). 3) Monitor in ASC.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Sandbox/WCX Specific Content/OCE APIs/CloudPC Redeploying

symptom: Windows 365 or AVD user cannot sign in - authentication failure during Cloud PC connection
rootCause: Authentication flow involves multiple Azure AD enterprise apps (different AppIDs for Web Client vs Windows Client vs RD Client). Failure can occur at any stage.
solution: Collect HAR traces (Chrome F12 or Fiddler). Find correlation ID from login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/v2.0/token request. Look up in ASC Tenant Explorer. Key AppIDs: Web=3b511579, WinClient=c76612c2, RDClient=a85cf173, VM Sign-in=372140e0.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Tools and Data Collection/Authentication & Identity

symptom: Cloud PC VM fails to boot or stuck on restarting
rootCause: Azure Host level changes - VMs running in certain Cluster or Host version may hit boot issue
solution: Engage RDOS DRI (OCE) via IcM to capture memory dump. Get NodeId/ContainerId/VMId from CPCD Tool or AzureCM.LogContainerSnapshot. AVD can use Serial Console first. Previous FC dump method unavailable since May 2023.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Tools and Data Collection/Dump Cloud PC

symptom: Application crash or hang inside Cloud PC or AVD session host
rootCause: Various application-level issues in guest OS
solution: Ask user to collect User-Mode Dump via Windows Error Reporting (WER) or Task Manager. Share via DTM. AVD can also use Diagnostic Settings > Crash Dumps. Fleet Diagnostic only for 1P VMs.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Tools and Data Collection/Dump Cloud PC

symptom: ConnectionFailedUserHasValidSessionButRDSHIsUnHealthy error when connecting to Cloud PC
rootCause: RDAgentBootLoader service is not running on the session host
solution: Collect Application Event Logs via Azure Support Center (ASC) to determine why RDBootLoader stopped, or file an IcM to SaaF for further investigation
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/Case Handling/Scoping/Delivery Strategy - Session Connectivity Issues

symptom: Cloud PC deleted/deprovisioned unexpectedly after grace period - active deletion
rootCause: W365 license or provisioning policy was removed/changed, triggering 7-day grace period followed by permanent active deletion
solution: Use CPCD Device Diagnostic and Action Diagnostic tabs to investigate timeline; active deletion is unrecoverable unlike passive deletion
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/ICM process & templates/Cloud PC recovery for passive deletion/Cloud PC active deletion review process

symptom: Cloud PC deleted/deprovisioned unexpectedly, end users lose access to original device, new Cloud PC reprovisioned after grace period
rootCause: W365 license or provisioning policy was removed or changed, triggering 7-day grace period followed by active deletion
solution: Verify both W365 license and provisioning policy are assigned to user. Remediate within 7-day grace period. Use CPCD Device Diagnostic (CPC Info Extended, VM Operations) and Action Diagnostic (Action Efficiency, Action Events) to investigate timeline. Active deletion is permanent and unrecoverable.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/ICM process & templates/Cloud PC recovery for passive deletion/Cloud PC active deletion review process

symptom: Cloud PC is deleted/deprovisioned and end users lose access; device enters grace period then gets permanently deleted after 7 days
rootCause: Provisioning policy removed or changed for the user, or W365 license removed/changed, causing Cloud PC to enter grace period followed by active deletion after 7 days
solution: Use CPCD (Cloud PC Diagnostics) to investigate: 1) Check Device Diagnostic tab for VM status and deletion timestamps, 2) Check Action Diagnostic tab for grace period and reprovision events, 3) Verify both requirements: valid W365 license AND valid Provisioning Policy assigned to user. Note: active deletion cannot be recovered - only passive deletion (license expiration/fraud) supports recovery
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/ICM process & templates/Cloud PC recovery for passive deletion/Cloud PC active deletion review process

symptom: Cloud PC unexpectedly reprovisioned when admin changes provisioning policy or adds user to secondary provisioning policy
rootCause: Adding user to a secondary provisioning policy or modifying the assigned provisioning policy triggers grace period on original Cloud PC and provisions a new one
solution: Advise customer not to remove or change provisioning policy assignment once Cloud PC is deployed. If using Group Based Licensing (GBL), follow the GBL resize guidance. Use CPCD Action Diagnostic tab to correlate provisioning policy changes with grace period events
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/ICM process & templates/Cloud PC recovery for passive deletion/Cloud PC active deletion review process

symptom: Cloud PC VM disk deleted due to service issue - need to recover managed disk
rootCause: Service issue caused active deletion of Cloud PC; managed disk may be recoverable via soft delete (if disk created >10 days ago and deleted <96 hours ago) or hard delete recovery (check IsRecoverable flag)
solution: For soft delete: file IcM to Support/APTS team. For hard delete: check IsRecoverable via Jarvis Action; if true, file IcM to Xstore/Triage team and engage GC on-call. Ultra Disk not supported for soft delete recovery.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/ICM process & templates/Cloud PC recovery for passive deletion/VM Disk Recovery Process

symptom: Cloud PC actively deleted/deprovisioned - users lose access to original device after grace period
rootCause: W365 license or Provisioning Policy was removed from user, triggering 7-day Grace Period followed by permanent active deletion
solution: Use CPCD Device Diagnostic to check LifecycleStatus and VM operations for ResourceOperation.DELETE. Use Action Diagnostic to trace GracePeriod/Delete/Reprovision events. Active deletion is NOT recoverable - only passive deletion (license expiration) can be recovered within 28 days.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/ICM process & templates/Cloud PC recovery for passive deletion/Cloud PC active deletion review process

symptom: Cloud PC(s) actively deleted/deprovisioned after 7-day grace period, end users lose access to original device and a new Cloud PC is reprovisioned
rootCause: W365 license removed/unassigned from user OR provisioning policy removed/changed, causing Cloud PC to enter 7-day grace period followed by permanent active deletion if not remediated
solution: Use CPCD (Cloud PC Diagnostics) to investigate: 1) Device Diagnostic tab - provide Device ID, check CPC Info Extended for LifecycleStatus/ProvisioningState, check VM Info for creation time, check VM Operations for ResourceOperation.DELETE. 2) Action Diagnostic tab - filter by UserID, check Action Efficiency for GracePeriod/Provision/Delete/Reprovision events with timestamps, check Action Events Overview for Device ID and Policy ID changes. Remediate within 7-day grace period by restoring license and/or provisioning policy. Note: active deletion is NOT recoverable (only passive deletion from license expiration can be recovered).
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/ICM process & templates/Cloud PC recovery for passive deletion/Cloud PC active deletion review process

symptom: Cloud PC managed disk deleted due to service issue (active deletion), need to recover disk data
rootCause: Service issue caused active deletion of Cloud PC; disk may be in soft-deleted state (created >10 days, deleted <96 hours ago) or hard-deleted state
solution: For soft-deleted disks: verify via DiskRPResourceLifecycleEvent Kusto query, file IcM to Support/APTS team. For hard-deleted: check Jarvis Get Blob Recovery Info IsRecoverable flag, if true file IcM to Xstore/Triage + engage GC on-call. Ultra Disk not supported for soft delete recovery.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/ICM process & templates/Cloud PC recovery for passive deletion/VM Disk Recovery Process

symptom: Error SubscriptionNotRegisteredForFeature when creating Public IP Address without registering AllowBringYourOwnPublicIpAddress feature for SFI compliance
rootCause: Subscription not registered for Microsoft.Network/AllowBringYourOwnPublicIpAddress feature required by SFI baseline for IP tagging
solution: 1. Register subscription for AllowBringYourOwnPublicIpAddress feature via SAW/AME account. 2. Verify with Get-AzProviderFeature. 3. Create IpTag with New-AzPublicIpTag -IpTagType FirstPartyUsage -Tag /Unprivileged. 4. Create IP with New-AzPublicIpAddress including -IpTag parameter
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/ICM process & templates/SFI Compliance

symptom: W365 Cloud PC provisioning is limited to 2 per week; customer cannot provision more Cloud PCs
rootCause: Tenant score is below 0.03, placing the tenant in the 'slow lane' due to anti-fraud tenant scoring
solution: Check tenant trust level via CPCD (https://aka.ms/cpcd) or Kusto query on DailyTenantEvaluation_Snapshot. If legitimate tenant, follow slow lane process flowchart to escalate for score adjustment.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/Processes and Collaboration/Windows 365 Anti Fraud and Abuse Process

symptom: All Cloud PCs under a W365 tenant are deallocated/stopped and customer cannot start them
rootCause: Tenant marked as 'highly suspicious' by anti-fraud system; Cloud PCs are automatically deallocated pending HIT (Human Investigation Team) review
solution: Check tenant HITStatus via Reporting Kusto query or CPCD. Follow suspicious tenant process: if confirmed legitimate, escalate to have fraud flag removed. If confirmed fraud, Cloud PCs will be deprovisioned.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/Processes and Collaboration/Windows 365 Anti Fraud and Abuse Process

symptom: All Cloud PCs under a W365 tenant are deprovisioned unexpectedly
rootCause: Tenant confirmed as fraud by HIT (Human Investigation Team); all Cloud PCs are automatically deprovisioned
solution: Check tenant fraud status via CPCD or Reporting Kusto. If fraud decision was incorrect, follow fraud-lifted process to re-enable subscription. Use 'Fraud Lifted' email template to notify customer.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/Processes and Collaboration/Windows 365 Anti Fraud and Abuse Process

symptom: Windows 365 Cloud PC provisioning throttled to 2 per week (slow lane) or tenant flagged as fraud/highly suspicious — need to check tenant trust level and score
rootCause: Tenant trust score below 0.03 places it in slow lane; highly suspicious tenants get Cloud PCs deallocated; confirmed fraud tenants get Cloud PCs deprovisioned
solution: Check tenant trust level via Kusto: CloudPCEvent where Col1 startswith "Evaluation: Called usage service" or DailyTenantEvaluation_Snapshot table. Use CPCD dashboard to view score history. Slow lane: [0, 0.03), Ordinary: [0.03, 0.9), Fast: [0.9, 1.0]. For fraud lifted: tenant re-enabled automatically. Customer email templates available in wiki.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/Processes and Collaboration/Windows 365 Anti Fraud and Abuse Process

symptom: Windows 365 Cloud PC provisioning is extremely slow or limited to only 2 per week
rootCause: Tenant score below 0.03, placing tenant in anti-fraud slow lane which limits provisioning to 2 Cloud PCs per week
solution: Check tenant trust level via Kusto (CloudPCEvent or DailyTenantEvaluation_Snapshot) or CPCD dashboard. If tenant is legitimate, follow slow lane escalation process to get tenant moved to ordinary/fast lane.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/Processes and Collaboration/Windows 365 Anti Fraud and Abuse Process

symptom: All Cloud PCs under tenant are deallocated/stopped and customer cannot start them
rootCause: Tenant marked as highly suspicious by anti-fraud system (HIT investigation pending), all Cloud PCs automatically deallocated
solution: Check tenant fraud status via CPCD dashboard and Kusto DailyTenantEvaluation_Snapshot. If HIT confirms fraud, Cloud PCs will be deprovisioned. If false positive, work with HIT to lift suspicious status and re-enable Cloud PCs.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/Processes and Collaboration/Windows 365 Anti Fraud and Abuse Process

symptom: All Cloud PCs under tenant are deprovisioned due to fraud confirmation
rootCause: Tenant confirmed as fraud by HIT (Human Investigation Team), triggering automatic deprovisioning of all Cloud PCs
solution: Check fraud status in CPCD. If fraud upheld: inform customer subscription remains disabled. If fraud decision is incorrect (false positive): escalate to get fraud status lifted and re-enable subscription.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/Processes and Collaboration/Windows 365 Anti Fraud and Abuse Process

symptom: Cloud PC provisioning limited to 2 per week, new provisioning requests fail or are throttled
rootCause: Tenant trust score is below 0.03, placing it in slow lane of anti-fraud system
solution: Check tenant score via CPCD (aka.ms/cpcd) or Kusto query on DailyTenantEvaluation_Snapshot table. If legitimate tenant, follow slow lane process to request trust level review.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/Processes and Collaboration/Windows 365 Anti Fraud and Abuse Process

symptom: All Cloud PCs under tenant are deallocated (stopped) and customer cannot start them, or Cloud PCs are deprovisioned
rootCause: Tenant marked as highly suspicious or confirmed fraud by Human Investigation Team (HIT)
solution: Check HIT status via CPCD or Kusto DailyTenantEvaluation_Snapshot. For suspicious: await HIT decision. For confirmed fraud upheld: inform customer subscription remains disabled. For fraud lifted: re-enable and apologize.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/Processes and Collaboration/Windows 365 Anti Fraud and Abuse Process

symptom: DMS Client command not recognized error (XXXX is not recognized) when running Customer Lockbox CLB operations in Torus
rootCause: DMS Client version is outdated and does not support the required CLB commands
solution: Upgrade DMS client using Update-TorusClient -Version 16.01.2079.008, then re-open the DMS Client
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/Processes and Collaboration/Windows 365 Customer Lockbox

symptom: 403 Permission Insufficient error when running Customer Lockbox (CLB) OCE operation in Windows 365
rootCause: The OCE does not have the CLB elevated claim for the target tenant, or the elevated claim has expired (4h default expiry)
solution: Request CLB elevated claim via DMS client: Set-MyWorkload Windows365, then Request-ElevatedAccess_V2.ps1 -Role AccessToCustomerData -Reason <reason> -Tenant <tenantId> -RequestNumber <ICM>. Wait for both Microsoft Manager and Customer approval before running the operation. Use GetCLBResult to verify claim.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/Processes and Collaboration/Windows 365 Customer Lockbox

symptom: Windows 365 Cloud PC provisioning limited to 2 per week; tenant placed in slow provisioning lane
rootCause: Tenant score below 0.03 (range 0-1) triggers slow lane classification, limiting provisioning to 2 Cloud PCs per week instead of normal 8 per 5 min
solution: Check tenant score via CPCD (aka.ms/cpcd) or Kusto DailyTenantEvaluation_Snapshot. If legitimate tenant, follow slow lane process to request HIT review. Normal lane requires score >= 0.03, fast lane >= 0.9
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/Processes and Collaboration/Windows 365 Anti Fraud and Abuse Process

symptom: All Windows 365 Cloud PCs deallocated or deprovisioned; tenant marked as highly suspicious or confirmed fraud by HIT
rootCause: Tenant flagged as highly suspicious (Cloud PCs deallocated) or confirmed fraud by HIT Human Investigation Team (Cloud PCs deprovisioned)
solution: Check tenant trust level via CPCD or Reporting Kusto (DailyTenantEvaluation_Snapshot). For fraud upheld: inform customer subscription remains disabled. For fraud lifted: confirm re-enablement and apologize. Use provided email templates
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/Processes and Collaboration/Windows 365 Anti Fraud and Abuse Process

symptom: Cloud PC deleted/deprovisioned after license or provisioning policy removed, user loses access
rootCause: License removed or provisioning policy unassigned triggers 7-day grace period then active deletion
solution: Use CPCD Device Diagnostic to check LifecycleStatus and VM operations for DELETE event. Check Action Diagnostic for grace period entries. Active deletion is unrecoverable - only passive deletions can be recovered.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/ICM process & templates/Cloud PC recovery for passive deletion/Cloud PC active deletion review process

symptom: Cloud PC VM disk deleted due to service issue, need to recover managed disk
rootCause: Service issue caused active deletion of Cloud PC VM disk
solution: Check disk soft-delete status via Kusto (DiskRPResourceLifecycleEvent). Soft-deleted (disk >10 days old, deleted <96h ago): file IcM to Support/APTS. Hard-deleted: check Jarvis IsRecoverable flag, if true file IcM to Xstore/Triage + GC on-call. Ultra Disk not supported.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/ICM process & templates/Cloud PC recovery for passive deletion/VM Disk Recovery Process

symptom: Customer requests Windows 365 Cloud PC provisioning in Brazil South region but region is restricted
rootCause: Brazil South is a restricted region for Windows 365; provisioning requires Business Desk approval and flighting tag BrazilSupportedRegionEnabled
solution: Direct customer to their Microsoft Account Team for approval process. If customer has no Account Team, no exception can be processed. Archive the CSS case — no action required from CSS or SaaF. Engineering workflow: Business Desk approves → ANC OCE team adds tenant to BrazilSupportedRegionEnabled flight → confirms via email
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/Restricted Regions/Brazil South Process

symptom: Windows 365 customer wants to deploy Cloud PCs in a restricted region (e.g., US West 2)
rootCause: Certain regions are restricted for Windows 365 provisioning; MHN deployments are blocked, but ANC (BYON) is allowed if customer has existing resources there
solution: Option 1 (ANC/BYON): If customer has existing resources in the restricted region (e.g., ExpressRoute circuit), they can configure an ANC using BYON — no exception needed. Option 2 (MHN): Customer must provision in another available non-restricted region; no exception process exists for MHN. Customer should work with account team for VNET peering or network architecture adjustments.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/Restricted Regions/Restricted region exception

symptom: Windows 365 provisioning fails in West Europe with error: Please use only supported Windows365 regions — new customers cannot deploy in WEU, region hidden in MHN policy, ANC listed as Unsupported
rootCause: West Europe data center capacity restrictions due to physical space limitations and Dutch Government power supply restrictions preventing new data center construction. New W365 customers blocked from deploying in WEU to conserve quota for existing customers
solution: 1) New customers: MHN policy hides WEU region; ANC referencing WEU shows Unsupported; deployments fail with region error. 2) Existing customers can continue deploying within capacity limits. 3) Exceptions NOT accepted through CSS support cases — customer or CSAM must submit via Get Help with: data residency justification, license count, S500 status, deployment size, and risk to Microsoft. 4) No workaround exists for new MHN customers.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Support Processes and Guidance/Restricted Regions/West Europe Capacity Restrictions

symptom: Windows 365 Frontline Shared Cloud PC provisioned with warnings, DPP (Device Preparation Profile) not assigned, error DppNotEnabled
rootCause: Device Preparation Profile (DPP) not properly configured: not created as Automatic (Preview) mode, not assigned to correct device group, device group ownership not set to Intune Provisioning Client (AppId f1346770-5b25-470b-88bd-d5744ab7952c), or DPP profile not added in Frontline Shared provisioning policy
solution: 1) Verify DPP profile created as Automatic (Preview) and assigned to device group. 2) Verify device group owner is Intune Provisioning Client (AppId f1346770-5b25-470b-88bd-d5744ab7952c). 3) Ensure DPP profile is added in FLS provisioning policy. 4) Use Kusto ServiceEvent queries to check provisioning flow and IntuneEvent queries for DPP assignment. 5) If config is correct, engage Intune Collab team.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Tools and Data Collection/Support Tools/Kusto Queries/Windows 365 | Cloud PC/Windows 365 FrontLine Dedicated and Frontline Shared/Windows 365 Frontline Shared: Autopilot V2 Device Preparation DPP Troubleshooting

symptom: Windows 365 Frontline Shared Cloud PC provisioned with warnings: DPP not enabled error (DppNotEnabled). DPP Provisioning failed with error message: Dpp is not enabled.
rootCause: Device Preparation Profile (DPP) not created as Automatic (Preview) or not assigned to the device group, or Intune Provisioning Client service principal (AppId f1346770-5b25-470b-88bd-d5744ab7952c) is missing from the device group owner
solution: 1. Verify DPP profile is created as Automatic (Preview) and assigned to a clean device group. 2. Ensure device group owner is set to Intune Provisioning Client (AppId f1346770-5b25-470b-88bd-d5744ab7952c). If SP missing, add it per MS docs. 3. Verify DPP profile is selected in the Frontline Shared provisioning policy. 4. If config looks correct, engage Intune Collab for DPP profile assignment issues.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Tools and Data Collection/Support Tools/Kusto Queries/Windows 365 | Cloud PC/Windows 365 FrontLine Dedicated and Frontline Shared/Windows 365 Frontline Shared: Autopilot V2 Device Preparation DPP Troubleshooting

symptom: Frontline Shared Cloud PC provisioned with warnings, DPP not enabled error (DppNotEnabled)
rootCause: Device Preparation Profile not assigned to device group, or Intune Provisioning Client service principal (AppId f1346770-5b25-470b-88bd-d5744ab7952c) missing from device group owner, or DPP profile not added in provisioning policy
solution: 1. Verify DPP profile created as Automatic (Preview) assigned to clean device group. 2. Verify device group owner is Intune Provisioning Client SP. 3. Ensure DPP profile added in Frontline Shared provisioning policy. 4. If CPC-side config OK, engage Intune Collab for service-side DPP assignment issues.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Tools and Data Collection/Support Tools/Kusto Queries/Windows 365 | Cloud PC/Windows 365 FrontLine Dedicated and Frontline Shared/Windows 365 Frontline Shared: Autopilot V2 Device Preparation DPP Troubleshooting

symptom: Windows 365 Frontline Shared Cloud PC provisioned with warnings, DPP profile not assigned, error DppNotEnabled
rootCause: Device Preparation Profile not created as Automatic (Preview), device group owner not set to Intune Provisioning Client (AppId f1346770-5b25-470b-88bd-d5744ab7952c), or DPP profile not added in provisioning policy
solution: 1. Create DPP profile as Automatic (Preview) and assign to device group. 2. Set device group Owner to Intune Provisioning Client SP. 3. Add DPP profile in Frontline Shared provisioning policy. 4. If CPC-side config OK, engage Intune Collab for service-side issues.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Tools and Data Collection/Support Tools/Kusto Queries/Windows 365 | Cloud PC/Windows 365 FrontLine Dedicated and Frontline Shared/Windows 365 Frontline Shared: Autopilot V2 Device Preparation DPP Troubleshooting

symptom: Windows 365 Frontline Shared Cloud PC provisioned with warnings; DPP (Device Preparation Profile) not applied; error DppNotEnabled
rootCause: DPP Automatic (Preview) profile not assigned to device group, or Intune Provisioning Client service principal (AppId f1346770-5b25-470b-88bd-d5744ab7952c) missing from device group owner, or DPP profile not added to FLS provisioning policy
solution: Verify: 1) DPP profile created as Automatic (Preview) and assigned to device group, 2) Device group owner is Intune Provisioning Client SP (AppId f1346770-5b25-470b-88bd-d5744ab7952c), 3) DPP profile added in FLS provisioning policy. Check Kusto ServiceEvent for DppNotEnabled error and IntuneEvent for profile assignment. If CPC config correct, engage Intune Collab
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Tools and Data Collection/Support Tools/Kusto Queries/Windows 365 | Cloud PC/Windows 365 FrontLine Dedicated and Frontline Shared/Windows 365 Frontline Shared: Autopilot V2 Device Preparation DPP Troubleshooting

symptom: Windows 365 Frontline Shared CPC provisioned with warnings; error DppNotEnabled - DPP Provisioning failed with error message: Dpp is not enabled
rootCause: Device Preparation Profile (DPP) not assigned to the device group, or Intune Provisioning Client service principal (AppId f1346770-5b25-470b-88bd-d5744ab7952c) missing from device group ownership
solution: 1) Verify DPP profile is created as Automatic (Preview) and assigned to a clean device group. 2) Ensure device group owner is Intune Provisioning Client SP (f1346770-5b25-470b-88bd-d5744ab7952c). 3) Verify DPP profile is added in Frontline Shared provisioning policy. 4) If CPC config is correct, engage Intune Collab for service-side DPP assignment issues.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Tools and Data Collection/Support Tools/Kusto Queries/Windows 365 | Cloud PC/Windows 365 FrontLine Dedicated and Frontline Shared/Windows 365 Frontline Shared: Autopilot V2 Device Preparation DPP Troubleshooting

symptom: FLS Cloud PC provisioned with warnings; DPP profile shows as not assigned; error DppNotEnabled
rootCause: Device Preparation Profile not configured correctly: not created as Automatic (Preview), device group owner not set to Intune Provisioning Client (AppId f1346770-5b25-470b-88bd-d5744ab7952c), or DPP profile not added in Frontline Shared provisioning policy
solution: 1. Verify DPP profile is created as Automatic (Preview) and assigned to correct device group. 2. Ensure device group owner is Intune Provisioning Client. 3. Verify DPP profile is added in provisioning policy. 4. If CPC-side config is correct, engage Intune Collab for service-side investigation.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Tools and Data Collection/Support Tools/Kusto Queries/Windows 365 | Cloud PC/Windows 365 FrontLine Dedicated and Frontline Shared/Windows 365 Frontline Shared: Autopilot V2 Device Preparation DPP Troubleshooting

symptom: AccessDenied error when performing Inspect IaaS Disk log collection in ASC for Windows 365 Cloud PC
rootCause: VM not pinned to the DfM case in Azure Support Center
solution: Pin the VM to the case using Resource Explorer (Step 6 in ASC workflow) and refresh the entire page
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Tools and Data Collection/Support Tools/ASC (Azure Support Center)/Collect ASC IaaS disk & Guest Analyzer logs

symptom: Error retrieving data when loading Diagnostics tab in DfM EU ASC for Cloud PC
rootCause: DfM EU region data retrieval limitation in Azure Support Center
solution: Use a Global (WW) DFM case to load the VM in ASC instead of the EU DFM case
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Tools and Data Collection/Support Tools/ASC (Azure Support Center)/Collect ASC IaaS disk & Guest Analyzer logs

symptom: Windows 365 custom image upload fails during provisioning validation
rootCause: Pre-installed antivirus solutions (e.g., Sophos) on custom image interfere with image validation process
solution: Remove AV solutions from the custom image before upload. Use Kusto queries to identify image validation VM SKU and detailed error from CloudPCEvent logs
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Tools and Data Collection/Support Tools/Kusto Queries/Windows 365 | Cloud PC/Custom Image Upload Failure. Get Details and ImageId

symptom: Unable to get federation from any specified endpoint when connecting to GCCH Kusto cluster for Windows 365
rootCause: Missing PME account setup, YubiKey not configured, Windows Hello not set up, or Azure VPN not connected on physical device
solution: Ensure: 1) PME account with YubiKey configured, 2) Windows Hello set up on physical device, 3) Azure VPN connected, 4) Use Kusto for SAW with advanced connection string for GCCH endpoint
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Tools and Data Collection/Support Tools/Kusto Queries/Windows 365 Government GCCH/Kusto cluster for Windows 365 GCCH

symptom: Cloud PC users consistently disconnected after fixed period of inactivity (e.g., 60 minutes idle or after set active session time)
rootCause: Session timeout policies configured via MaxIdleTime or MaxConnectionTime registry values, set through GPO or Intune MDM policy
solution: Check MSRD-Diag.html > AVD/RDS/W365 > Session Time Limits. Review registry at HKLM/HKCU\\SOFTWARE\\Policies\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\Terminal Services for MaxIdleTime and MaxConnectionTime
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Troubleshooting Guides/Session Connectivity/Persistent Disconnections

symptom: AVD Web Client: User cannot connect; 'TimeSkew' error — 'We couldn't connect to the remote PC because there's a date or time difference between your device and the remote PC'
rootCause: Date/time difference (time skew) between user's device and the remote PC
solution: Ensure the clock on the client device is set to the correct time (sync with time server). Then retry the connection
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/Archived Content/Deprecated Content/DEPRECATED_Mobius/DEPRECATED_Unified Web Client

symptom: AVD Web Client: User cannot connect; 'PasswordExpired' or 'PasswordMustChange' error
rootCause: User's account password has expired or must be changed before sign-in is allowed
solution: User must change their password first, then retry connection
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/Archived Content/Deprecated Content/DEPRECATED_Mobius/DEPRECATED_Unified Web Client

symptom: AVD Web Client: 'SessionHostResourceNotAvailable' error — 'We couldn't connect because your VM failed to start'
rootCause: The session host VM failed to start during the connection attempt
solution: Retry connection after waiting; if issue persists check session host VM health in the portal and contact support
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/Archived Content/Deprecated Content/DEPRECATED_Mobius/DEPRECATED_Unified Web Client

symptom: AVD Web Client: Session disconnected with 'IdleTimeout' error
rootCause: Session timed out due to inactivity — idle timeout policy enforced by admin or network policy
solution: Reconnect; ask admin to review idle timeout policy settings if too aggressive
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/Archived Content/Deprecated Content/DEPRECATED_Mobius/DEPRECATED_Unified Web Client

symptom: AVD Web Client: 'NoLicenseAvailable' or 'NoLicenseServer' error — cannot connect to remote PC
rootCause: No licenses available for the remote PC (or no license server available to provide a license)
solution: Contact admin to check license availability and assignment; may need to procure additional licenses
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/Archived Content/Deprecated Content/DEPRECATED_Mobius/DEPRECATED_Unified Web Client

symptom: AVD Web Client: 'AccountLockedOut' error — 'account has been locked due to too many sign in or password change attempts'
rootCause: User account locked out in Azure AD due to brute-force protection or too many failed attempts
solution: Wait for lockout period to expire or have admin unlock the account; review sign-in attempts for suspicious activity
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/Archived Content/Deprecated Content/DEPRECATED_Mobius/DEPRECATED_Unified Web Client

symptom: AVD MSRDC 客户端 Feed 或 Web 客户端 Feed 在 Private Link 环境下无法加载
rootCause: Private Endpoint 配置了错误的 sub-resource type，或链接到了错误的资源（如链接到 hostpool 而非 workspace）
solution: MSRDC Feed：PE 必须链接到 workspace，sub-resource type 为 'workspace'，取消勾选 'Allow end user access from public network'；Web Client Feed：PE 必须链接到 workspace，sub-resource type 为 'global'，取消勾选 public access
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/Archived Content/Deprecated Content/DEPRECATED_Understanding Private Link Configuration in AVD

symptom: AVD 会话主机连接在 Private Link 环境下失败，用户无法通过私有网络连接到 Session Host
rootCause: Private Endpoint 未链接到 hostpool 或 sub-resource type 配置错误；或未为订阅创建全局 PE sub-resource
solution: MSRDC Connection：PE 必须链接到 hostpool，sub-resource type 为 'hostpool'，取消勾选 'Allow end user access from public network' 和 'Allow session hosts access from public network'；同时必须在订阅中至少创建一个 hostpool sub-resource 的 PE（每个订阅强制一次，用于访问全局 AVD URLs）
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/Archived Content/Deprecated Content/DEPRECATED_Understanding Private Link Configuration in AVD

symptom: 使用 Context Based Redirection 时，Clipboard/Drive/Printer/USB 重定向在 AVD 或 Windows 365 会话中意外被禁用或不生效
rootCause: CA 策略配置为 allow-based（不支持，只支持 block-based）；或用户 token 中缺少预期的 authentication context claims (acrs)；或 CA 策略未正确匹配目标用户/设备条件
solution: 1) 验证 CA 策略为 block-based（allow-based 不支持）；2) 检查 Entra 登录日志确认 acrs 声明是否存在；3) 修正 CA 策略的目标用户/组/设备条件；4) 确保所有 auth contexts 至少被一个 CA 策略引用；5) 断开重连会话。注意：不支持会话中途修改重定向设置
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Context Based Redirection/Troubleshooting

symptom: Cannot connect to remote computer - 'a security package error occurred at the transport layer' error when connecting to AVD session host
rootCause: TLS or Cipher Mismatch between the RDP client and the session host
solution: Check for TLS or Cipher Mismatch. Reference: https://dev.azure.com/Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/_wiki/wikis/WindowsVirtualDesktop/464991/TLS-or-Cipher-Mismatch
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/Errors/Your computer can't connect to the remote computer because a security package error occurred at the transport layer

symptom: AVD Insights workbook (new, not Insights Legacy) displays unexpected or incorrect data at the Host Pool level in Azure Portal
rootCause: Default Insights workbook selects DCR based on 'microsoft-avdi-*' naming convention and Log Analytics workspace based on alphanumerical sort of Diagnostic Settings; when naming conventions are not followed or incorrect Diagnostic Setting is first alphabetically, the wrong data source is selected
solution: 1. Ensure DCR name follows 'microsoft-avdi-*' naming convention (the configuration workbook creates DCR as 'microsoft-avdi-<location>' by default). 2. Ensure the desired Diagnostic Setting (with LA workspace destination) appears first in alphanumerical sort – delete and recreate with appropriate name if needed (names cannot be edited).
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/Insights/Insights Workbook displaying unexpected or incorrect data

symptom: AVD Insights workbook (new, not Insights Legacy) not selecting the expected Data Collection Rule (DCR) – wrong DCR selected, incorrect data displayed
rootCause: The default Insights workbook selects DCR based on naming convention 'microsoft-avdi-*'; a DCR that does not follow this naming convention will not be selected. The workbook looks for DCRs starting with 'microsoft-avdi-' prefix.
solution: Ensure the desired DCR name follows the 'microsoft-avdi-*' naming convention. The configuration workbook creates DCR as 'microsoft-avdi-<location>' by default. Rename or recreate the DCR with a name starting with 'microsoft-avdi-'.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/Insights/Insights Workbook not selecting expected DCR

symptom: AVD Insights workbook not selecting the expected Log Analytics workspace – showing data from wrong workspace
rootCause: Default Insights workbook selects LA workspace based on alphanumerical sort of Diagnostic Settings; the first Diagnostic Setting that starts with numbers or letters AND has a LA workspace as destination wins. Diagnostic Settings starting with special characters (e.g., '_') are deprioritized. Priority order: allowed symbols → numbers → letters.
solution: Rename/recreate the desired Diagnostic Setting to appear first in alphanumerical sort (must start with numbers or letters). Note: Diagnostic Setting names cannot be edited – must delete and recreate with the correct name.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/Insights/Insights Workbook not selecting expected Log Analytics workspace

symptom: AVD connection refused with error: 'Connection was refused because you tried to access a private endpoint resource without being connected to the private endpoint. Please retry after connecting to the private endpoint.'
rootCause: The host pool network access policy is set to private endpoint only (public access disabled, PrivateLinkID=2), but the client is attempting to connect via a public endpoint rather than through the configured private endpoint.
solution: 1) In ASC go to Host Pool > Connection tab, find the connection. 2) Copy activity ID, go to Tracing tab, paste activity ID, Tracing Type: Operation, Run. 3) Add Filter: Props contains PrivateLinkID — value 2=Disabled means public access is blocked. 4) Change Tracing Type to Server to see 'connection failed with access denied because network access from public endpoint is not allowed'. 5) Either have customer connect via the configured private endpoint, or adjust the host pool network access policy to allow public access.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/PL/Connection Failure

symptom: Workspace feed/subscription fails with error: 'The resource in <workspace name> can't be accessed from this network'. Users unable to subscribe or enumerate AVD resources via workspace feed.
rootCause: The workspace network access policy is configured to disallow connections from public endpoints (private link only). The client is connecting via a public endpoint, which is denied by the policy.
solution: 1) In ASC go to Workspace > Workspaces Feed tab and find the failed feed operation. 2) Copy activity ID, go to Tracing tab, paste activity ID, Tracing Type: Service, Run. 3) Add Filters: Category contains WebFeedLogic AND Msg contains Access. 4) Confirm feed request failed with 'access denied because network access from public endpoint is not allowed'. 5) Check workspace network access settings — either configure the client to connect via private endpoint, or update the workspace to allow public endpoint access.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/PL/Feed Failures

symptom: Customer cannot configure or use AVD Private Link; Private Link option not available, feature not visible in portal, or Private Link not functioning for workspace/host pool even after configuration attempts.
rootCause: Customer's Azure subscription has not been enrolled in the AVD Private Link Public Preview. Without enrollment, the Private Link feature is not available to the subscription.
solution: 1) In ASC select customer subscription > Azure Activity Logs. 2) Set appropriate time range, Filter: ResourceId contains AllowPrivateEndpoints. 3) If results found in logs, customer IS enrolled. If no results, customer is NOT enrolled. 4) To enroll: guide customer to Portal > Preview Features (https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Resources/PreviewFeaturesBlade) > search 'Private' > select 'Azure Virtual Desktop Private Link Public Preview' checkbox > click Register (bottom-right). 5) Enrollment may take time to propagate before Private Link features become available.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/PL/TSG/Enrolled in Preview

symptom: User session UPNs not displayed in AVD portal user sessions list; UPN column is empty or missing for active sessions despite users being connected.
rootCause: User subscribes to the AVD workspace with one set of credentials (e.g. User1@domain.com) but connects to a resource using a different set of credentials (e.g. direct1@domain.com or account from a different domain). This 'switched user credentials scenario' causes the orchestration layer to lose the UPN mapping.
solution: 1) Get ActivityID for an affected connected user session. 2) Run Kusto query on RDInfraTrace (all regions union) with ActivityId filter and Msg contains 'switched user credentials scenario' to confirm. 3) On session host, run 'quser <sessionID>' to get username. 4) Run 'Get-WmiObject win32_useraccount | Where-Object{$_.Name -like "<Username>"} | Select name,sid' to get SID. 5) Run '([ADSI]"LDAP://<SID=<User SID>>").UserPrincipalName' to get the actual UPN. 6) Compare with orchestration trace using connection ActivityID (filter on OrchestrationService and RDmiAuthorizationService categories) to confirm credential mismatch. 7) Inform customer to use consistent credentials for workspace subscription and resource connection.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/Portal Not Showing UPNs

symptom: RDP Shortpath (UDP transport) fails to establish for client connections; AVD falls back to TCP transport only. Client side UDP is not being initiated.
rootCause: Client machine GPO policy is blocking UDP traffic, preventing RDP Shortpath from sending/receiving UDP ICE candidates. Confirmed when no UdpICEReceivedCandidates events exist in RDPCoreTSEventLog for the ActivityID.
solution: Run Kusto query: cluster('rdsprod.eastus2.kusto.windows.net').database('WVD').RDPCoreTSEventLog | union cluster('rdsprodus.eastus2.kusto.windows.net')... | where ActivityId == '<id>' | where TaskName == 'UdpICEReceivedCandidates' | project TIMESTAMP, Level, HostInstance, ProviderName, TaskName, EventMessage, Message. If no events are returned, UDP is disabled on client via GPO. Investigate client GPO settings and modify to allow UDP traffic to AVD STUN server IP ranges (13.107.17.41, 13.107.64.0/18, 20.202.0.0/16, 52.112.0.0/14, 52.120.0.0/14).
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/RDP Shortpath Public/Kusto

symptom: RDP Shortpath (UDP transport) fails to establish from session host VM side; UDP ICE negotiation not initialized on the VM even when client UDP may be functioning.
rootCause: Session host VM GPO policy is blocking UDP traffic, preventing RDP Shortpath from initializing on the VM. Confirmed when no UdpICEInitialized events exist in RDPCoreTSEventLog for the ActivityID.
solution: Run Kusto query: cluster('rdsprod.eastus2.kusto.windows.net').database('WVD').RDPCoreTSEventLog | union cluster('rdsprodus.eastus2.kusto.windows.net')... | where ActivityId == '<id>' | where TaskName == 'UdpICEInitialized' | project TIMESTAMP, Level, HostInstance, ProviderName, TaskName, EventMessage, Message. If no events are returned, UDP is disabled on the VM via GPO. Investigate session host GPO settings and modify to allow UDP traffic for RDP Shortpath.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/RDP Shortpath Public/Kusto

symptom: Session Host Update (SHU) fails with error ImageHyperVGenerationDoesNotMatchExistingVms_1024: 'Image requires a HyperV generation V2 incompatible with the HyperV generation V1 associated with existing VMs in the Host Pool.' Customer attempts to update session hosts using a Gen2 gallery image but the host pool was originally deployed with Gen1 VMs.
rootCause: HyperV generation mismatch between the selected update image (Gen2/V2) and the existing session host VMs (Gen1/V1). AVD Session Host Update cannot change the VM generation in-place.
solution: Select an image that matches the HyperV generation of the existing VMs in the host pool. If existing VMs are Gen1, use a Gen1 image for the update. To migrate from Gen1 to Gen2, the host pool must be recreated with new Gen2 VMs rather than using in-place session host update.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/SHU/testing/Failed - GEN1 to GEN2

symptom: Session Host Update fails with DeniedAccessToCustomerResource / AuthorizationFailed: 'The client does not have virtualMachines/write or disks/write permission.' Update reaches UpdateFailed state with faultCode DeniedAccessToCustomerResource.
rootCause: The AVD service principal used by Session Host Update is missing required RBAC permissions. Specifically, 'Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/write' and/or 'Microsoft.Compute/disks/write' were removed from the custom role assigned to the AVD SHU service principal (appid: 9cdead84-a844-4324-93f2-b2e6bb768d07).
solution: Restore the required RBAC permissions on the custom role: ensure 'Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/write' and 'Microsoft.Compute/disks/write' are included. Verify the AVD SHU service principal (oid: fccec22d-e6c5-4844-8ef1-1838c8b7cb8b) has the correct role assignment on the resource group containing the host pool.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/SHU/testing/Failed - RBAC Perms

symptom: Session Host Update fails with UnableToAccessKeyvaultSecret_1024: 'Unable to access Keyvault Secret for secret VM-Local-Administrator-Password. Keyvault request failed with Status 403 Forbidden, ForbiddenByPolicy - does not have secrets get permission on key vault.'
rootCause: The AVD SHU service principal lacks the 'Get' permission on Key Vault secrets. Only 'List' was enabled under Secret Management Operations; 'Get' was unchecked. The service principal (appid: 9cdead84-a844-4324-93f2-b2e6bb768d07) cannot retrieve the VM local admin password secret required for the update.
solution: In the Key Vault access policy or RBAC assignment for the AVD SHU service principal, enable both 'Get' AND 'List' under Secret Management Operations. 'Get' is required to read the VM-Local-Administrator-Password secret during Session Host Update.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/SHU/testing/KV Failure

symptom: Session Host Update fails with MigrationMayExceedCapacityLimit_1024: 'Resources created during the update process may cause subscription limits to be exceeded for Total Regional vCPUs or StandardBSFamily vCPUs.' Update is blocked before new VMs are deployed.
rootCause: The subscription's vCPU quota (Total Regional or SKU family) is insufficient to accommodate the temporary additional VMs created during Session Host Update. SHU creates new VMs before deleting old ones, requiring headroom above current usage.
solution: Increase the vCPU quota for the affected subscription and region via Azure portal (Subscriptions > Usage + quotas > Request increase), or delete unused compute resources to free capacity. Ensure the quota limit exceeds projected usage by at least the number of VMs being updated simultaneously (maxVMsUnavailableDuringUpdate).
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/SHU/testing/KV Failure

symptom: During Session Host Update, the portal displays 'update is pausing' status for 40+ minutes with no apparent progress. Customer believes the operation is stuck or hung.
rootCause: SHU waits for active user sessions to drain before fully transitioning to paused state. The 'update is pausing' status persists while session hosts are in drain mode, which can take extended time depending on session activity and drain timeout settings.
solution: This is expected behavior. The update will eventually transition to fully paused once all active sessions have disconnected or drained. Advise customer to wait for the operation to complete. If stuck for several hours with no session activity, retry or cancel and re-initiate the update.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/SHU/testing/Pause-Resume

symptom: W365 app displays custom connection monitor UI error or status messages to the end user
rootCause: W365 app has its own custom connection monitor UI messages separate from standard AVD. AVD sends connection status to CPC, which W365 app then takes and displays using its own UI layer
solution: CPC engineer should create an incident with CPC PG to investigate. If CPC determines it is not a CPC issue, transfer the incident to AVD PG
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/w365/Connection Monitor

symptom: W365 Cloud PC session connects to wrong feed URL (e.g., subscribes to PROD feed instead of GOV feed)
rootCause: CPC app incorrectly constructs the .avd file URI, causing msrdcw.exe to subscribe to the wrong feed URL. AVD host app uses .avd file launch mechanism (similar to URI launcher) and depends on CPC correctly constructing the URI
solution: Create an incident with CPC PG to investigate the URI construction issue. If CPC confirms it is not their issue, transfer to AVD PG
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Outdated? - Needs review if still useful/w365/avd file launch mechanism

symptom: Customer requests ability to selectively enable or disable RAW printing for specific individual printer devices only
rootCause: Feature limitation: SmartRawPrinters RDP property does not support targeting individual printer instances; it is either global (all printers) or filtered by printer driver name prefix
solution: Inform customer that RAW printing is a global toggle only. Options: (1) SmartRawPrinters:s:* to enable for all printers, or (2) SmartRawPrinters:s:<DriverPrefix1>;<DriverPrefix2> to filter by driver name prefix. Cannot enable/disable for specific individual devices.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/RAW Printing Support/FAQ

symptom: RAW printing not working or experiencing issues with RAW printing redirection in AVD or W365 remote session
solution: No additional troubleshooting specific to RAW printing is needed. Follow the same TSG steps as: (1) Printer Redirection — see 'Printer Redirection on AVD from a Print Server' wiki page; (2) General Device Redirection — see 'Device Redirection Overview' wiki page
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/RAW Printing Support/Troubleshooting

symptom: Relative Mouse feature not functioning in AVD session - mouse does not behave as relative mouse
rootCause: RDP property `AllowRelativeMouseMode:i:1` not added to Host Pool
solution: In ASC, go to Host Pool > RDP Properties > confirm/add `AllowRelativeMouseMode:i:1`
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Relative Mouse/Troubleshooting

symptom: Need to verify if Relative Mouse is being triggered successfully in an AVD session
solution: Use ASC Tracing tab with connection Activity ID (filter Task Name = RelativeMouse), or Kusto query on RDClientTrace `| where TaskName == 'RelativeMouse'` across all regional clusters. Alternatively run RelativeMouseSupportTool.exe on AVD VM (Ctrl+O to toggle); if Relative Mouse Movement field shows N/A, it is not enabled.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/Relative Mouse/Troubleshooting

symptom: TWAIN scanner not working or not detected in AVD/W365 remote session
rootCause: WIA (Windows Image Acquisition) driver selected instead of TWAIN driver, or scanner issues on local client endpoint
solution: 1. First validate scanner works E2E on local client using free TWAIN software (e.g. NAPS2). 2. In scanner application on session host, ensure TWAIN driver is selected (not WIA). 3. Confirm TWAIN redirection enabled on session host: `reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services" /v fTWAINRedirectionEnabled /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f`. 4. Ensure Windows App >=2.0.415.0 or MSRDC >=1.2.6220 on client.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/TWAIN/Troubleshooting

symptom: Multiple TWAIN scanners attached to client endpoint; wrong or no scanner selected as default in AVD session
rootCause: No default TWAIN scanner configured on client device
solution: Set default TWAIN scanner via client-side registry: `reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services\Client\TWAINSelectDeviceByName" /v 8000 /t REG_SZ /d "<scanner device name>" /f`
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/TWAIN/Troubleshooting

symptom: Session hosts intermittently lose access to FSLogix profiles on Azure Files share
rootCause: MCAPS policies periodically enforce disabling Public network access on storage account, breaking session host connectivity to FSLogix profile share
solution: Re-adjust Public network access on storage account: set to 'Enable from selected virtual networks and IP addresses' with session host vnet subnets included. Monitor for MCAPS policy enforcement that may reset this setting.
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/In-Development Content/[AVD Lab] 11 - [Optional] Set up FSLogix Profile for your users/[AVD Lab] 11.1 - [FSLogix] Set up a Storage Account

symptom: Unable to connect to Azure AD-joined VMs in AVD from non-Windows devices, non-AAD-joined Windows devices, Microsoft Store client, or Web Client
rootCause: Custom RDP property required for non-AAD-joined/non-Windows device connections to AADJ session hosts not configured
solution: Add the custom RDP property to allow non-AAD/non-Windows clients per: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-desktop/deploy-azure-ad-joined-vm#connect-using-the-other-clients
sourceRef: Supportability/WindowsVirtualDesktop/WindowsVirtualDesktop:/Sandbox/PCY or Region Specific Process Information Content/STA-EEE Corner/Centennial/Apollo Solutions/AADJ

symptom: Unable to sign in to Azure AD-joined VMs from non-Windows clients when multifactor authentication (MFA) is enabled
rootCause: MFA not configured correctly for AADJ VM connections from non-Windows clients; MFA blocks sign-in from non-Windows clients by default
