solution: Create Conditional Access policy: (1) Target needed users (2) Select AVD app (3) Set condition to Browser only (4) Set grant action to Block access. Users can access W365 portal but connection will be blocked.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Dependencies/Authentication/Block users from connecting on the Cloud PCs from the browser

symptom: CloudPC sign-in blocked by Conditional Access policy due to unexpected private IPv6 address appearing as source client IP in Azure AD sign-in logs
rootCause: VNet with Microsoft.AzureActiveDirectory service endpoint enabled converts VM source IP to private IPv6 via VFP. AAD gateway uses X-Forwarded-For header (IPv6) as client IP. IPv6 is unpredictable and cannot be whitelisted.
solution: Disable Microsoft.AzureActiveDirectory service endpoint on the VNET/Subnet where CloudPC is located. Use IPv6-to-IPv4 converter tool to verify. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-faq
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Dependencies/Authentication/CloudPC failed to AAD sign-in with IPv6 address by conditional access policy

symptom: Users get disconnected instead of seeing OS lock screen when Device Lock or screen saver policy triggers on Cloud PC with SSO enabled in provisioning policy
rootCause: Lock screen does not support new Azure AD authentication used by SSO. It also does not allow passwordless credentials like FIDO keys and some configurations of WHfB.
solution: This is by design when SSO is enabled. Disconnection ensures CA/MFA policies are re-evaluated upon reconnection. Ref: https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/windows-365/windows-365-sso-preview-disconnects-on-lock-instead-of-showing/m-p/3781695
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Dependencies/Authentication/DeviceLock causing disconnect event when SSO is enabled in Provisioning

symptom: Windows 365 Cloud PC does not reconnect via Windows App after 1 hour of disconnection; clicking Reconnect fails to find a valid session and user cannot resume disconnected Cloud PC
rootCause: By design: after 1 hour of disconnection, the Conditional Access sign-in policy triggers because the old access token has expired. The service tries to redirect to the disconnected session on the session host, but the session has already expired and is no longer valid.
solution: Expected behavior. Users must re-authenticate after 1 hour as the CA sign-in policy requires a new sign-in when the access token expires. The disconnected session is no longer valid after token expiry. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-desktop/terminology#disconnected-user-session
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Client Applications/Windows App/W365 Cloud PC does not reconnect using Windows app

symptom: Need to collect CMD (Cloud Managed Desktop) Agent logs for Windows 365 provisioning or disk size troubleshooting
solution: CMD Agent logs are located at: C:\ProgramData\Microsoft\CMDExtension\Logs
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Cloud PC Actions/Provisioning/CMD Agent Troubleshooting and Log collection

symptom: Windows 365 Cloud PC provisioning or ANC (Azure Network Connection) fails when tenant deploys GPO with AllSigned execution policy for PowerShell; provisioning error: internalError; ANC check 'Environment and configuration is ready' fails with VMDeploymentTimeout or internalServerErrorVMDeploymentTimeout
rootCause: Built-in DSC scripts used during Windows 365 provisioning are internally signed by Azure, not with a public Microsoft certificate. AllSigned execution policy requires all scripts to be signed by a trusted publisher, which blocks the Azure-internal DSC scripts from running.
solution: AllSigned execution policy is not supported for Windows 365 Cloud PCs. Switch to RemoteSigned execution policy. An internal work item has been raised to support AllSigned in the future (no ETA). References: ICM 281915148, ICM 284109792, ADO 13707443
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Cloud PC Actions/Provisioning/Cloud PC provisioning & ANC fails when deploying GPO with "AllSigned" execution policy for PowerShell

symptom: Windows 365 Cloud PC is provisioned with half of the disk size specified in the SKU license; or after Cloud PC resize, the new Cloud PC is created with smaller disk than expected (half disk size)
rootCause: CMD (Cloud Managed Desktop) Agent running inside the VM fails to reach Azure endpoints to fetch license information. During provisioning, W365 first allocates basic SKU capacity, then the CMD Agent retrieves license details to expand the disk. When blocked by firewall, provisioning falls back to the basic SKU disk size. Required endpoint: *.infra.windows365.microsoft.com (formerly *.cmdagent.trafficmanager.net, retired Nov 2025).
solution: Allow outbound access to *.infra.windows365.microsoft.com (covers *.cmdagent.infra.windows365.microsoft.com). Also allow execution of PS DSC scripts at: c:\packages\plugins\microsoft.compute.customscriptextension\*\bin\customscripthandler.exe. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-365/enterprise/requirements-network
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Cloud PC Actions/Provisioning/CloudPC provisioned with half Disk Size or resize creates CPC with half disk size

symptom: Group Based Licensed (GBL) Cloud PC resize fails to reach 'Resize pending license' state; CPC starts resizing immediately without waiting for admin to change user group; or a duplicate additional CPC is provisioned with the original SKU instead of reprovisioning the existing machine
rootCause: A direct license assignment coexists with a group license assignment for the same user. GBL resize requires the license to be assigned exclusively through the group. A direct assignment prevents the CPC from entering the 'Resize pending license' status and causes unexpected provisioning behavior.
solution: Remove the direct W365 license assignment from the affected user(s) before starting the resize. Use ASC (Tenant Explorer > User > Licenses) or Azure Portal (Entra ID > Users > Licenses) to verify and remove direct assignments. Ensure the user is not in both source and target license groups simultaneously. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-365/enterprise/resize-cloud-pc
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Cloud PC Actions/Provisioning/Group Based Licensed (GBL) resize fails to fall into "Resize Pending License" status

symptom: M365 license not returned to available pool after end user removal; Cloud PC not deprovisioned or placed in grace period after user is removed from M365 admin portal; user appears in soft-delete state but CPC remains visible and provisioned in Intune after 7 days
rootCause: Improper user deletion flow: user was removed only from M365 Admin Center but the Entra ID object was not deleted. For on-premises (hybrid identity) environments, the AD object was not removed and Entra ID Connect sync was not run. CPC deprovisioning and license release require complete Entra ID object deletion.
solution: For hybrid identities: delete user from on-premises AD DC (Server Manager > AD Users and Computers) then run: Start-ADSyncSyncCycle -PolicyType Delta. For cloud identities: delete from Entra ID directly. Verify user removed from both Entra ID and M365 portal. CPC enters grace period, removed after 7 days. For soft-deleted users: restore > manually remove W365 license and provisioning policy group > delete again.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Cloud PC Actions/Provisioning/Process to properly revoke M365 licenses & Cloud PCs for end users

symptom: Windows 365 Cloud PC provisioned with warnings; Language Pack (LP) installation fails during provisioning; Kusto/ASC shows error: Cannot complete the request due to a conflicting Group Policy setting. Modify the GP setting 'Allow remote server management through WinRM' to either 'Not Configured' or 'Enabled'
rootCause: A GPO, Settings Catalog, or Custom OMA-URI policy sets 'Allow remote server management through WinRM' to Disabled. This conflicts with DSC (Desired State Configuration) installation scripts used during Windows 365 provisioning and LP installation.
solution: Set the 'Allow remote server management through WinRM' policy to 'Not Configured' or 'Enabled'. Do not configure this policy as Disabled in any GPO, Settings Catalog, or Custom OMA-URI that applies to Cloud PCs.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Cloud PC Actions/Provisioning/Provisioned with warnings - Policy caused, Firewall caused

symptom: Windows 365 Cloud PC provisioning, Language Pack installation, activation, or WNS (Windows Notification Service) fails even though firewall FQDN/port rules for required endpoints are correctly configured; Palo Alto firewall is in use
rootCause: Palo Alto Next-Gen Firewall has App Categories configured in its firewall policy that block specific application traffic independently of FQDN/port rules. Even with correct endpoint allowlisting by FQDN and port, App Categories can detect and block Microsoft/Windows update traffic.
solution: Check Palo Alto firewall App Categories policy configuration. Identify App Category rules that may be blocking Microsoft update or download traffic. Remove or adjust these App Category rules. This issue can affect provisioning, LP installation, activation, and WNS simultaneously.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Cloud PC Actions/Provisioning/Provisioned with warnings - Policy caused, Firewall caused

symptom: Windows App for macOS crashes when user joins Teams Town Hall meeting from Cloud PC using Teams desktop client
rootCause: Teams media optimization (VDI 1.0) does not support Town Hall meetings in Teams
solution: Turn off Teams media optimizations before joining Town Hall meeting, or use Teams web client, or connect to Cloud PC using Windows App for Windows instead of Mac
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/🤝Dependencies/Microsoft Teams/Joining Teams Town Hall Broadcast meeting crashes Windows App on Mac

symptom: In Teams Slimcore VDI 2.0, presenter shared screen shows as black screen with only mouse cursor visible to participants when Screen Capture Protection (SCP) is enabled
rootCause: Screen Capture Protection (SCP) is incompatible with Teams Slimcore VDI 2.0 screen sharing - known limitation
solution: Disable SCP if screen sharing is required, or wait for Teams VDI update. No workaround currently available for SCP + VDI 2.0 screen sharing.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/🤝Dependencies/Microsoft Teams/Known Issues

symptom: Teams calls drop on local machine that has an HID peripheral connected when user launches a virtual desktop from the same local machine and logs into Teams (VDI 2.0)
rootCause: Known Teams Slimcore VDI 2.0 limitation: HID peripheral on local endpoint conflicts with Teams VDI session launched from same machine
solution: Known limitation - no fix currently available. Workaround: disconnect HID peripheral before launching virtual desktop session.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/🤝Dependencies/Microsoft Teams/Known Issues

symptom: Citrix app sharing session freezes for other participants when presenter is on VDA 2402 + CWA for Windows 2309.1 or higher; freeze triggered when video element is destroyed (camera turned off, call monitor maximized)
rootCause: Bug in Teams Slimcore VDI 2.0 on Citrix with VDA 2402 + CWA 2309.1+: freeze when video element is destroyed during app sharing session
solution: Immediate workaround: stop and reshare the window. Permanent fix: upgrade to new Teams version 24335.206.X.X or higher.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/🤝Dependencies/Microsoft Teams/Known Issues

symptom: Blank screen shows in Teams meeting window instead of video feed when user opens Start menu on the virtual machine during a video call (Teams Slimcore VDI 2.0)
rootCause: Known Teams Slimcore VDI 2.0 behavior: opening Start menu on VM disrupts video rendering in meeting window
solution: Known limitation - no fix currently available. Avoid opening Start menu during active video calls.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/🤝Dependencies/Microsoft Teams/Known Issues

symptom: Teams shows AVD Media Not Connected or no AVD Media Optimization banner on Cloud PC; Teams does not attempt VDI optimization connection even after restart
rootCause: IsWVDEnvironment registry key (HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Teams\IsWVDEnvironment REG_DWORD) is missing or not set to 1; Teams does not recognize it is running in a VDI environment
solution: 1) Set HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Teams\IsWVDEnvironment REG_DWORD = 1. 2) Sign out, quit and restart Teams. 3) Verify RDWebRTCSvc (Remote Desktop Services WebRTC Redirector) is running and set to Automatic. 4) If still failing: delete Teams AppData (C:\Users\<user>\AppData\Local\Microsoft\Teams), sign out of Cloud PC, wait 5 min, sign back in.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/🤝Dependencies/Microsoft Teams/Issues Related to AVD Media Optimization

symptom: Teams shows AVD Media Not Connected after sign-in on Cloud PC; RD WebRTC Redirector service is stopped or not configured for automatic startup
rootCause: Remote Desktop Services WebRTC Redirector service (RDWebRTCSvc) is not running on the Cloud PC, preventing Teams WebRTC redirection connection required for AVD media optimization
solution: Open Services on Cloud PC, set Remote Desktop Services WebRTC Redirector to Automatic startup and start it. Verify with: sc query RDWebRTCSvc. Sign out, quit and restart Teams.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/🤝Dependencies/Microsoft Teams/Issues Related to AVD Media Optimization

symptom: Cloud PC in Microsoft Hosted Network (MHN) fails outbound connections; Palo Alto firewall HIP checks fail on reconnect placing user in restricted VLAN
rootCause: MHN outbound SNAT holds ports for 60s (FIN close) or 15s (RST close) to prevent port exhaustion. Palo Alto HIP checks (health checks on first connect) fail when encountering SNAT port reuse behavior within the hold window.
solution: Contact Palo Alto support to address HIP check behavior with SNAT port reuse. Azure/W365 cannot adjust port allocation range or SNAT port-reuse behavior for MHN - this is by design and RFC 793 compliant.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/🤝Dependencies/Networking/SNAT Port Allocation. Microsoft Hosted Network | MHN

symptom: Cloud PC users cannot access certain web services or websites; requests are blocked or rejected by the web service provider
rootCause: Cloud PC uses Azure Data Center IP addresses (Virtual Machine IPs) for outbound traffic. Some web service providers block access from Azure DC IP ranges.
solution: Use browser developer mode to reproduce and identify which component rejects the request. Customer contacts web service provider to unblock Azure DC IP ranges. Azure IP reference: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=56519
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/🤝Dependencies/Networking/Unable to access web service in CPC

symptom: Large Cloud PC provisioning batch fails with service error or NameResolutionFailure for Windows365 endpoint; multiple users in same vNet experience simultaneous soft hangs, slowness or disconnections; customer has 0.0.0.0/0 default route routing all Cloud PC traffic through VPN Gateway
rootCause: VPN Gateway overwhelmed by packet volume from Cloud PCs. With AES256+SHA256 algorithm, VpnGw2AZ handles only 52,000 pps per tunnel - insufficient for large deployments. Azure Networking confirmed packet count exceeded 100k threshold.
solution: 1) Upgrade VPN Gateway to higher SKU. 2) Change algorithm to GCMAES256 (120k pps vs 52k with AES256/SHA256). 3) Remove 0.0.0.0/0 default route from Cloud PC vNet. Monitor VPN GW packet metrics. Engage Azure Networking team for root cause confirmation.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/🤝Dependencies/Networking/VPN Gateway sizing cause issues with provisioning, performance and-or connectivity

symptom: SharePoint site configured for auto-sync via Intune policy takes 8 or more hours to appear and sync on Cloud PC
rootCause: OneDrive sync timer behavior - not a Windows 365 issue. The Timerautomount registry key must be set to trigger sync promptly.
solution: 1) Set HKCU\Software\Microsoft\OneDrive\Accounts\Business1 QWORD Timerautomount = 1. 2) Close and reopen OneDrive app (mandatory). The key is removed after sync completes. Automate via scheduled task or Intune remediation script with user credentials to set the key periodically.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/🤝Dependencies/SharePoint & OneDrive/Sharepoint Sites sync without delay

symptom: Windows 365 GPU-enabled Cloud PC shows low FPS (<60), choppy video, or GPU not detected in Task Manager. WebGL test (webglsamples.org/dynamic-cubemap) shows FPS below 60.
rootCause: GPU registry keys (DWMFRAMEINTERVAL, DisplayRefreshRate, bEnumerateHWBeforeSW, AVCHardwareEncodePreferred) not properly set during provisioning, or CPC needs restart after key changes.
solution: 1) Verify GPU presence via PowerShell: Get-WmiObject -Query "Select * FROM Win32_VideoController WHERE NOT Caption LIKE '%Hyper-V%'". 2) Check registry: DWMFRAMEINTERVAL=15, DisplayRefreshRate=60, bEnumerateHWBeforeSW=1, AVCHardwareEncodePreferred=1, AVC444ModePreferred=1 (NVIDIA only, not AMD). 3) Restart CPC if keys correct but FPS low. 4) Check display Hz >= 59. 5) Reduce connected displays if driving too many (e.g. 4K 120Hz or 8K 60Hz).
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Dependencies/Azure VM/GPU Enabled CPC

symptom: Cloud PC DeviceModel changed to 'Virtual Machine' in Intune. Devices fall out of dynamic groups or filters using 'Cloud PC' device model syntax.
rootCause: Third-party app (e.g., Carbon Black) blocks the SetDeviceModel scheduled task, or environment variable changes prevent PowerShell from running. After VM restart, SystemProductName registry reverts to 'Virtual Machine' and startup task fails to restore it.
solution: 1) Check registry HKLM\SYSTEM\ControlSet001\Control\SystemInformation\SystemProductName - should show Cloud PC license name. 2) Check HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\DeviceManageabilityCSP\Provider\CPC_Agent_Server. 3) Open Task Scheduler, verify SetDeviceModel task runs without errors. 4) Workaround: New-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\ControlSet001\Control\SystemInformation' -Name 'SystemProductName' -Value 'Cloud PC Enterprise 4vCPU/16GB/128GB' -Force. 5) Identify root cause blocking the scheduled task. Ref ICM: 344711142.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Dependencies/Intune/Cloud PC DeviceModel changed to "Virtual Machine"

symptom: Windows App for macOS crashes when user attempts to join a Teams Town Hall meeting from Cloud PC using Teams desktop client. Issue does not occur with Teams web client or when using Windows App for Windows.
rootCause: Teams media optimization (VDI 1.0) does not support Town Hall meetings in Teams. The crash occurs only on macOS Windows App with media optimization enabled.
solution: Turn off media optimizations and join the Town Hall meeting, or connect to the Cloud PC using the web client instead of the macOS Windows App.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Dependencies/Microsoft Teams/Joining Teams Town Hall Broadcast meeting crashes Windows App on Mac

symptom: Screen Capture Protection (SCP) causes the presenter shared screen to show as a black screen with only the mouse cursor visible to receiving participants. Occurs in Teams on AVD/W365 with Slimcore VDI 2.0 optimization enabled.
rootCause: Known limitation in Slimcore VDI 2.0 optimization - SCP conflicts with Teams screen sharing functionality, blocking the screen content from being transmitted to other participants.
solution: Disable Screen Capture Protection (SCP) if screen sharing is required in Teams meetings. This is a known issue tracked by the VDI 2.0 team.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Dependencies/Microsoft Teams/Known Issues

symptom: In Citrix VDI 2.0 environment, Teams app sharing sessions freeze for other participants when presenter is on VDA 2402 and CWA for Windows 2309.1 or higher. Freeze triggered when a video element is destroyed (e.g. participant turns off camera or presenter maximizes call monitor).
rootCause: Bug in Citrix VDA 2402 + CWA 2309.1+ interaction with Slimcore VDI 2.0 when video element is destroyed during app sharing session.
solution: Stopping and resharing the window resolves the issue temporarily. Permanent fix available in new Teams version 24335.206.X.X or higher.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Dependencies/Microsoft Teams/Known Issues

symptom: Teams calls drop on the local machine that has an HID peripheral connected if a user launches a virtual desktop from that same local machine and logs into Teams with VDI 2.0 (Slimcore) optimization.
rootCause: Known issue with Slimcore VDI 2.0 - HID peripheral connected to the local endpoint conflicts with Teams running both locally and in the virtual desktop session simultaneously.
solution: Known issue - avoid running Teams simultaneously on the local machine and in the virtual desktop when HID peripherals are connected. Disconnect HID peripheral or close local Teams before launching virtual desktop Teams.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Dependencies/Microsoft Teams/Known Issues

symptom: Cloud PC (or Azure VM without public IP) cannot access certain websites. Website blocks connections from SNAT source port range 1024-1087 allocated by Azure Default Outbound Access. Issue is not limited to Cloud PCs but affects all Azure VMs without static public IP.
rootCause: Azure Default Outbound Access uses SNAT with ephemeral ports starting from 1024-1087. Some websites block traffic from this low source port range despite it being valid per RFC 6056 (valid range 1024-65535).
solution: 1) Configure proxy inside Cloud PC guest OS to bypass Azure SNAT port allocation. 2) Create Azure NAT Gateway on the same subnet to provide dedicated public IP for SNAT (incurs additional cost). 3) Long-term: request website owner to unblock source port range 1024-1087. Use Geneva Action List-NAT-Range to confirm allocated port range.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Dependencies/Networking/Outbound Connection Issue for Cloud PC

symptom: Cloud PC on Microsoft Hosted Network (MHN) experiences network session drops related to Palo Alto firewall HIP (Host Identity Profile) checks. SNAT port held for 60s after FIN close and 15s after RST close; new connection with same source port reuses previously allocated SNAT port during hold period.
rootCause: Palo Alto HIP checks (health checks on first network connect) fail, causing user to be placed in a restricted VLAN. SNAT port-reuse behavior during hold time is by design to avoid port exhaustion and compliant with RFC 793.
solution: Contact Palo Alto support to investigate HIP check failures. SNAT port allocation range and reuse behavior cannot be adjusted from Microsoft side - this is by design. Customer should work with Palo Alto to resolve HIP check configuration.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Dependencies/Networking/SNAT Port Allocation. Microsoft Hosted Network | MHN

symptom: Cloud PC cannot access certain web services because web service providers block Azure Data Center IPs used as outbound traffic
rootCause: Web service providers block Azure Data Center IP ranges that Cloud PCs use for outbound connections
solution: Use browser developer mode to identify which web service component rejects the request, then customer contacts the web service provider to unblock Azure IPs
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Dependencies/Networking/Unable to access web service in CPC

symptom: Large Cloud PC provisioning batch fails with 'We encountered a service error'; users experience soft hangs, slowness, or disconnections in same vNet with default route to VPN Gateway
rootCause: Default route sends all Cloud PC traffic through VPN Gateway, overwhelming it; packet rate exceeds VPN Gateway SKU capacity (e.g., VpnGw2AZ with AES256 & SHA256 handles only 52K pps vs 120K with GCMAES256)
solution: Options: 1) Upgrade VPN Gateway to higher SKU; 2) Change encryption algorithm to GCMAES256 for better throughput (120K pps); 3) Remove default route 0.0.0.0/0 to route traffic differently. Monitor VPN GW metrics per https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/vpn-gateway/monitor-vpn-gateway. Collaborate with Azure Networking team to validate.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Dependencies/Networking/VPN Gateway sizing cause issues with provisioning, performance and-or connectivity

symptom: SharePoint site auto-sync configured via Intune policy takes 8+ hours to appear on Cloud PC
rootCause: OneDrive sync timing behavior (not a Windows 365 issue) - the Timerautomount registry key needs to be set to trigger faster sync
solution: Set registry key HKCU\Software\Microsoft\OneDrive\Accounts\Business1\Timerautomount (QWORD=1), then close and reopen OneDrive app. Key is removed after sync completes; use Intune remediation script for persistence.
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Dependencies/SharePoint & OneDrive/Sharepoint Sites sync without delay

symptom: DeviceLock policy (Max Inactivity Time Device Lock) not working on Windows 365 Cloud PCs with Citrix HDX Plus - lock screen does not appear after idle time despite policy being applied
rootCause: Citrix VDA overrides the display-required state, preventing Windows from triggering the lock screen on idle timeout. The SetDisplayRequiredMode registry key is not set by default.
solution: Add registry key on the Cloud PC and reboot: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Citrix\Graphics, Value Name: SetDisplayRequiredMode, Type: DWORD, Value: 0. This applies to any scenario with Citrix VDA (Windows 365 with Citrix integration or Citrix cloud solution).
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Partner Connectors/Citrix HDX Plus/Known issues/DeviceLock policy functionality is broken

symptom: Windows 365 Graph API returns HTTP 403 Forbidden when using Python requests module to call cloudPCs endpoint, despite having CloudPC.Read.All application permission granted
rootCause: Python requests module does not pass a readable User-Agent header (or omits it entirely), causing the Graph endpoint to reject the request with 403
solution: Add a User-Agent string to the HTTP request headers. Any value works; it does not need to be a standard browser user-agent. Example: headers['User-Agent'] = 'MyApp/1.0'
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Dependencies/Microsoft Graph/Graph API (REST Endpoints)/Known Issues/Windows 365 Graph API Error 403 Forbidden when using Python script

symptom: Windows 365 Migration API importSnapshot fails with error MissingProvisionPolicy - assigned user lacks a provisioning policy
rootCause: The user specified in assignedUserId does not have a provisioning policy assigned in the Windows 365 admin portal
solution: Assign a provisioning policy to the target user before calling the importSnapshot API. Ensure the policy has provisioningSourceType set to 'snapshot'
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Dependencies/Microsoft Graph/Migration API/Troubleshooting

symptom: Windows 365 Migration API importSnapshot fails with InvalidVHD error - VHD file is not a fixed hard disk format (VHDX or dynamic VHD rejected)
rootCause: The uploaded VHD file is in VHDX or dynamic VHD format. Windows 365 snapshot import only supports fixed-format VHD files
solution: Convert the VHD to fixed format before upload. Use Convert-VHD PowerShell cmdlet: Convert-VHD -Path source.vhdx -DestinationPath dest.vhd -VHDType Fixed. Ensure data file is 10MB-2TB and OS disk only (no data disks)
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Dependencies/Microsoft Graph/Migration API/Troubleshooting

symptom: Windows 365 Migration API importSnapshot fails with InvalidSasUrl error - SAS URL is not in Azure storage domain or not accessible by W365 service
rootCause: The SAS URL provided is not from an Azure storage domain, or the Windows 365 service cannot access the blob due to firewall/permission restrictions
solution: Ensure the SAS URL points to an Azure page blob (not block blob). Verify the storage account firewall allows access from W365 service. Check SAS token expiry and permissions (Read required). URL must be under 1024 bytes
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Dependencies/Microsoft Graph/Migration API/Troubleshooting

symptom: Windows 365 Migration API importSnapshot fails with TooManyRequests error - daily request limit exceeded (more than 1000 requests per day)
rootCause: The tenant has exceeded the daily request limit of 1000 importSnapshot API calls
solution: Wait until the next day for the limit to reset. Implement rate limiting and batching in the migration automation. Spread large migration waves across multiple days
sourceRef: Supportability/Windows365/Windows365 Support Wiki:/Features/Dependencies/Microsoft Graph/Migration API/Troubleshooting

symptom: Azure systems fail to boot with a 0x1E KMODE_EXCEPTION_NOT_HANDLED bugcheck. The bugcheck is almost immediate after the system is turned on (<1 second). The first bugcheck parameter is 0xc0000096.
rootCause: Spectre/Meltdown mitigation related issue that is mitigated in current kernel patch versions (July 2018 and newer).
solution: Change registry value to allow system to boot so current patch levels can be applied: 1. Attach the broken VM OS disk to a recovery VM. 2. Load the system registry hive using regedit. 3. Change FeatureSettingsOverride from 8 to 3 under HKLMSYSTEMCurrentControlSetControlSession ManagerMemory ManagementFeatureSettingOverride. 4. Conduct a disk swap and apply latest patches.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#96771

symptom: Logging in to VM joined to Azure AD DS with an Azure AD user sourced from Windows Server (on prem) Active Directory fails. Users synchronized to Azure AD through Azure AD Connect. Error in Diagnostics: ErrorSource: RDBroker, ErrorOperation: Orchestration.
rootCause: By design: strict enforcement on UPN to enable reliable re-connections and no duplication of sessions. When synchronized from AD to Azure AD, the user comes with a SID. However, the identity on Azure AD DS has a different SID, so login is blocked.
solution: Organizations using Azure AD DS for their AVD environment need users created directly in Azure AD.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#107802

symptom: Windows 10 Enterprise multi-session: signing in with an administrative account shows notification "Remote Desktop licensing mode is not configured, Remote Desktop Services will stop working in X days."
sourceRef: ContentIdea#108790

symptom: WVD shows "No Healthy RDSH Available" with SxSStacklistenerNotready status on custom image.
rootCause: Customer has Windows 10 Pro installed from the Azure marketplace to create managed custom image (unsupported OS version).
solution: Scoping: 1. Check Remote Desktop Service running and RDP works. 2. Check RDAgent and RDAgentBootloader running. 3. Check HKLMSOFTWAREMicrosoftRDInfraAgentisRegistered is set to 1. 4. Review Agent install log at C:Program FilesMicrosoft RDInfraAgentInstall.txt. 5. Ensure supported OS version per https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-desktop/overview#supported-virtual-machine-os-images.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#112352

symptom: After creating a new WVD Host Pool, users see a Gateway error when they try to connect.
rootCause: The AAD account used as an RDS Owner for the tenant is not associated with a Windows Server AD account.
solution: Redeploy the Host Pool using an "RDS Owner" account sourced from the Windows Server AD.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#112710

symptom: Scenario:User adds the Azure AD Domain Services for use with Windows Virtual Desktop. After deploying the host pool, users are unable to log into the VM using their AAD accounts.
rootCause: The password hash between the AAD and the AADDS accounts has not been synced because the AAD accounts existed prior to adding AADDS.
solution: Users will need to reset their password to sync the password hash between AAD and AADDS.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#112711

symptom: WVD Deployment failed with DSC extension error. User is not authorized to query the management service.
rootCause: SPN was missing role assignment.
solution: Create service principal role assignment per docs.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#113529

symptom: WVD login behavior changed after client version .127. Client keeps prompting for credentials.
rootCause: Issue with WVD client version released after .127
solution: Resolved with new WVD client version .605.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#113742

symptom: WVD gateway error. Kusto shows Failed to add user to Remote Desktop Users group: Win32.ERROR_NO_SUCH_MEMBER.
rootCause: UPN translation fails in cross-domain trust scenarios.
solution: Use actual domain UPN instead of email account.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#115171

symptom: WVD host pool deployment fails with ResourceDeploymentFailure WinRM related.
rootCause: GPO Allow remote server management through WinRM had restrictive filters.
solution: Change IPv4/IPv6 filter to * in GPO.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#117649

symptom: WVD host pool deployment fails: User not authorized and Host pool does not exist in Tenant.
rootCause: Incorrect tenant name in host pool wizard.
solution: Deploy with correct tenant name.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#117705

symptom: RDClient Subscribe error: Something went wrong, could not authenticate. AAD.BrokerPlugin activation failed for admin with full token.
rootCause: UAC Admin Approval Mode disabled for Built-in Administrator.
solution: Enable UAC Admin Approval Mode, gpupdate /force, re-login.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#117749

symptom: WVD no available resource error. O_AGENT_NOT_CONNECTED and E_LB_NO_SESSIONHOST_AVAILABLE. Agent cannot connect to broker.
rootCause: Network routing UDR incorrect and VNet Peering missing between Azure Firewall VNet and WVD VNet.
solution: Rectify routing and set up VNet Peering.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#118649

symptom: Users get error while accessing WVD hostpool: Oops, we could not connect to Hostpool Name. We could not connect to the gateway because of an error. If this keeps happening, ask your admin or tech support for help.
rootCause: Two major reasons: 1) rdgateway.wvd.microsoft.com is not allowed through firewall or proxy. 2) DNS forwarder is not configured properly to resolve DNS Non Authoritative requests.
solution: Configure the DNS forwarders to forward DNS queries for Non-Authoritative Answers. Open Server Manager > Tools > DNS > DNS Manager. Right click DNS Server > Properties > Forwarders tab > Edit. Add appropriate forwarder IPs.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#118928

symptom: WVD Fall 2019 Release: selecting Power as Usage Profile shows NaN instead of VM count. Validation passes for Light, Medium, Heavy, Custom but fails for Power.
rootCause: Known issue in Marketplace, fixed in next WVD Marketplace release.
solution: Workaround: select Custom option and manually enter number of VMs. Power option calculates VMs assuming each user needs 1 vCPU.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#119246

symptom: Need to restrict one-way clipboard redirection: allow paste TO remote desktop but prevent copy FROM remote session to local machine.
rootCause: Clipboard redirection only works bidirectionally by design.
solution: Not possible with RDP or WVD. Clipboard redirection is either enabled or disabled both ways. Controlled via GPO: Do not allow Clipboard redirection set to Enabled.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#119630

symptom: Azure portal shows Session Ending and Experiencing Authentication Issues when assigning users to WVD Application Group. The +Add option is greyed out for existing Application Groups.
rootCause: Conditional Access Policy requiring users to accept TOU for data storage. Azure Portal tries to obtain token for Microsoft Graph which depends on SharePoint Online.
solution: 1) Remove the Conditional Access Policy. 2) Have user sign in to SharePoint first to accept TOU, then sign in to Azure Portal. Alternative: assign users manually via PowerShell.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#119634

symptom: After subscribing in MSRDC or RDWEB client, users see: Unavailable - There are currently no resource assigned to you at rdweb.wvd.microsoft.com feed discovery endpoint.
rootCause: No workspace created/assigned for application group, and/or it was not registered.
solution: Create a workspace and register the application group to it. After registration, the resource will appear in WVD client.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#120552

symptom: Unable to deploy Azure Monitor for RDS and WVD with CSP subscription. Error: This offer is not available for subscriptions from Microsoft Azure cloud solution providers.
rootCause: Azure Monitor for RDS and WVD is not available in CSP subscriptions.
solution: Not available in CSP. Not all Azure Marketplace items are available in Azure CSP subscriptions.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#120621

symptom: WVD ARM deployment fails: The template deployment vmCreation-linkedTemplate is not valid according to the validation procedure. Error appears after validation succeeded.
rootCause: Azure subscription quota limit exceeded for a region or VM family.
solution: Check detailed error by clicking error banner in Portal. Request quota increase for affected region/VM family.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#120704

symptom: WVD ARM deployment fails with terminal provisioning state Failed when processing extension dscextension. DeploymentFailed error in JSON output.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#120705

symptom: By default RDP setting created to WVD resource will have drive redirection setting as drivestoredirect:s:* If we access WVD local drive will be redirected to a session host.
rootCause: RDP file at C:\Users\Username\AppData\Local\rdclientwpf\GUID contains drivestoredirect:s:* which redirects all local drives to the session host.
solution: Spring 2020 update: Use PowerShell Update-AzWvdHostPool -ResourceGroupName <RG> -Name <hostpool> -CustomRdpProperty drivestoredirect:s: to set empty. Fall 2019: Use Set-RdsHostPool -Name <hostpool> -TenantName <tenant> -CustomRdpProperty drivestoredirect:s: to set empty.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#121118

symptom: When logged in to WVD VMs, the user gets licensing warning about Remote Desktop licensing mode not configured. Sudden disconnections also occur across all WVD machines.
rootCause: Registry key HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server\RCM\Licensing Core\LicensingMode was set to 5.
solution: Set the LicensingMode registry value to 6.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#123798

symptom: WVD Classic: Users cannot subscribe to WVD feed in desktop client or login to web client. Error AADSTS50105: The signed in user is not assigned to a role for the application (Windows Virtual Desktop Client or Windows Virtual Desktop AME). Error code CAA20004.
rootCause: In WVD Classic, User assignment required was enabled on Windows Virtual Desktop AME or Windows Virtual Desktop Client enterprise app in Azure AD, blocking users without explicit role assignment.
solution: In Azure AD Enterprise applications, set User assignment required to No for both Windows Virtual Desktop AME and Windows Virtual Desktop Client (default setting). Adding all users with TenantCreator or Default Access role is not recommended.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#124311

symptom: Need to disable local drive visibility on WVD RD Client.
rootCause: Drive redirection is enabled by default and needs GPO configuration to block.
solution: Configure GPO: Computer Configuration\Policies\Administrative Templates\Windows Components\Remote Desktop Services\Remote Desktop Session Host\Device and Resource Redirection. Enable Do not allow drive redirection and link to OU with WVD Session host computer accounts. Note: This policy works at machine level for RD Client app only, not for WVD Web client.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#125208

symptom: RDClient as a Published Citrix App fails to do Feed Discovery for WVD. Error: Failed to find the cloud instance for user. Works when launched locally or via browser.
rootCause: Citrix remote app uses InitialProgram RDP option to launch WVD Client in place of Explorer. WVD Client uses WAM for authentication, which depends on Immersive Shell normally started by Explorer. Without it, WAM UI cannot spawn.
solution: PG confirmed WVD Client was not designed/tested for Citrix published app usage. Use HTML Client or WVD Client as standalone app instead.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#126369

symptom: Depth First Load Balancing keeps reverting to Breadth First every 5 minutes due to WVD Automation runbook/Logic App scaling script.
rootCause: The WVD scaling tool script enforces breadth-first load balancing by default, overriding manual settings.
solution: Add SkipUpdateLoadBalancerType: true to the HTTP request body in the Logic App scheduler.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#126371

symptom: Users get self-signed certificate not trusted prompt via WVD desktop client and cannot proceed.
rootCause: CredSSP for RDP was disabled in the Azure portal (enablecredsspsupport:i:0).
solution: Change RDP setting to RDP will use CredSSP from WVD portal and refresh subscriptions in MSRDC client.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#147012

symptom: Unable to access WVD VM via desktop client only from organization laptops. Works from personal laptops. Error: Logon attempt failed.
rootCause: Organization machine settings do not support NTLMv2 authentication. NTLM auth fails with 0xC000006A.
solution: Create registry DWORD lmcompatibilitylevel=5 at HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa and reboot client.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#147013

symptom: Need to disable file upload option on WVD sessions. drivestoredirect:s:1 is set in RDP properties.
rootCause: Drivestore redirect value set to 1 in RDP properties for the WVD hostpool.
solution: Classic: Set-RdsHostPool -CustomRdpProperty drivestoredirect:s:0. ARM: Change drivestoredirect from 1 to 0 in Azure portal RDP properties.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#148069

symptom: MSIX App Attach fails: No MSIX Packages could be retrieved from the image Path. Type1: Access Denied. Type2: untrusted signature.
rootCause: Type1: Computer account lacks share permissions. Type2: App signature is untrusted, disk mounts then dismounts.
solution: Type1: Add computer account with Read permission on share (Azure Files: Storage File Data SMB Share Reader role). Type2: Import app Publisher Certificate under Trusted People.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#148316

symptom: Excel shows incorrect/limited number of processors in Windows 10 Enterprise Multi-session VM, causing slowness.
rootCause: In certain VMs Excel is forced to use limited processors to preserve other apps performance.
solution: Add registry: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Office\Common, DWORD IdealConcurrencyValueOverride=0.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#149473

symptom: Unable to connect to WVD VM. Reverse connect to rdgateway failed with error 0x80072F7D.
rootCause: GPO controlling ECC curve order enabled on WVD VMs restricting SSL cipher configuration.
solution: Disable ECC curve order GPO or remove EccCurves registry under SSL config.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#149571

symptom: How to disable Maximize/Minimize buttons from Remote Desktop Connection so WVD users cannot take screenshots.
solution: Enable displayconnectionbar:i:0 under hostpool RDP properties. Note: only works with WVD sessions, not MSTSC.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#149584

symptom: Issue - RDP property changes through Azure portal under HostPool is not working while accessing the WVD through Windows Virtual Desktop Client.
rootCause: The Hostpool .RDP under RDClientwpf Folder is not getting updated with the latest RDP properties which we are changing through Azure Portal.
solution: Rename the Hostpool GUID folder under C:\Users\<username>\AppData\Local\rdclientwpf. This will force the WVD client to download the RDP files again with the latest properties.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#150090

symptom: Using Windows Server 2019 with FSLogix or WVD with FSlogix you may encounter Search issues with Search hanging or crashing. Event IDs: 2, 102, 103, 104, 105, 300, 301, 302, 325, 326, 439, 482, 492.
rootCause: SearchIndexer failed to unload per user index cleanly, causing chain impact like index corruption/crash.
solution: Install private fix for RS5/19H1/VB. Enable test signing, install KB, restart.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#150196

symptom: Apps published through MSIX App Attach not displayed under Start Menu or not findable through Windows Search. Get-AppxPackage shows the MSIX package listed.
rootCause: Known issue: using FSlogix or Roaming Profile, the Start Menu breaks so apps are not visible.
solution: Add FSLogix Redirection.xml exclusion for AppData\Local\Packages\Microsoft.Windows.StartMenuExperienceHost_cw5n1h2txyewy\TempState.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#150380

symptom: Adding MSIX Packages VHD(x) path under WVD Hostpool gives error Error Accessing Virtual Disk and Code: 400.
rootCause: Access to WVD where the MSIX packages are published not properly configured.
solution: Assign session host VMs permissions for the storage account and file share: Create AD DS security group, add computer accounts, sync to Azure AD, create storage/file share, join storage to AD DS, assign Storage File Data SMB Share Contributor role.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#150391

symptom: Adding MSIX packages under WVD Hostpool gives error No MSIX packages could be retrieved from the image path. Downloading VHD(x) and mounting throws Access is Denied.
rootCause: MSIX image expansion done under the VHD without creating a root/parent folder inside it.
solution: Create a root folder inside the VHD(x) when expanding MSIX Image with MSIXMGR.exe: msixmgr.exe -Unpack -packagePath <pkg>.msix -destination D:\<root folder> -applyacls.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#150392

symptom: MSIX app attach fails to import into the WVD Host Pool. No MSIX packages could be retrieved error. Excluding 3rd party services from MSIX package allows adding successfully.
rootCause: UI prevalidation fails for MSIX packages with services unless manifest provides icons for all entry points.
solution: Use PowerShell to add app attach package, or edit manifest to assign icons to all entry points including services. Turn off auto update.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#150566

symptom: MSIX app attach application shortcut not appearing in Start Menu when using FSLogix. Disabling FSlogix or new user shows shortcut correctly.
rootCause: FSLogix profiles contain start menu layout cache under %localappdata%\Microsoft\Windows\Shell that overwrites start menu on machine.
solution: Apply Redirections.xml via FSLogix with exclusion for AppData\Local\Packages\Microsoft.Windows.StartMenuExperienceHost_cw5n1h2txyewy\TempState.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#150739

symptom: Users cannot connect using AVD client (freshly installed). Web client works fine. Subscribe fails with error code 0x80004005. RDClient trace shows Authentication failed because the remote party has closed the transport stream.
rootCause: Client machine missing required TLS cipher suites (TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_256_GCM_SHA384, etc). AVD gateway and RDWeb are behind AFD.
solution: Add all required cipher suites supported by Azure Front Door on the client machine.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#154708

symptom: AADJ VMs in AVD: Web Client connection fails with We could not connect because of a security error. PKU2U enabled, VM User Login role assigned.
rootCause: Per-user MFA is enabled. Per-user Enabled/Enforced Azure AD Multi-Factor Authentication is not supported for VM sign-in.
solution: Remove per user MFA. Configure MFA using conditional access policy instead.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#155411

symptom: AAD joined AVD machine: Admin Users get error We could not connect to the remote PC because of a security error. Normal users can login. AAD Operational logs show Event ID 1081 and 1160.
rootCause: Azure AD Security Default Settings is enabled, requiring administrators to do MFA.
solution: Disable Security Defaults: Azure portal -> Azure AD -> Properties -> Manage security defaults -> Set to No.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#157015

symptom: AVD error: we are working on refreshing your token, please try again after a short while or refresh your page
rootCause: Network issues causing user connectivity drop when session cannot talk to the on prem DC.
solution: Open all dynamic ports on session hosts. Engage networking to check packet drops between session host and on prem DC. Collect netmon traces if needed.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#157646

symptom: MSIX App Attach X64 apps do not register on user logon when in CIM format. App is staged but not present for user.
rootCause: Issue in Win 10 20H2 and 21H1 when requesting On Demand Registration with CIM format.
solution: Use VHDx instead of CIM disks in X64 format. Or run Add-AppxPackage registration script manually.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#158410

symptom: AVD users launching remote app for first time have 5 min wait on Preparing Windows until successful launch.
rootCause: RunOnce key for OneDrive causes rdpinit.exe to wait 5 min (hardcoded timeout) on runonce.exe.
solution: Remove OneDrive key from RunOnce in default user profile hive.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#159753

symptom: Windows 10 AVD: Terminal service crash 0xC0000409 subcode 0xA in termsrv!CRemoteConnectionManager::UALInstrument during logon.
rootCause: Bug: CRemoteConnectionManager members pfnUalStart/pfnUalStop/pfnUalInstrument not properly initialized.
solution: Fixed in Windows 11. Apply latest Windows updates.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#160264

symptom: In AVD or RDP: The number of connections to this computer is limited and all connections are in use. No actual limit reached.
rootCause: GPO policy Limit number of connections restricting connections.
solution: Check MaxInstanceCount registry and set to not configured (ffffffff).
sourceRef: ContentIdea#160365

symptom: Start VM on Connect enabled but VM does not start. Error: There was a problem connecting to the remote resource.
rootCause: AVD service principal lacks required RBAC permissions to start VMs.
solution: Create custom RBAC role with start/read/instanceView permissions and assign to Windows Virtual Desktop service principal.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#160931

symptom: Start VM on Connect enabled but VM does not start. Error: no available resources.
rootCause: VM was shutdown while active session present. Known bug.
solution: Update RBAC role permissions for Start VM on Connect.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#160932

symptom: AVD session hosts Bugcheck 0xA IRQL_NOT_LESS_OR_EQUAL from Desktop Client. USB redirection causing invalid memory access via Intel Wireless Bluetooth driver tsusbhub.
rootCause: USB redirection of Intel Wireless Bluetooth driver accessing invalid memory.
solution: Downgrade to Intel Wireless Bluetooth driver version 21.110.0.3.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#161069

symptom: ConnectionFailedAdTrustedRelationshipFailure: service cannot add user to Remote Desktop Users group, session host lacks AD credentials.
rootCause: RPC communication issue with Domain Controller, blocked dynamic ephemeral ports.
solution: Open necessary RPC/dynamic ephemeral ports between session hosts and DC.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#162556

symptom: Not able to connect to AVD session host using AVD HTML Client.
rootCause: Customer had added Azure AD extension targetisaadjoined:i:1 to RDP advanced properties.
solution: Fix the issue by removing the extension targetisaadjoined:i:1 as customer was using Hybrid domain join not Azure AD setup.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#162568

symptom: Unable to delete AVD resources from both Azure portal and PowerShell. Migration from Classic HostPool to ARM incomplete. Delete fails with Cannot delete HostPool in Migrating state (Code: 400).
rootCause: Host Pool Migration was still in Migration state.
solution: Complete or revert the migration. PowerShell: Revert-RdsHostPoolMigration or Complete-RdsHostPoolMigration.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#168523

symptom: Session host VMs going in Unavailable status. Reinstalling agents with new registration token does not help.
rootCause: RDAgentBootLoader registry missing default agent entry under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\RDAgentBootLoader.
solution: Check registry HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\RDAgentBootLoader. Create the missing agent key matching the existing syntax. Restart session hosts.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#168533

symptom: Fails to get the print job from AVD clients to Dell Wyse thin client.
rootCause: Print job spooled from AVD but not reaching Dell Wyse device.
solution: Update Dell Wyse firmware and client version to latest.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#169257

symptom: Assigning VMs to users using PowerShell CLI fails with No resource group error for personal hostpool.
rootCause: Multiple subscriptions present, CLI using default subscription but VMs reside on different subscription.
solution: Use Set-AzContext to switch to the correct subscription where the resource group resides.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#169258

symptom: Intermittently black screen appears and session disconnects. Winlogon termination 4005 event in application event log.
rootCause: Session not terminated completely on logoff. At around 250-260 session IDs, netprofm.dll causes the issue.
solution: Known issue fixed in 20H1 (2004) and later. For earlier versions, schedule netprofm service restart at regular intervals. Upgrade OS recommended.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#169261

symptom: Similar remote apps (e.g. Chrome) on AVD desktop client overlap on the taskbar.
rootCause: Taskbar grouping controlled by local Windows settings, not AVD.
solution: On client system: Settings -> Taskbar -> Combine Taskbar buttons -> Select NEVER.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#169616

symptom: Unable to configure custom alert when scaling plan is enabled/disabled for AVD hostpool. No logs found in Log Analytics.
rootCause: Data/logs not available by design. Scaling plan assignment activities are not logged.
solution: PG confirmed log location not available. Feature change request filed. Logs will be available after design change.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#169618

symptom: Multiple applications crash (0x80000003) in AVD pooled session using FSLogix profile. Temp profiles appear. FSLogix logs show VHD(x) reattachment warnings (Event 46).
rootCause: No space available for FSLogix profile size.
solution: Increase FSLogix profile container size. Update FSLogix version if outdated.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#169665

symptom: Adding VM to existing host pool fails. Windows update error 0x8024401c.
rootCause: Corrupted OS update KB5017315 found in CBS logs.
solution: Run DISM /Online /Cleanup-image /Restorehealth and sfc /scannow. Download and inject update: Dism /online /Add-Package /packagepath:C:\Temp\KB5017315.cab.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#171126

symptom: AVD VM status becomes unavailable with health check status 'DomainTrustCheck' failed. Cannot fix by reinstalling AVD agents.
rootCause: Wrong DNS setting which cannot resolve the domain controller, resulting in domain trust failure.
solution: Check DNS settings and modify to a proper DNS which can resolve the Domain Controller.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#172400

symptom: WVD Classic: All session host status changed to 'Shutdown' after OS upgrade (21H2 to 22H2). Users cannot connect, error 0x3000046 'no available resources'.
rootCause: After OS upgrade, session host failed to register automatically to the host pool as the registration key was not valid/expired.
solution: Uninstall all agent/boot loader/stack programs. Remove session host from host pool (Remove-RdsSessionHost). Generate new registration key (New-RdsRegistrationInfo). Reinstall agent and boot loader, re-register VM.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#175220

symptom: AVD VM not available due to 'SxSStackListenerCheck' failed error. Remote Desktop Services SxS Network Stack Error 1722.
rootCause: DisableRegistryTools registry key on VMs prevents system account from editing registry, causing Agent stack installation to fail.
solution: Use PsExec to launch regedit in system context (psexec.exe -s -i regedit.exe). Delete registry key HKEY_USERS\S-1-5-18\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\DisableRegistryTools. Or remove GPO 'Prevent access to registry editing tools'.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#177288

symptom: Few users unable to login to AAD joined AVD VMs. Error: 'Sign in failed. Please check your user name and password.' RDSTLS authentication error code 1326.
rootCause: Duplicate UPN entries for affected user accounts in on-prem AD synced via Azure AD Connect.
solution: Remove duplicate UPN entries in on-prem AD and reset passwords for affected users.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#177983

symptom: Azure AD joined Windows 11 AVD VMs: users get 'The sign-in method you're trying to use isn't allowed'. Most users cannot login, only few can.
rootCause: AVD VMs deployed via Intune never got into managed state. Intune compliance shows 'See ConfigMgr' instead of 'Compliant' for non-working machines.
solution: Ensure machines are successfully provisioned and managed in Intune. Once machines reach managed state with proper compliance status, login issue resolves.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#180337

symptom: AVD (Classic) PowerShell error "User is not authorized to query the management service."
rootCause: Customer account has no "RDS Owner" role on the tenant. Error: WVD_50001: user is not authorized, code=Forbidden errorCode=UnauthorizedAccess.
solution: Ask PG to manually grant the customer account the RDS Owner role on the AVD Classic tenant using New-RdsRoleAssignment cmdlet with -RoleDefinitionName "RDS Owner".
sourceRef: ContentIdea#172841

symptom: Azure KMS Activation on Azure VMs running Windows 10 Enterprise for Virtual desktops fails with error 0xC004F074 (SL_E_VL_BINDING_SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE).
rootCause: Microsoft announced new KMS IP addresses 20.118.99.224 and 40.83.235.53 (azkms.core.windows.net) replacing old address 23.102.135.246 (kms.core.windows.net). Firewall rules only allowed old IP; activation request redirected to new endpoints which were blocked.
solution: Add new Azure KMS IP addresses (20.118.99.224 and 40.83.235.53) to firewall rules. Verify with Test-NetConnection to both old and new endpoints on port 1688.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#175319

symptom: WVD/AVD session hosts become unresponsive, users unable to logon. Server hang, sessions disconnected, sessions fail to initialize with GraphicsInitializationTimeout.
rootCause: RDPCoreCDV.dll regression in latest RDP SXS stack caused a two-thread deadlock via Critical Section object. Reference: Incident-390450332.
solution: AVD Product Group rolled back RDP-SXS stack from rdp-sxs230307400 (faulty) to rdp-sxs230103930 (last known good). A permanent fix was planned for future RDP-SXS stack release via auto-update.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#176106

symptom: Few users unable to login to AAD joined AVD VMs. Error: Sign in failed. Please check your user name and password. RD console shows LogonFailed(10006) with RDSTLS authentication error code=1326.
rootCause: Duplicate UPN entries for the affected user accounts in on-prem AD synced to Azure AD via Azure AD Connect.
solution: Remove the duplicate UPN entries in on-prem AD and reset the password for the affected users.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#177983

symptom: Users trying to login to Azure AD VMs get error: "The sign-in method you're trying to use isn't allowed." Most users unable to login, few users can.
rootCause: AVD VMs deployed using Intune had broken settings. Provisioned machines never got into a managed state with Intune. Compliance showed 'See ConfigMgr' for non-working machines vs 'Compliant' for working ones.
solution: Engage internal Intune team. Ensure machines are successfully provisioned in Intune managed state. Once Intune provisioning completed, the issue was resolved.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#180337

symptom: File association GPO does not work in AVD remote application environment on Windows 10/11 Enterprise multi-session.
rootCause: Windows 10/11 multi-session lacks the shell32 DefaultAssociationsProfileHandler responsible for processing file association GPO. Server OS (Windows 2019/2022) has explorer.exe shell with the handler.
solution: Import the DefaultAssociationsConfiguration registry from Windows 2019 or 2022 server OS to multi-session AVD session host: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\ProfileNotification\DefaultAssociationsProfileHandler
sourceRef: ContentIdea#184813

symptom: Need to block AVD web client access and allow connection only from AVD Desktop and mobile clients.
solution: Create a Conditional Access policy: select users/groups, target AVD app IDs (9cdead84-a844-4324-93f2-b2e6bb768d07, a4a365df-50f1-4397-bc59-1a1564b8bb9c, 270efc09-cd0d-444b-a71f-39af4910ec45), set conditions to Browser client app, then Block Access. Granularity at hostpool level is not supported.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#185224

symptom: User login to Session Host via RDP/AVD fails with The system administrator has limited the computers you can log on with and Error code 0x1107 when CrashOnAuditFail=2. Devices may reboot with 0xc0000244 bugcheck (STATUS_AUDIT_FAILED).
rootCause: CrashOnAuditFail registry key at HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\LSA was set to 2 after a 0xc0000244 bugcheck. This blocks logon by non-administrators. Root cause: event log writes failed during OS shutdown triggering the crash.
solution: Change CrashOnAuditFail from 2 to 1 and restart. Back up and clear Security logs before restart. Investigate why value transitions from 1 to 2. Install corrective OS updates if available.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#185746

symptom: Upload feature in AVD Web Client is not working when browser language is set to non-English. Upload prompt shows success but file never appears in Uploads folder.
rootCause: Race condition in Web Client JavaScript: folder name strings were not translated before virtual drive creation, causing mismatch between local and protocol-side folder names. Bug 48638042 fixed this.
solution: Change browser primary language to English or use Legacy version of Web Client as workaround. The issue has been fixed in a subsequent product update via setTranslations gallery function.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#186967

symptom: Users unable to connect intermittently with AVD Web Client. Error appears without credential prompt. Kusto shows ConnectionInitiationSequenceTimeout. WebSocket closed with code 1006.
rootCause: Forcepoint WebSense proxy alters HTTP headers: Connection header shows Keep-Alive instead of Upgrade and no Upgrade header for WebSocket. This prevents WebSocket connection to AVD gateway.
solution: Follow up with Forcepoint (WebSense vendor) to exclude AVD gateway URLs from proxy inspection. Ensure WebSocket upgrade headers are not modified by network proxies.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#187857

symptom:
Issue: When Stopping the VM the
Health state of the Session Host goes to Unavailable instead 'Shutdown'    
 
 
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
 
 
 
  VM gets deallocated and no cost
incurred. When the VM was started it comes in available state and accepts
connections. Customer concern was why is it going
to Unavailable state instead of Shutdown when stopped, which is giving the wrong impression to the admins. On investigation found that the Issue
only present on the VMs with "hardened image" not with any
other marketplace image or other custom image which isn't hardened image. Customer is hardening the OS image as per their company security
policy before creating a custom image.   Environment: WVD ARM deployment 
Host pool type: Pooled OS:
Microsoft Windows 10 Enterprise 22H2 64-bit
rootCause:
After
 comparing the Working and Non-working Session Hosts, it was found that, all the
 services listed below are part of �Hyper-V Integration services�  were in disabled state.  
 
  
  
   
   
   
   
   
   
   
   
   
   
   
   
  
  
  
 
  
  
   
 Customer
 disabled these services while hardening the image. 
 Azure
 VM team on the collaboration educated that these services should not be
 disabled as each
 service listed here has its own responsibility to perform. For example:
 Hyper-V Guest Shutdown Service is responsible for gracefully OS shutdown from
 its underlying host when you send Stop call from Azure
 portal/CLI/PowerShell. When disabled, the
 host can't trigger a friendly shutdown inside the virtual machine and
 it may report different type of issues. While hardening the image, these
 services were getting disabled.
solution:
We were able to enable
the "Hyper-V Integration services" as each service listed here
has its own responsibility to perform. After which, we found that when the VM
stops, it now goes to the Shutdown state instead of Unavailable state, which
the expected behavior.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#188487

symptom: The Office Apps were not deploying from the Intune Portal.
rootCause: The Co-Management configuration was not referencing Intune for the App Package deployment.
solution: The SCCM co-management setting was directed to the Intune portal for Office applications. The machine was refreshed and a current SCCM configuration profile, which correctly referenced the Intune portal, was pulled down along with the associated apps. The primary challenge was that the co-management portion of SCCM was not linked to Intune for the deployment of the Office application.

Microsoft Entra ID user assignment list
is not getting populated intermittently for some admins in the AVD blade.

Cx
has a normal and an admin account. Due to SSO enable, user browser session

MS edge Extension and Registry.

symptom: When trying to activate Windows 11 client you may receive the following error message  0xc004f042	-1073418174	SL_E_VL_KEY_MANAGEMENT_SERVICE_ID_MISMATCH	The Software Licensing Service determined that the specified Key Management Service (KMS) cannot be used.	slerror.h Note:  At time we did not know this was Azure Virtual Desktop (AVD)
rootCause: Install was Azure Virtual Desktop (AVD) and it was configured to contact onprem KMS host instead of Azure KMS Hosts
solution: Azure Virtual Desktop (AVD) must use Azure KMS hosts.  Cannot use onprem KMS hosts Windows Enterprise multi-session FAQ - Azure | Microsoft Learn  We don't allow customers to run Windows Enterprise multi-session in production environments outside of the Azure Virtual Desktop service. Only Microsoft or the Azure Virtual Desktop Approved Providers, Citrix and VMware, can provide access to the Azure Virtual Desktop service. It's against the licensing agreement to run Windows multi-session outside of the Azure Virtual Desktop service for production purposes. Windows multi-session also won�t activate against on-premises Key Management Services (KMS).
sourceRef: ContentIdea#192232

symptom: The issue experienced is lack of session connectivity to the AVD Session Hosts affected as the machine is shown as unavailable.  When looking at the Azure Portal, the machine status is seen as follows:   Image 1: Screenshot showing the error message seen in the Azure Portal for a machine in an unavailable state due to a failed SxSStackListenerCheck.  Error message: This health check verifies that the side-by-side stack is up and running, listening and ready to receive connections.  If this check fails, It is fatal, and no connections will succeed.  The agent is taking corrective action to solve this. you may want to restart your VM as well.  If this doesn't work please open a support ticket.   It is very likely that this view will only be available for an administrator of the Azure environment.  If the information you have on your case is a Remote Desktop Client error message, it'll most likely like this:    Image 2: Screenshot of the error message shown when the AVD Session Host is unavailable for a connection due to a failed SxSStackListenerCheck.  Error message: We couldn't connect because there are currently no available resources. Try again later or if this keeps happening. ask your admin or tech support tor help.  This message will be similar on all versions of the Remote Desktop Client.  When checking the "qwinsta" command for the listener in the Session Host, it showed as empty as well:  Image 3: Result of the qwinsta command in the affected machine which doesn't show the Stack Listener.  The services we expected that should be running at the time were running normally.
rootCause: For an issue like this, there could be multiple causes:  Unsupported SKUs: AVD does not support Non Enterprise Client SKU's. For example Windows 10/11 Pro. 32-bit operating systems. N, KN, LTSC, and other editions of Windows operating systems not listed in the previous table. Ultra disks for the OS disk type. Ephemeral OS disks for Azure VMs. Virtual Machine Scale Sets.  Having the DisableRegistryTools key enabled: If this is true, machines might present the following errors:   Image 4: Event ID 11722 showing an installation error for the Remote Desktop Services SxS Network Stack Listener.   Image 5: Event ID 3277 showing an installation error for the Remote Desktop Services SxS Network Stack Listener.
solution:
Taking into account the possible causes for this issue written above, let's take a look at the workarounds.  Unsupported SKUs:   For this issue specifically, there's no workaround. If the customer is using a SKU that is not supported for Azure Virtual Desktop machines, a switch needs to happen in the environment to machines that we support.  Having the DisableRegistryTools key enabled:  If this is the cause that you've identified by the events shown above, here are the registry keys you need to check to find the DisableRegistryTools value: HKU:\.DEFAULT\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\DisableRegistryTools = 1

HKU:\S-1-5-18\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\DisableRegistryTools = 1 (this one is for the SYSTEM account, which is used by our agent to auto-install the stack)

HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\DisableRegistryTools = 1 (this one affects manual installation attempts by the current user)It is possible that when you encounter this issue, you still have an old installation of the Network Stack in the session host and you will need to reinstall it.In order to do it correctly, make sure to follow the guidance on our wiki:SxS Stack Agent Reinstallation Guide
sourceRef: ContentIdea#192289

symptom: After deployment AVD. VM shown as 'Can't connect' status in hostpool.   VM is running on a Server OS.  From WVDAgentUrlTool there is no URLs under 'NOT Accessible URLs'. In the installed program, we can see Remote Desktop Agent Boot Loader and Remote Desktop Services Infrastructure Agent. But below 2 applications are missing: Remote Desktop Services Infrastructure Geneva Agent Remote Desktop Services SxS Network Stack
rootCause: Remote Desktop Session Host role is required for server OS.  This is not mentioned in prerequisites. But it's mentioned in the step 2 of 'Register session hosts to a host pool'. Add session hosts to a host pool - Azure Virtual Desktop | Microsoft Learn
solution: Install Remote Desktop Session Host role on the OS.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#192560

   +0x000 FamilyName               :  "Microsoft.AAD.BrokerPlugin_cw5n1h2txyewy"
   +0x084 UserSid                  : _RM_SID
   +0x0c8 SessionId                : 0x23 (0n35)
 The Host Activity Manager (HAM) manages packaged (UWP, Centennial, Win32-a-la-carte) applications execution. It supports executing app activities, running in app host processes, managing the resource usage of those hosts including their execution state (running, suspended, terminated, etc.). HAM had requested to change the application status during the very early stage of the login process. Before this thread got into the waiting state, it would queue a work item to change the app state. The worker thread that picks up this work item may have exited because we cannot see such a thread in the DcomLaunch process. Hence, it is not clear what happened to the worker thread.  To debug this further, we need more details. Please follow these steps before reproducing the issue to collect logs: Start the TTD of DcomLaunch. Start the TTD of Explorer. If this is difficult to capture for some reason, then we can collect AdexTrace (step 3). Start AdexTrace (Details are provided in the more information section). Please run the PowerShell script as follows: adextrace.ps1 -start -profiles adexproviders.wprp. Reproduce the issue. Stop the TTD of DcomLaunch and Explorer. Stop AdexTrace by running: adextrace.ps1 -stop From the affected session, please collect the following data:Process dump of sihost.exe of the affected session. Process dump of explorer.exe of the affected session.    However, if we do not know when the issue may occur, we can target one of the AVD machines and enable circular tracing.
solution: We are tracking this issue in this Bug record (Please do not share this Bug details with Customer): 49315254 Application event log has the following errors logged (this is consistent on all environments where this issue was observed):   Log Name: ApplicationSource: Microsoft-Windows-AppModel-StateDate: 06-09-2024 04:53:20Event ID: 10Task Category: NoneLevel: ErrorKeywords:User: S-1-5-21-xxxxxxxxx-xxxxxxxxx-xxxxxxxxx-179916Computer: xxxxxxxxxDescription:Failure to load the application settings for package Microsoft.AAD.BrokerPlugin_cw5n1h2txyewy. Error Code: -2147024893 //The system cannot find the path specified.Log Name: ApplicationSource: Microsoft-Windows-AppModel-StateDate: 06-09-2024 04:53:20Event ID: 23Task Category: NoneLevel: WarningKeywords:User: S-1-5-21-xxxxxxxxx-xxxxxxxxx-xxxxxxxxx-179916Computer: xxxxxxxxxDescription:Triggered repair of state locations because operation Settings Initialize against package Microsoft.AAD.BrokerPlugin_cw5n1h2txyewy hit error -2147009096.//Loading the state store failed.  Looks like this event is related to the black screen issue the users are experiencing. We are investigating this further.    Suggestions on troubleshooting: Application state Store for Microsoft.AAD.BrokerPlugin will be located in the following location: C:\users\AppData\Local\Packages\Microsoft.AAD.BrokerPlugin_cw5n1h2txyewy\Settings\settings.dat State Registry hive would be loaded in the following Registry location: HKCU\<User SID>\Local Settings\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModel\SystemAppData\Microsoft.AAD.BrokerPlugin_cw5n1h2txyewy\ Please find out if there are any open handles to this Registry / hive file which is blocking us from loading the file.

symptom:
MMR video redirection has failed while playing few Udemy videos in AVD
  
  
     
  
  
    
      
    
  
  
    MMR gives smooth video playback while watching videos in a browser in Azure Virtual Desktop. Multimedia redirection redirects the media content from Azure Virtual Desktop to your local machine for faster processing and rendering.
  
  
    
    We need to check below prerequisites and basic troubleshooting by following below TSG.
  
  
    
      
      
      Troubleshooting - Overview (azure.com)
      
    
  
  
    
    I have captured client and server trace and engaged PG over the ICM
    .
rootCause: MMR doesnt support DRM (digital rights managed) content
solution:
U
    demy uses DRM content on some of their videos. MMR however does not currently support DRM content playback. Some of the Udemy videos are not DRM protected while some are and MMR today should work fine with non-DRM Udemy videos.
  
  
    Our DEV team has fine-tuned this feature as a fallback path task is to support video playback without redirection when user is watching a DRM content. This is an enhancement to allow users not having need to turning MMR off on non-supported DRM content. 
    They
     currently made a public extension release (Version 1.0.2307.25001) to support fallback path on DRM sites.
  
  
    However, there's a problem with Udemy sites structure specifically that would not let MMR component know which video is DRM and which is not. Hence, MMR fallback path could not possibly engage during time when DRM content is played. MMR component would only able to know when the video is already unable to play. Hence, current suggestion is to turn off the MMR plugin during Udemy playback. 
  
  
    You can refer below work around to disable MMR for required websites.
  
  
    We have suggested customer to perform below work around to play Udemy videos without any error. We have added 
    *://*.udemy.com
     under runtime blocked hosts via below GPO.
  
  
    Path :
     Computer Configuration > Administrative Templates > Microsoft Edge > Extensions. Open the policy setting Configure extension management settings and set it to Enabled. In the field for Configure extension management settings, enter the following:
    
      
    { "joeclbldhdmoijbaagobkhlpfjglcihd": { "installation_mode": "force_installed", "runtime_allowed_hosts": [ "*://*.youtube.com" ], 
    "runtime_blocked_hosts": [ "*://*" ],
     "update_url": 
    
      
      
      https://edge.microsoft.com/extensionwebstorebase/v1/crx
      
    
     } }
