symptom:
Sxs not getting auto installed post registering the machine to hostpool this makes the machine not showing up on the hostpool -- session host blade   Application event log shows following error. Unable to retrieve DefaultAgent from registry: System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object  Collected MSI logs with procmon while installing the agents.   Identified that the loading module for RDagentbootloader was from Syswow64 which is for 32bit.  As per the internal bug if the RDagent bootloader uses 32bit module it will fail to query the registry to set the values  Bug 44929841
[AVD] RDAgentBootLoader.exe cannot open registry _bootLoaderRegPath if it is
running as 32-bit process
rootCause: The following registry was causing the issue.  HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\.NETFramework ---- Enable64bit was set to 0   This forces RDagentbootloader to load 32bit module and that was not allowing the agentbootloader to query the registry and fails with "Unable to retrieve default agent from registry"
solution:
On
     the affected machine navigate to following registry path. 
 HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\.NETFramework 
 Change
     Enable64bit value from 0 to 1.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#208009

symptom: Windows app is crashing when starting up on specific machine. The user experience is this: customer click on the Windows App (Windows365.exe) Nothing happens. =>The app does not launch because it crashed.  Crash is visible in the application logs : Error	1/21/2026 5:56:16 AM	Application Error	1000	Application Crashing Events Faulting application name: Windows365.exe, version: 2.0.916.0, time stamp: 0x69541063Faulting module name: ucrtbase.dll, version: 10.0.26100.7623, time stamp: 0x53a0792e Exception code: 0xc0000409 Fault offset: 0x00000000000a4ace Faulting process id: 0x1694 Faulting application start time: 0x1DC8ADDB596E097 Faulting application path: C:\Program Files\WindowsApps\MicrosoftCorporationII.Windows365_2.0.916.0_x64__8wekyb3d8bbwe\Windows365.exe Faulting module path: C:\WINDOWS\System32\ucrtbase.dll Report Id: 192f0a43-f7da-4c59-b044-6befa3a422ed Faulting package full name: MicrosoftCorporationII.Windows365_2.0.916.0_x64__8wekyb3d8bbwe Faulting package-relative application ID: Windows365
rootCause: Taking a dump of the Windows App shows an issue to open the Health Check log on startup of the Windows App (see More Information section).  The cause has 2 factors : customer has changed the TEMP and TMP user environement variables to a different location than the default The user does not have permissions on the new location.
solution: a) Ensure to put back the correct settings for the user environment variable TEMP and TMP. Here are the correct settings:     b) OR another possibility is to have the user have access to the new location if you set a new location for TEMP or TMP in User Environement Variables.

symptom:
Customers are getting protocol error
 (code: 0x112F) due to which the remote session will be disconnected. And error
 message displayed is Please try connecting to the remote computer again.
rootCause:
This is happening only when customer is
 using Nvidia GPU enabled VMs and enable multi-monitor features from RDP
 properties. This is an AVC hardware issue. AVC HW
 is enabled in the session hosts which has Nvidia GPU enabled for which
 multi-monitor is not working and throwing the error protocol error (code:
 0x112f) due to which the remote session will be disconnected.
solution:
We need to Disable the AVC HW in the
 session hosts which has Nvidia GPU enabled for things to work in multi-monitor
 environments as well. 
 Mitigation steps to disable is: 
 Launch "Group Policy Editor"
 (gpedit.msc) in the affected machine (Local group policy)/ Or you can
 implement the same via GPO in case of multiple session hosts got affected. Drill down to the policies: Computer
 Configuration> Administrative Templates> Windows Components> Remote
 Desktop Services> Remote Desktop Session Host> Remote Session
 Environment Disable the following policy:
 Configure H.264/AVC hardware encoding for Remote Desktop connections.   
 
 
 This needs to be done in all the session
 hosts which has Nvidia GPU enabled if done locally for testing. If successful,
 ask to implement the same through GPO.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#208141

symptom: Abstract  An issue was observed in which copy-and-paste functionality fails when using the Windows App (Web) in full-screen mode on macOS with the Chrome browser to access Azure Virtual Desktop (AVD).While content copied on the local macOS client cannot be pasted into the AVD session in full-screen mode, the issue does not occur in windowed mode, and paste functionality continues to work after returning to full-screen once a paste has been performed. This behavior is unintended and was confirmed by the development team. Workaround is to Disabe [Keyboard shortcuts] setting this option at Windows App version  2.0.03267.1373. Further investigation is ongoing to determine whether the issue can be fully resolved with keyboard shortcuts enabled. Additional details are tracked in the related ICM incident.  Symptoms **Steps to Reproduce:** 1.  On macOS, open Chrome browser and connect to Windows App  2.  Connect to a desktop in the AVD environment. 3.  Switch to full-screen mode. 4.  Copy content on macOS (the client machine). 5.  Attempt to paste into the AVD environment in full-screen mode (step 3). 6.  Confirm that paste does not work.              **Shortcut key used:**  (Command) + V.       Right-click paste also reproduces the issue.            The following behavior was observed: *   **Full Screen:** Right-click paste and shortcut paste are not possible. *   **Window Mode:** Right-click paste (_) and shortcut paste are possible._       _(_) Until the shortcut paste is performed, right-click paste cannot be executed.
solution:
In Windows App (Web) version 2.0.03267.1373, disabling the Keyboard shortcuts option in the settings enables copyandpaste functionality and serves as an effective workaround for this issue. Red marked setting in attached image is it. Whether this issue can be resolved while keeping Keyboard shortcuts enabled is currently under investigation by the development team as of January 26, 2026.
For further details, please refer to the attached ICM.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#208174

symptom: A user tries to sign in on Windows App. The following error message appears."No Network Connection"Please check your network settings and try again.  In Windows App Log, we can see the following trace, which indicates that some required endpoints may not be reachable. Log Folder: \%TEMP%\DiagOutputDir\Windows365\LogsIn this example, it's the Entra ID-related URLs.[2025-10-14 18:20:03.550] [async_logger] [error] [MSAL:0001]    ERROR   ErrorInternalImpl:134   Created an error: 4qm49, StatusInternal::NoNetwork, InternalEvent::None, Error Code -2146697211, Context '' [2025-10-14 18:20:03.551] [async_logger] [error] [OneAuth:0001:Error:4qm49:0df86f49] (Code:2603) The network is offline.    Note: This issue can be reproduced 100 %. If the issue is intermittent, it might be in a different scenario.  OneAuth Error Code 2603  Learnings From ICMs - Overview
rootCause: The client network does not allow all required URLs. Required FQDNs and endpoints for Azure Virtual Desktop - Azure Virtual Desktop | Microsoft Learn  This is usually caused by customers proxy setting (either wininet/winhttp) so make sure the customer's network is configured to allow all required URLs.
solution: Allow all required URLs as described in the following document. Required FQDNs and endpoints for Azure Virtual Desktop - Azure Virtual Desktop | Microsoft Learn  Make sure all required URLs described in the "important" section including " Microsoft Entra ID, Office 365, custom DNS providers, or time services" are all allowed in the client network.

symptom: Non persistent VDI environment accessing internet based remote management consoles which has a virtual keyboard and the virtual keyboard works well on the Windows 10 AVD non persistent VDI but virtual keyboard doesn't work on the Windows 11 AVD VDI host.    Customer was using RDP method for connecting to AVD VDI.
rootCause: Actual cause was not determined but appears to be problem with using RDP method for connection having problem recognizing keystrokes as opposed to a cloud-based method for connection to AVD desktop which was then connecting to web-based 3rd party management consoles. Hypothesis is it just needed a cloud-based method for connecting to AVD in order for the keystrokes to be recognized with an internet-based management console.
solution: Utilized Windows App to connect to AVD VDI which resolved recognizing keystrokes with internet-based management type consoles.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#208871

symptom: The session hosts appear in a 'Shutdown' state under hostpool blade, even though the virtual machine is running:  When users attempt to restart the VM or perform a stop/start operation, the session host transitions to a 'Needs Assistance' state and reports that the required URLs are not reachable.
rootCause: After reviewing the VNET configuration, it was identified that VNET encryption was enabled, which also has an ExpressRoute gateway attached.  This combination is a known limitation and is not supported due to its instability and it may cause connectivity issues.  What is Azure Virtual Network encryption? - Azure Virtual Network | Microsoft Learn
solution: Once VNet encryption was disabled, the session host was able to successfully connect to all required endpoints without reporting any further issues.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#209022

symptom: When you connect through the Windows App web client to an AVD session host, Windows 365 Cloud PC, or Dev Box, and launch PowerShell in the legacy Windows Console Host, you can only input the Enter key, while all other character inputs (regardless of language or type) are ignored. This occurs regardless of whether PowerShell is launched with standard or administrator permissions. The issue occurs only in these scenarios: Connection is established through the Windows App web client. PowerShell is launched in the Windows Console Host.  The issue doesn't occur when: Connecting via the Windows App (desktop version). Using cmd.exe (Command Prompt). Launching PowerShell in Windows Terminal.  This means the issue can be easily avoided by using Windows Terminal instead of Windows Console Host, or by using the Windows App instead of the web client. Windows Console Host (issue occurs)Windows Terminal (issue doesn't occur)
rootCause: According to the product groups formal statement, this behavior is caused by a limitation of the Windows App web client and is deemed by design. Because the issue can be avoided by connecting through the desktop version of Windows App or by using Windows Terminal, related IcMs were resolved with this explanation.  If the customer has sufficient BI by which they strongly require to use PowerShell launched via the legacy Windows Console Host specifically through the web client, you are advised to submit a DesignChange Request (DCR).DCR or Feature Request - Overview
solution: Resolution 1: Connect using the Windows App desktop version Instead of connecting through the Windows App web client, use the Windows App desktop version. Resolution 2: Use Windows Terminal to run PowerShell Launch PowerShell in Windows Terminal rather than in the Windows Console Host. On Windows 11, new PowerShell prompt are launched in Windows Terminal for standard users and in Windows Console Host for users with administrative privileged. You can configure the default terminal application for both instances by going to Settings > System > Advanced settings.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#209057

rdpnanoTransport!Microsoft::Basix::Dct::ICECore::ReceiveForLinks+0x229  00007ffd5a436361 rdpnanoTransport!Microsoft::Basix::Dct::ICECore::CandidateBase::HandleDataReceived+0x17e5  00007ffd5a436149 rdpnanoTransport!Microsoft::Basix::Dct::ICECore::CandidateBase::HandleDataReceived+0x15cd  00007ffd5a44352c rdpnanoTransport!Microsoft::Basix::Dct::ICECore::CandidateBase::OnDataReceived+0xcc  00007ffd5a3e1082 rdpnanoTransport!Microsoft::Basix::Dct::ChannelThreadQueue::ThreadedProcess+0x342  00007ffd5a51fcda rdpnanoTransport!Microsoft::Basix::Pattern::IThreadedObject::ThreadProcedure+0x5ba
rootCause: This issue happens due to a code defect in the UDP transport handler of the Windows App. The issue is currently being investigated in the following bug:  Bug 60104911 Memory Leak in rdpnanoTransport!Microsoft::Basix::Instrumentation::TraceManager::TraceMessage
solution: The issue can be temporarily mitigated by disabling tracing, which is done by adding the following value to the registry:  DisableEHUpload_<ClientVersion>  *The ClientVersion can be obtained in the "About" section of the Windows App.  For example:  reg.exe add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Terminal Server Client\Default" /v DisableEHUpload_1.2.6980.0 /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f However, it only lasts until the app is updated to the next version.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#209409

symptom: AVD management portal shows 'Failed to create registration key' error
rootCause: Registration token creation failed, possibly due to too long expiry time
solution: Retry creating registration key with shorter expiry time (between 1 hour and 1 month)

symptom: Cannot add user assignments to application group - Azure portal shows 'Session Ending' or 'Experiencing Authentication Issues' warning
rootCause: Conditional Access policy 'Microsoft Office 365 Data Storage Terms of Use' blocks token acquisition for Microsoft Graph; user hasn't accepted Terms of Use via SharePoint
solution: Admin must first sign in to SharePoint and accept Terms of Use before accessing Azure portal

symptom: User-assigned managed identity assigned to host pool does not appear in managed identity's associated resources view
rootCause: Bug in host pool API - accepts identity ID even if missing leading '/' in the resource path
solution: Double-check the user-assigned managed identity ID format, ensure it starts with '/' (e.g., '/subscriptions/...')

symptom: RDAgentBootLoader or Remote Desktop Agent Loader stopped running on session host; session host shows Unavailable
rootCause: Boot loader unable to install agent properly; agent service not running
solution: Start Remote Desktop Agent Loader service via Services console. If it stops after restart, check for INVALID_REGISTRATION_TOKEN or EXPIRED_MACHINE_TOKEN errors

symptom: Event ID 3277 with INVALID_REGISTRATION_TOKEN or EXPIRED_MACHINE_TOKEN in Application event log; session host cannot register
rootCause: Registration key is expired or invalid
solution: Generate new registration key, update registry (HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\RDInfraAgent - set IsRegistered=0, set new RegistrationToken), restart RDAgentBootLoader service

symptom: Event ID 3277 with INVALID_FORM - agent cannot connect to broker
rootCause: Firewall or DNS settings blocking broker endpoints (BrokerResourceIdURI and BrokerResourceIdURIGlobal)
solution: Verify connectivity to broker endpoints by checking registry keys under HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\RDInfraAgent and testing {BrokerURI}/api/health. Unblock required URLs per Safe URL List. Also check TLS 1.2 cipher compatibility

symptom: Event ID 3703 - 'RD Gateway Url is not accessible'; agent unable to reach gateway URLs
rootCause: Firewall or proxy blocking required AVD gateway URLs
solution: Unblock required URLs from Safe URL List. Run Required URL Check tool. For Azure Firewall, configure per AVD deployment protection guide

symptom: Event ID 3019 - agent can't reach web socket transport URLs
rootCause: Network blocking web socket transport URLs required by AVD
solution: Unblock URLs from Safe URL List. Check network trace logs to identify where AVD service is blocked. Work with networking team on firewall/proxy/DNS settings

symptom: Event ID 3277 with InstallationHealthCheckFailedException - stack listener not working
rootCause: Terminal server toggled registry key for stack listener, disabling it
solution: Check if stack listener is working; if not, manually uninstall and reinstall the stack component

symptom: Event ID 3277 with ENDPOINT_NOT_FOUND - broker can't find endpoint to connect
rootCause: No active session host VMs in host pool, or all exceeded max session limit, or agent service not running
solution: Verify VM is powered on and not removed from host pool; check max session limit not exceeded; ensure agent service running and stack listener working; verify valid registration token

symptom: Event ID 3277 with InstallMsiException - agent installer fails
rootCause: Another MSI installer already running, or Group Policy blocking msiexec.exe (Windows Installer > Turn off Windows Installer = Enabled)
solution: Wait for other installer to finish, or check RSOP for 'Turn off Windows Installer' policy under Computer Config > Admin Templates > Windows Components > Windows Installer and work with AD team to allow msiexec.exe

symptom: Event ID 3389 with MissingMethodException during agent update - agent reverts to earlier version
rootCause: .NET Framework version installed on VM is lower than 4.7.2
solution: Upgrade .NET Framework to version 4.7.2 or later

symptom: Session host VMs stuck in Upgrading or Unavailable state
rootCause: Agent or side-by-side stack didn't install successfully
solution: Reinstall SxS network stack: stop RDAgentBootLoader, uninstall SxS Network Stack, reinstall latest from C:\Program Files\Microsoft RDInfra\SxSStack*.msi, restart VM, verify qwinsta shows rdp-tcp and rdp-sxs in Listen state

symptom: NAME_ALREADY_REGISTERED error when registering session host
rootCause: Session host VM name is a duplicate of an existing registration
solution: Remove session host from host pool, create new VM with unique name, re-register

symptom: RDAgentBootLoader startup timeout at OS boot - session host shows Needs Assistance or Unavailable; Event 7000/7011 in Service Control Manager
rootCause: Boot loader service doesn't start fast enough during OS boot due to boot pressure (high IOPS, heavy GPO, AV scans)
solution: Set RDAgentBootLoader to Automatic (Delayed Start); increase ServicesPipeTimeout registry to 60000ms; verify boot performance and IOPS; run WVDAgentUrlTool.exe to validate endpoints

symptom: Stack listener not working on Windows 10 2004 - rdp-tcp/rdp-sxs not in Listen state
rootCause: Registry keys fReverseConnectMode or fEnableWinStation not set to 1 under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server\WinStations
solution: Set fReverseConnectMode=1 and fEnableWinStation=1 in registry for matching stack version folder, restart VM, verify SessionDirectoryListener matches rdp-sxs version

symptom: DownloadMsiException - agent fails to download/install
rootCause: Insufficient disk space on session host VM for RDAgent installation
solution: Free up disk space by deleting unused files or increase storage capacity of session host VM

symptom: DisableRegistryTools registry key prevents agent from installing SxS stack - installMSIException; session hosts stuck Unavailable
rootCause: DisableRegistryTools=1 set in HKU\DEFAULT, HKU\S-1-5-18, or HKCU policies preventing registry operations
solution: Remove DisableRegistryTools key from all three locations, uninstall affected SxS stack, remove its registry keys, restart VM, let agent auto-install SxS stack

symptom: AD DS group membership for VM not working for Azure Files authentication
rootCause: VM needs to be restarted to activate new AD DS group membership
solution: Restart the VM after adding it to the AD DS group

symptom: Cannot add storage account to AD DS domain
rootCause: Incorrect OU syntax or ignored warning messages during PowerShell account creation
solution: Delete domain account representing storage account, recreate with correct OU syntax: DC=ouname,DC=domainprefix,DC=topleveldomain. Wait 30min for Entra ID sync

symptom: New-AzRoleAssignment fails with 'does not map to an AD object ID'
rootCause: User specified in -SignInName not found in Microsoft Entra ID; user not synced, or is B2C/B2B user
solution: Ensure user is synced to Entra ID, not B2C/B2B, and AVD environment is tied to correct Entra ID tenant

symptom: New-AzWvdHostPool fails with 'location is not available for resource type'
rootCause: AVD service metadata location not available in selected region (e.g. southeastasia)
solution: Use one of the supported regions listed in the error message for host pool creation

symptom: New-AzWvdApplicationGroup fails with 'must be in same location as associated HostPool'
rootCause: Application group being created in different location than the host pool
solution: Retrieve host pool location and create application group in the same location. All associated objects (host pool, app group, workspace) must share the same metadata location

symptom: Entra joined VM: 'Your account is configured to prevent you from using this device'
rootCause: User account not assigned Virtual Machine User Login RBAC role on the VM
solution: Assign Virtual Machine User Login role to user on the VM or resource group

symptom: Entra joined VM: 'The user name or password is incorrect' despite correct credentials
rootCause: Per-user MFA enabled/enforced (not supported for Entra joined VMs), or VM User Login role not assigned, or Conditional Access requiring MFA for Azure Windows VM Sign-In app
solution: Disable per-user MFA; use Conditional Access instead with exclusion for Azure Windows VM Sign-In. Assign VM User Login role. Check AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs with AppId 372140e0-b3b7-4226-8ef9-d57986796201

symptom: Entra joined VM: 'The logon attempt failed' on Windows Security credential prompt
rootCause: Device not Entra joined/hybrid joined to same tenant, or PKU2U protocol not enabled on local PC and/or session host
solution: Ensure device is Entra joined/hybrid joined to same tenant. Enable PKU2U protocol on both local PC and session host. Disable per-user MFA

symptom: Entra joined VM: 'A specified logon session does not exist. It may already have been terminated'
rootCause: Kerberos server object not properly created/configured for SSO
solution: Verify Kerberos server object is properly created and configured per SSO configuration guide

symptom: Session host fails UrlsAccessibleCheck health check - stuck in Needs Assistance state
rootCause: Firewall blocking outbound traffic to required URLs, or local hosts file blocking required websites
solution: Add firewall rule allowing outbound TCP 80/443 to required URLs. Check Hosts file (path in registry HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters\DataBasePath) for blocked entries

symptom: Session host fails MetaDataServiceCheck health check - can't access IMDS endpoint
rootCause: Networking/firewall/proxy blocking IMDS endpoint 169.254.169.254
solution: Unblock IP 169.254.169.254; ensure HTTP clients bypass web proxy for IMDS; add proxy bypass: netsh winhttp set proxy bypass-list='169.254.169.254'

symptom: Session host fails MonitoringAgentCheck - Geneva Agent not functioning
rootCause: Remote Desktop Services Infrastructure Geneva Agent not installed, has multiple versions, or GenevaTask scheduled task not running
solution: Verify Geneva Agent installed (check Apps & Features); uninstall older versions keeping only latest; check C:\Program Files\Microsoft RDInfra\GenevaInstall.txt for errors; verify GenevaTask_<version> scheduled task is enabled and running; reinstall from .msi if needed

symptom: Custom image template build fails with Resource group must be empty error
rootCause: Specified resource group for Azure Image Builder contains existing resources
solution: Delete all resources in the resource group or specify a new empty one

symptom: Custom image template fails with Resource URI is unavailable error
rootCause: Script URI is not publicly accessible or URL is incorrect
solution: Verify script URI is publicly available

symptom: Custom image template fails with Hyper-V generation mismatch between source image and Compute Gallery definition
rootCause: Source image Gen1/Gen2 does not match Compute Gallery VM image definition generation
solution: Ensure source image generation matches VM image definition generation

symptom: Custom image template fails with PrivateLinkService Network Policy not disabled for subnet
rootCause: Private service policy enabled on subnet used by Azure Image Builder
solution: Disable private service policy on the subnet

symptom: Custom image template fails with ContainerInstance authorization error
rootCause: Microsoft.ContainerInstance resource provider not registered
solution: Register Microsoft.ContainerInstance resource provider on the subscription

symptom: WebAuthn redirection not working - no Windows Hello or security key option in AVD session
rootCause: FIDO2 method not enabled in Entra ID, or user signed in single-factor, or unsupported OS
solution: Enable FIDO2 in Entra ID; use Sign in with Windows Hello option; verify supported OS

symptom: Clipboard redirection fails or window cannot be resized after SxS Network Stack update 1.0.2501.05600+
rootCause: rdpclipcdv.exe and rdpinputcdv.exe blocked by AppLocker/SRP/endpoint protection
solution: Allow-list rdpclipcdv.exe and rdpinputcdv.exe from C:\Program Files\Microsoft RDInfra subfolders

symptom: Azure portal shows Create a free account when accessing AVD
rootCause: No active subscriptions or account lacks view permissions
solution: Sign in with Contributor-level access account

symptom: Host pool deployment fails with Exceeding quota limit
rootCause: VM core quota exceeded for SKU in subscription/region
solution: Create host pool with fewer VMs or submit quota increase request

symptom: User assignments not visible after moving subscription between Entra tenants
rootCause: Old assignments tied to previous tenant
solution: Reassign users to application groups in new tenant

symptom: Azure region not visible when selecting AVD service object location
rootCause: Microsoft.DesktopVirtualization resource provider needs re-registration
solution: Re-register Microsoft.DesktopVirtualization resource provider

symptom: Host pool deployment fails with joindomain error
rootCause: Incorrect credentials, domain not resolvable, or VNet DNS set to Default
solution: Verify credentials; peer VNets; set DNS to Custom pointing to DC IPs

symptom: Deployment fails Unauthorized - scale operation not allowed for subscription in region
rootCause: Subscription type (MSDN/Free/Education) lacks VM features in region
solution: Change subscription type or deploy to different region

symptom: DSC extension fails to download Configuration.zip - catalogartifact.azureedge.net not resolved
rootCause: Firewall/NSG/static route blocking download
solution: Remove blocking network rules or download zip manually

symptom: Cannot delete session host from host pool after VM deleted
rootCause: Session host record must be deleted before VM
solution: Drain mode then sign out users then delete session host then delete VM

symptom: MMR extension shows not loaded in first browser tab
rootCause: Known initialization issue in first tab
solution: Open a second browser tab

symptom: Multimedia redirection does not work on session host
rootCause: cmd.exe is blocked; MMR requires it
solution: Unblock cmd.exe on session hosts

symptom: MMR extension version disappears, fails to load on supported sites
rootCause: Missing latest Visual C++ Redistributable
solution: Install latest Visual C++ Redistributable

symptom: ShortpathTransportNetworkDrop error in Log Analytics - UDP connection drops
rootCause: UDP has no RST mechanism; loss detected only by timeout
solution: Use avdnettest.exe to verify STUN/TURN connectivity and NAT type

symptom: ConnectionBrokenMissedHeartbeatThresholdExceeded - RDP timeout before UDP timeout
rootCause: RDP heartbeat timeout misconfiguration
solution: Review RDP heartbeat timeout settings

symptom: User connects but no feed/icons displayed in AVD
rootCause: Not assigned to app groups, cached creds, or distribution group instead of security group
solution: Check Get-AzRoleAssignment; clear cache; use security groups

symptom: User loses feed after subscription moved between Entra tenants
rootCause: Assignments tied to old tenant or CSP transfer
solution: Reassign users; re-register DesktopVirtualization RP for CSP

symptom: Conditional Access error prevents AVD access
rootCause: CA policy blocks AVD/W365 apps or Windows Cloud Login app for SSO
solution: Update CA policies to allow AVD and W365 apps; allow Windows Cloud Login for SSO

symptom: Session host config fails during host pool creation - VmSkuNotAvailableInRegion etc
rootCause: Incompatible VM SKU/region/zone/disk/image/VNet combination
solution: Use Get-AzComputeResourceSku; use PowerShell cmdlet or recreate host pool

symptom: Session host update fails and rollback fails - host unavailable
rootCause: Image/SKU mismatch, agent failure, resource locks, or resource modifications during update
solution: Check errors; retry or cancel; remove DSC extension from image; check activity logs

symptom: Domain join fails with Timeout waiting for user input
rootCause: Domain join account has MFA enabled
solution: Remove MFA or use service account

symptom: Computer account reuse blocked during domain join - KB5020276 hardening
rootCause: Oct 2022+ Windows updates enforce join hardening; insufficient permissions
solution: Use original creator account, Domain Admin, or apply computer account re-use GPO

symptom: AVD Agent and Boot Loader not installed; ScriptLog.log missing
rootCause: PowerShell DSC failed - wrong credentials or insufficient permissions
solution: Manually install via PowerShell; verify admin access and no MFA

symptom: O_REVERSE_CONNECT_STACK_FAILURE error - SxS stack not installed
rootCause: Side-by-side stack not installed on session host VM
solution: RDP as local admin and reinstall SxS stack via host pool registration

symptom: Remote Desktop licensing mode not configured - RDS will stop working in X days
rootCause: Missing Windows updates or GPO Set the Remote Desktop licensing mode is Enabled
solution: Disable the licensing mode GPO; install KB4516077 for 1809 or redeploy with latest image

symptom: Could not connect to remote PC because of a security error
rootCause: Allow log on through Remote Desktop Services policy misconfigured
solution: Fix the security policy to include appropriate user groups

symptom: Golden image causes agent issues - agent pre-installed in image
rootCause: Golden image contains pre-installed AVD agent
solution: Never include AVD agent in golden images; install after deployment

symptom: Ephemeral OS disk deployment fails on certain VM sizes
rootCause: VM size does not have enough local cache or temporary disk space to accommodate the OS image
solution: Verify VM size supports EOSD using Azure CLI EphemeralOSDiskSupported check; for AVD use Temp Disk placement (NVMe not supported in public preview)

symptom: OS-level changes lost after stopping/deallocating/reimaging VM with ephemeral OS disk
rootCause: Ephemeral OS disks are stateless; stop/deallocate operations wipe OS state by design
solution: Avoid stop/deallocate operations; design stateless workloads; persist user profiles externally via FSLogix or Azure Files; use delete/recreate logic for autoscale instead of deallocate

symptom: Snapshots, backups, or Azure Disk Encryption not available on ephemeral OS disk
rootCause: Ephemeral OS disks do not support persistent features (snapshots, backups, encryption) by design
solution: Use managed OS disks instead of EOSD if persistent features like backups, snapshots, or encryption are required

symptom: Ephemeral OS disk deployment fails with incorrect DiffDiskPlacement (e.g. NVMe selected)
rootCause: Selecting unsupported placement option such as NVMe causes deployment issues during public preview
solution: For AVD session hosts, select Temp Disk placement during deployment; avoid NVMe placement during public preview

symptom: Ephemeral OS disk deployment fails in certain regions due to SKU rollout gaps
rootCause: Incomplete rollout of supported SKUs or backend bugs cause regional deployment issues
solution: Monitor Azure announcements for SKU availability updates; deploy to another region or select a different SKU as workaround

symptom: Windows key remains held in local session after pressing Ctrl+Win+L for live captions in AVD remote session and returning to local desktop
rootCause: Known Windows limitation: when using Win key, certain key combinations are locked to prevent overriding Win+L; releasing Win key last after Ctrl+Win+L causes Win key to stay held in local session
solution: Release L or Win key before the Ctrl key when pressing Ctrl+Win+L; alternatively start live captions from the Start menu instead of keyboard shortcut

symptom: After Azure Site Recovery (ASR) replication/failover, AVD VM is billed at higher rate showing 'Dsv5 Series Windows' instead of 'Dsv5 Series' in cost report
rootCause: ASR replication does not preserve the 'licenseType: Windows_Client' parameter on the VM object. Without this parameter, Azure charges the Windows license fee on top of compute cost
solution: After ASR failover, manually set licenseType on the replicated VM: Set-AzVM -ResourceGroupName <rg> -Name <vm> -LicenseType Windows_Client. For AVD VMs, the deployment template normally includes this parameter automatically
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Common Issues/Licensing.md

symptom: When using MSIX app attach in AVD, launching an application (e.g. PuTTY) incorrectly launches a different application from the same MSIX package (e.g. PuTTYGen)
rootCause: Bug in AVD MSIX app attach where the system launches the last application declared in the appxmanifest rather than the intended one, affecting multi-entry MSIX packages
solution: PG has a fix in the pipeline. As workaround, modify the appxmanifest to reorder application entries so the desired app is listed last, or split multi-entry apps into separate MSIX packages
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Common Issues/MSIX App multi-entry app issue.md

symptom: MSIX remote app launches Windows Explorer instead of the intended application after user disconnects from a session desktop and relaunches the MSIX app. Sequence: launch MSIX app -> close (disconnect) -> launch session desktop -> sign out (session stays Disconnected) -> relaunch MSIX app -> Explorer opens instead
rootCause: Bug in AVD MSIX app attach where a disconnected session state causes the MSIX remote app process to incorrectly launch explorer.exe instead of the registered application executable
solution: Bug tracked (OS Bug 34349845). Workaround: ensure the user fully signs out from the session desktop (not just disconnect) before relaunching the MSIX remote app. Alternatively, log off all disconnected sessions before launching MSIX apps
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Common Issues/MSIX App starts explorer issue.md

symptom: Windows 10 EVD multi-session images (19h2-evd, 20h2-evd) fail to activate in Azure Mooncake with Windows cannot activate error. O365 variants (19h2-evd-o365pp, 20h2-evd-o365) are not affected
rootCause: Platform bug on specific AVD multi-session images in Mooncake (ICM 255528022). The non-O365 EVD images lacked proper KMS activation configuration for Mooncake environment
solution: Fixed per ICM 255528022. Workaround: run slmgr.vbs /ipk NPPR9-FWDCX-D2C8J-H872K-2YT43 then slmgr.vbs /ato to manually activate. Or use O365 image variants which activate correctly
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Common Issues/Windows 10 activation issue.md

symptom: FSLogix profile roaming does not work with Azure AD-joined VMs in pooled host pool scenarios. Users cannot use roaming profiles with AADJ pooled desktops
rootCause: Azure Files does not support Azure AD as a Kerberos realm. FSLogix requires Kerberos authentication to access Azure Files shares for profile containers, which is unavailable with pure Azure AD-joined VMs
solution: Use AADJ VMs only for: (1) personal desktops with local user profiles, (2) pooled desktops as jump boxes without saved data, (3) apps that save data online/remote DB. For pooled desktops requiring profile roaming, use hybrid AD-joined or AD DS-joined VMs
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Feature Verification/Azure AD-joined VMs.md

symptom: Workgroup (non-AAD-joined) Windows client fails to RDP connect to Azure AD-joined AVD session host with your credentials did not work error, when RdsAADAuth is enabled in host pool but disabled on the VM
rootCause: CredSSP fallback is not supported from workgroup/non-joined clients to AADJ VMs. When RdsAADAuth is disabled on the VM, the connection attempts CredSSP which fails for non-domain-joined source machines
solution: Add targetisaadjoined:i:1 as custom RDP property to the host pool to enable RDSTLS protocol fallback for non-joined clients. Alternatively, enable enablerdsaadauth:i:1 on both host pool and VM for full RDS AAD Auth SSO experience
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Feature Verification/Azure AD-joined VMs/AVD Authentication.md

symptom: Cannot enroll AVD session hosts to Microsoft Intune in Azure Mooncake (China). Intune management option not available for AVD VMs in Mooncake
rootCause: Microsoft Intune management for AVD session hosts is only supported in Azure Public and Azure Government clouds. Azure China (Mooncake) is not a supported cloud for Intune-managed AVD session hosts
solution: No officially supported method in Mooncake. Unsupported workaround: manually disconnect session host from Azure AD, then re-join with Intune auto-enrollment enabled (tested but not officially supported by PG). Manual enrollment of personal pool hosts to Intune is not supported at all
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Feature Verification/Azure AD-joined VMs/With Intune.md

symptom: MSIX app attach fails with Azure AD DS (AADDS) environment. Cannot add MSIX image to host pool when storage account is joined to AADDS
rootCause: MSIX app attach does not support Azure AD DS because AADDS computer objects are not synchronized to Azure AD, making it impossible to provide the required RBAC permissions for Azure Files
solution: Use AD DS (on-premises Active Directory) instead of Azure AD DS for MSIX app attach. Join storage account to AD DS, sync computer objects to AAD, then assign RBAC and NTFS permissions
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Feature Verification/MSIX app attach/[Lab]MSIX+AADDS.md

symptom: AVD session host domain join fails with error code 1355 during provisioning. Error: 'Failed to join domain, option NetSetupJoinDomain, NetSetupAcctCreate. Error code 1355'
rootCause: Error 1355 (ERROR_NO_SUCH_DOMAIN) indicates the specified domain cannot be contacted during automated provisioning. Common causes: DNS resolution failure, network connectivity to DC, incorrect domain credentials or UPN format
solution: Verify DNS settings point to domain controller, check VNet DNS configuration, ensure credentials have domain join permissions. Workaround: manually domain-join the VM after provisioning using domain admin credentials
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Feature Verification/MSIX app attach/[Lab]MSIX+ADDS.md

symptom: Remote Desktop client (MSI and Store versions) end of support timeline for Mooncake. Customers need to migrate to Windows App
rootCause: Microsoft is retiring Remote Desktop client in favor of Windows App. Store version EOL: May 27, 2025. MSI version EOL extended to September 28, 2026 for Mooncake (previously March 27, 2026). MSI retirement deferred until Windows App fully supports Mooncake
solution: Migrate to Windows App. For Mooncake: MSI version will continue to be supported until Windows App is fully supported in China cloud. Check Azure Health notification 8Q5F-800 for latest timeline. Legacy macOS Remote Desktop available from App Store or App Center
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Feature Verification/Windows App replacement.md

symptom: Microsoft Teams running very slow in AVD session. High P95 input delay reported in AVD Insights portal metrics
rootCause: High input delay on session host indicates either network performance bottleneck (if metrics green) or session host/process performance issue (if metrics red). Input delay measures time between user input and when application processes it
solution: 1. Check AVD Insights input delay metrics in portal. 2. If green -> focus on network performance (latency/bandwidth). 3. If red -> use Perfmon/Host Analyzer for host performance, Procmon for process-level slowness. 4. Query User Input Delay per Process counter via Log Analytics Perf table. 5. Follow VM sizing best practices
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======20. AVD=======/20.10 AVD _ The teams is running very slow in AVD.md

symptom: AVD user cannot login to session host. Session connects then shows Windows is preparing, FSLogix is loading, user profile is loading then user is signing out and auto-disconnects. FSLogix log shows error 00000002/80070002, UsrClass.dat fails to load
rootCause: Known FSLogix bug where profile hive unload fails, leaving refcount stuck in registry. On next logon, ntuser.dat and UsrClass.dat fail to load due to stale refcount, causing profile service to fail and auto-sign-out
solution: Upgrade to FSLogix v2201 hotfix 2 (2.9.8228.50276). Quick workaround: 1. Login with working user. 2. Force logoff the affected user session. 3. Open regedit -> HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\ProfileService\References -> find affected user GUID -> set refcount to 0. 4. Retry login

symptom: New AAD DS user login to AVD session host fails with wrong user name and password error, even after confirming password is synced in AAD DS. Occurs when AAD DS is secured with NTLMv2-only setting
rootCause: AAD DS security hardening sets NTLM authentication to accept NTLMv2 response only. If AVD session host VM is not configured to use NTLMv2, the login is denied by AAD DS
solution: Force session host to use NTLMv2 only: set registry HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\LmCompatibilityLevel = 5, or configure via Group Policy: Network security: LAN Manager authentication level = Send NTLMv2 response only
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======20. AVD=======/20.7 AVD _ AAD DS integration related troubleshoot.md

symptom: Legacy per-user MFA enabled on Azure AD cloud user causes login failure on AADJ AVD session host desktop. AAD Operational event log shows MFA cannot be performed during authentication
rootCause: Legacy per-user MFA is not supported for AADJ session host desktop logon. Both RD client logon and desktop logon interact with AAD, but desktop logon cannot complete MFA challenge interactively on AADJ VMs
solution: Replace legacy per-user MFA with Conditional Access policy-based MFA. CA MFA only triggers on AVD client sign-in (not desktop logon), allowing successful desktop authentication. For local admin logon on AADJ host, use format: localhost\<account name>
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======20. AVD=======/20.8 AVD _ MFA _ Fail to log onto AADJ session hos.md

symptom: AVD Remote Desktop client Subscribe button connects to Global Azure AVD endpoint instead of Mooncake (21v) endpoint. Domain user on domain-joined PC subscribing with own account hits Global
rootCause: RD client Subscribe flow first checks registry DefaultFeedUrl, then performs home cloud discovery via token request. For domain-joined PCs with hybrid identity, home cloud discovery returns Global Azure authority, causing client to subscribe to wrong endpoint
solution: Set registry DefaultFeedUrl to Mooncake: reg.exe ADD HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\MSRDC\Policies /v DefaultFeedUrl /t REG_SZ /d https://rdweb.wvd.azure.cn/api/arm/feeddiscovery. Deploy via Group Policy for domain-joined clients
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======20. AVD=======/20.4 AVD _ remote desktop client app Subscribe beh.md

symptom: Cannot delete assigned user from personal AVD session host via Azure portal. No option available in portal UI to unassign user
rootCause: Azure portal does not provide UI option to remove assigned user from personal desktop session host. This operation is only supported via PowerShell
solution: Use PowerShell: 1. New-AzWvdRegistrationInfo to create token. 2. Get-AzWvdSessionHost to find host. 3. Remove-AzWvdSessionHost to remove. 4. Re-register VM using script. 5. Restart-AzVM
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======20. AVD=======/20.6 AVD _ How to delete assigned user from a pers.md

symptom: AVD user sees black screen for ~5 minutes after login. FSLogix error: FindFile failed - The network path was not found (ErrorCode 53).
rootCause: User lacks permissions to access Azure File Share for FSLogix profile. Missing Storage File Data SMB Share Contributor RBAC or Modify NTFS permission.
solution: 1) Assign Storage File Data SMB Share Contributor on Azure File Share. 2) Assign Modify NTFS permission. 3) Configure SIDDirNameMatch/SIDDirNamePattern registry.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 1 - 25th Oct 2021/[Bob] 2107140060000056 - Black Screen After Login.md

symptom: AVD users redirected to new session host instead of reconnecting to disconnected session. FSLogix profile fails to load on new host because VHD still attached to old host.
rootCause: Known race condition in AVD backend: feed refresh during 2-min connection window removes user session info from DB. RDCB cannot find existing session.
solution: PG fix deployed mid-May 2021. Workaround: wait 2+ minutes after opening WVD client before connecting. Temp fix: forcefully logoff user via PowerShell.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 1 - 25th Oct 2021/[Bruce] 2103250060001559 - User is not redirected.md

symptom: Cannot access WVD via Remote Desktop client or RDWeb. Error Code: 0x80090016 on Windows 10 client.
rootCause: Error 0x80090016 related to corrupted system files, certificate/key issues on client. Can occur with PIN login.
solution: Try connecting from different client to isolate. Collect WVD logs. Check for corrupted system files. Reset PIN/credential store.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 1 - 25th Oct 2021/[Icy] 2103300060004004 - Unable To Connect.md

symptom: AVD random/intermittent connection failures in regular time pattern (e.g. at XX:38 minutes). ErrorCodeSymbolic: ConnectionFailedReverseConnectStackTransportError.
rootCause: Under PG investigation (ICM-261514251). Suspected environment scheduled tasks coinciding with failure pattern.
solution: Diagnose via Kusto DiagActivity/DiagError in WVD database (rdskmc.chinaeast2.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn). Collect netsh network trace. Check scheduled tasks matching failure times. Verify Azure Firewall WVD service tags.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 1 - 25th Oct 2021/[Ning] 2108230060005499  - Random Connection Failu.md

symptom: After sysprep/capture of AVD VM and deploying from custom image, new VM visible but not in session pool. Users cannot login.
rootCause: Custom image VM needs fresh AVD agent installation and registration. Agent from original VM invalidated after sysprep.
solution: 1) Verify RD Agent services running. 2) Check HKLM Software Microsoft RDInfraAgent registry. 3) Review AgentInstall.txt. 4) If newer agent: install MSI with new registration token. 5) If not: uninstall all, fresh install with New-RdsRegistrationInfo token.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 1 - 25th Oct 2021/[Philip] 2103050050000863 - Custom Image Issue.md

symptom: AVD connection fails. FSLogix error code 112: There is not enough space on the disk. User VHD profile has less than 200MB free.
rootCause: FSLogix profile VHD(x) nearly full. FSLogix cannot mount profile when VHD has less than 200MB free.
solution: Expand FSLogix VHD(x) disk. Verify VHD size via storage path.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 1 - 25th Oct 2021/[Rika] 2109030040000074 - Unable To Connect.md

symptom: AVD connection fails: session host is unhealthy. Zombie/orphan session exists on shutdown session host preventing new connections.
rootCause: Zombie session not cleaned up after host shutdown. Stale session records in AVD backend route user to unhealthy host.
solution: 1) Start host and Log off users in Azure Portal. 2) PowerShell: Remove-AzWvdUserSession -Force. 3) Update AVD agent to latest. 4) Schedule Remove-AzWvdUserSession script before shutdown.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 1 - 25th Oct 2021/[Rika] 2109030040000074 - Unable To Connect.md

symptom: AVD session host shows Unavailable. Event log: Unable to retrieve DefaultAgent from registry: NullReferenceException at RDAgentBootLoader.BootLoaderSettings.get_DefaultAgentPath().
rootCause: RDAgentBootLoader service failed to read DefaultAgent registry key under HKLM SOFTWARE Microsoft RDAgentBootLoader. Possible transient corruption.
solution: 1) Restart: net stop RDAgentBootLoader then net start RDAgentBootLoader. 2) If persists: export regkey, stop service, check for illegal chars, delete and reimport regkey, restart. 3) Alt: remove host from pool, set IsRegistered=0, paste new token, restart service.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 1 - 25th Oct 2021/[Steve] 2107010010000614 - Session Host Not Availa.md

symptom: AVD session randomly reconnects 1-2 times per hour. Backend log: connection closed as client stopped receiving heartbeats from session host.
rootCause: Client intermittently loses connectivity to WVD service URLs. AVD heartbeat: 1 pkt/sec via gateway. 8 missed=warning, 16 missed=disconnect. Azure IP ranges may be blocked by firewall.
solution: 1) Whitelist all WVD URLs on client firewall. 2) Whitelist Azure IP ranges: AzureCloud, WindowsVirtualDesktop, AzureFrontDoor tags. 3) Diagnose with WVDAgentURLTool.exe, netmon, tracert to RDGateway cluster.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 1 - 25th Oct 2021/[Susan] 2105250060001138 - Random Disconnects.md

symptom: AVD slow logon when new user profile is created on customized image. PerUserSetup logon task takes 20+ seconds for large profiles (100+ MB)
rootCause: First-time user profile creation triggers PerUserSetup logon task which is proportional to profile size. On customized images with many pre-installed apps, this task takes significantly longer
solution: Collect shell trace (SBSL SDP) to identify time-consuming logon tasks. PerUserSetup duration is expected for large profiles. Consider optimizing base image to reduce profile initialization time. Use FSLogix profile containers to avoid per-machine profile creation
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 1 - 25th Oct 2021/[Wei] 2103300060003380 - Slow Logon Issue.md

symptom: AVD session host not accepting new user connections while other hosts in the pool work fine. Users get no resource available or connection fails to specific host
rootCause: Domain trust relationship was corrupted on the affected session host VM, preventing Kerberos authentication for new RDP sessions
solution: Remove the affected session host from the domain and re-join it. Verify domain trust with Test-ComputerSecureChannel -Verbose. For Hybrid AD-joined VMs, also verify AAD sync status after domain re-join
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 1 - 25th Oct 2021/[Xuzhou] 2107150060001055 - Unable To Connect.md

symptom: Windows Hello For Business (WHfB) authentication fails when RDP to Azure-hosted AVD session hosts. Face/fingerprint/PIN login fails while AD user/password works
rootCause: Two missing prerequisites: (1) Issuing CA certificate for KDC certificate not in NTAuth store, (2) Azure domain controllers not enrolled for KDC certificates due to missing firewall rules between DCs and Certificate Authority
solution: 1. Add CA cert to NTAuth store: Certutil -enterprise -addstore NTAuth issuingCA.cer. 2. Fix firewall rules for DC-to-CA connectivity. 3. Enroll Azure DCs for KDC certificates. Ref: Hybrid Azure AD joined WHfB PKI setup
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 2 - 1st Nov 2021/[Bob]2107090050002010 _ Unable to login.md

symptom: New VM created from captured image cannot join AVD host pool. Deployment times out and backend VM is not created normally
rootCause: Master image was captured from a VM already joined to a host pool without running sysprep. Existing host pool registration info causes conflicts
solution: Always run sysprep (generalize) before capturing master image. Use a VM never joined to a host pool as source. If already occurred, create new clean image from fresh VM
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 2 - 1st Nov 2021/[Bruce] 2110210060000174 _ Unable to Create Sessio.md

symptom: AVD user sees black screen after login followed by error message. Session host shows upgrading status in backend
rootCause: Session host VM stuck in upgrading state after RD Agent auto-update did not complete properly, leaving VM in transitional state rejecting connections
solution: Restart the affected VM to trigger re-check of WVD agent status. VM returns to Available after restart. Use Kusto DiagActivity query by UPN to identify upgrading state
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 2 - 1st Nov 2021/[Philip] 2107290060001583 _ AVD login issue.md

symptom: AVD VMs not joining host pool after provisioning. VMs created successfully but fail to register. Agent connectivity errors in event logs
rootCause: Transient network issue between session host VNet and AVD control plane. RD Agent cannot reach broker endpoint to complete registration
solution: 1. Verify connectivity with WVDAgentURLTool.exe. 2. Check required URL list. 3. Retry with manual RD Agent install + fresh registration token. 4. Engage networking if persistent
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 2 - 1st Nov 2021/[Philip] 2107290060001583 _ AVD login issue.md

symptom: Cannot add AVD session host using Windows Server 2019 Datacenter from marketplace. Error: MarketplacePurchaseEligibilityFailed - offer not available in region
rootCause: Windows Server 2019 Datacenter marketplace offer not available in customer subscription region. Marketplace/region availability issue, not AVD-specific
solution: Workaround: Create VM separately then register to host pool manually with PowerShell registration token. PG fixed the marketplace availability eventually
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 2 - 1st Nov 2021/[Rika]2105260060001876 _ Cannot deploy additional.md

symptom: AVD RemoteApp keeps asking for credentials when launching second RemoteApp after closing the first one
rootCause: Expected behavior. When all RemoteApps close, shared session enters Disconnected state. New RemoteApp launch treated as new connection requiring authentication
solution: By design. Workaround: Keep at least one RemoteApp open to maintain Active session. Configure SSO/credential delegation. Educate users this is expected
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 2 - 1st Nov 2021/[Steve]2108110060002763 _ WVD keeps asking authent.md

symptom: AVD users in China connecting through HK proxy fail to authenticate. Login page reaches login.microsoftonline.com but fails to connect to aafcdn.msftauth.net, resulting in blank authentication page.
rootCause: Proxy/firewall blocking authentication endpoints (aafcdn.msftauth.net, logincdn.msauth.net) required for Azure AD authentication flow during AVD login.
solution: Whitelist URLs in proxy/firewall: aadcdn.msftauth.net, lgincdnvzeuno.azureedge.net, logincdn.msauth.net, login.live.com, *.msftauth.net, *.azureedge.net, *.msauth.net.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 2 - 1st Nov 2021/[Susan] 2106180040000243 _ Unable to access VWD fr.md

symptom: AVD host pool VM deployment fails in subnet with error: subnet referenced by external PaaS resource (HostingEnvironment). Cannot create IaaS VM in PaaS-integrated subnet.
rootCause: Subnet has VNet integration with PaaS service (Microsoft.Web/hostingEnvironments). Azure does not support creating IaaS VMs in a subnet integrated with PaaS services.
solution: 1) Remove VNet integration link from subnet. 2) Or deploy AVD VMs in a different subnet without PaaS integration.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 2 - 1st Nov 2021/[Wei] 2106110060000413 _ Issue deploying WVD host.md

symptom: All AVD session hosts across multiple host pools become unavailable simultaneously. Users get no resource available error. Heartbeat fails: Unable to connect to wss://rdbroker-g-us-r1.wvd.microsoft.com.
rootCause: Session hosts lost WebSocket connectivity to RDBroker URL due to network/firewall (Palo Alto) blocking. Agent upgrade also contributed to transient unavailability.
solution: 1) Reboot affected session hosts. 2) Whitelist all AVD required URLs on firewall. 3) Monitor agent upgrade windows.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 2 - 1st Nov 2021/[Xuzhou] 2109220060000730 _ all host pool shows un.md

symptom: AVD sign-in fails after Conditional Access sign-in frequency policy rejects PRT (error 70044). Seamless SSO fallback also fails because autologon.microsoftazuread-sso.com is blocked.
rootCause: CA sign-in frequency policy rejects PRT (expected). Seamless SSO fallback URL autologon.microsoftazuread-sso.com blocked by corporate firewall.
solution: Unblock autologon.microsoftazuread-sso.com in corporate firewall. Verify URL accessible from client devices via browser test.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 3 - 8th Nov 2021/Concepts# Condition access_.md

symptom: AVD RemoteApp authentication fails with auto-populated corporate account after 20-30 minutes. CA sign-in frequency set to 1 hour but users cannot authenticate with pre-populated account.
rootCause: Conditional Access sign-in frequency policy interacts with RemoteApp session token management. Subsequent launches fail to authenticate with cached account.
solution: Workaround: Use 'Use another account' option and manually enter credentials. Review CA sign-in frequency policy interaction with RemoteApp.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 3 - 8th Nov 2021/[Icy] 2109220060000172 _ remote app is not autheti.md

symptom: AVD user application settings disappear and desktop files lost when logging on. FSLogix profile container not loading properly.
rootCause: FSLogix registry key DeleteLocalProfileWhenVHDShouldApply set to 1, causing local profile deletion when VHD profile loads. DNS issue also prevented FSLogix from mounting profile VHD.
solution: 1) Set DeleteLocalProfileWhenVHDShouldApply to 0 in FSLogix registry. 2) Fix DNS resolution for FSLogix profile container mount.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 3 - 8th Nov 2021/[Ning] 2110080060000382 _ fslogix issue.md

symptom: AVD session host domain join fails via domainjoin extension with VMExtensionProvisioningError. Netsetup log shows INSUFF_ACCESS_RIGHTS (0x5) when setting computer attributes.
rootCause: Duplicate computer objects in on-prem AD with same name. Domain join extension fails with 0x5 because computer account already exists and join account lacks permission to modify it.
solution: 1) Delete conflicting/stale computer objects in AD. 2) Or change VM naming prefix. 3) Grant appropriate permissions on target OU.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 3 - 8th Nov 2021/[Philip] 2109240060001498 _ failed to add desktop.md

symptom: AVD session establishes then immediately logs off. RDInfra trace shows RdpStackDisconnect with UserLogoff, force logoff due to critical failure (LogoffReason 12). Reproducible via direct RDP.
rootCause: VM-level issue (suspected NLA configuration or performance problem) causing session to force logoff immediately after connection establishment.
solution: 1) Disable NLA via Azure Portal script, restart VM. 2) Re-enable NLA. Issue resolved after reboot cycle. 3) Monitor VM Insights for performance issues.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 3 - 8th Nov 2021/[Philip] 2109240060001498 _ failed to add desktop.md

symptom: China users accessing AVD via HK proxy fail to authenticate. MFA/login page does not load after reaching login.microsoftonline.com. Fiddler trace shows connection to aadcdn.msftauth.net fails.
rootCause: Proxy/firewall blocks aadcdn.msftauth.net endpoint which is required for loading the authentication page after AAD login redirect.
solution: Whitelist the following URLs in proxy/firewall: aadcdn.msftauth.net, lgincdnvzeuno.azureedge.net, logincdn.msauth.net, login.live.com, *.msftauth.net, *.azureedge.net, *.msauth.net. Collect Fiddler trace + Network Monitor simultaneously on both working/non-working scenarios for comparison if issue persists.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 2 - 1st Nov 2021/[Susan] 2106180040000243 _ Unable to access VWD fr.md

symptom: Deploying AVD host pool VMs in a subnet fails with error that subnet is referenced by external PaaS resource (e.g., Microsoft.Web/hostingEnvironments ASE).
rootCause: IaaS resources (VMs) cannot be created in a subnet that has existing PaaS VNet integration (e.g., App Service Environment). Azure does not allow mixing IaaS and delegated PaaS resources in the same subnet.
solution: 1) Remove the VNet integration/service association link from the subnet before deploying AVD VMs. 2) Or deploy AVD host pool VMs in a different subnet without PaaS delegations.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 2 - 1st Nov 2021/[Wei] 2106110060000413 _ Issue deploying WVD host.md

symptom: Multiple AVD session hosts become unhealthy simultaneously across several host pools. Users get session host is unhealthy error. Agent heartbeat service throws Unable to connect to broker endpoint (wss://rdbroker-*.wvd.microsoft.com). Event 3019 logs transport connection failure.
rootCause: Network connectivity disruption between session hosts and AVD RDBroker WebSocket endpoint. Firewall (Palo Alto) or cloud proxy intermittently blocking outbound WebSocket connections to AVD control plane URLs.
solution: 1) Reboot affected session hosts to recover immediately. 2) Ensure firewall/proxy permanently allows all AVD required URLs per https://docs.azure.cn/virtual-desktop/safe-url-list. 3) Monitor Event ID 3019 for early detection of heartbeat failures.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 2 - 1st Nov 2021/[Xuzhou] 2109220060000730 _ all host pool shows un.md

symptom: AVD sign-in fails when Conditional Access sign-in frequency policy is configured. User selects auto-populated account but authentication fails. AAD reports error 70044 (PRT rejected due to sign-in frequency enforcement).
rootCause: With sign-in frequency CA policy active, the Primary Refresh Token (PRT) is rejected by AAD. Client falls back to Seamless SSO mechanism, but the required endpoint autologon.microsoftazuread-sso.com is blocked by corporate network/firewall.
solution: 1) Ensure autologon.microsoftazuread-sso.com is accessible from client devices. 2) Test by visiting https://autologon.microsoftazuread-sso.com/ in browser. 3) If still failing, collect Fiddler trace + Network Monitor for further analysis.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 3 - 8th Nov 2021/Concepts# Condition access_.md

symptom: AVD user application settings and desktop files disappear on every login. FSLogix profile container is configured but user data is not persisted across sessions.
rootCause: FSLogix registry key DeleteLocalProfileWhenVHDShouldApply is set to 1, which deletes the local profile on each login when a VHD profile match exists. Additionally, DNS resolution issues to the file share prevented proper VHD mounting.
solution: 1) Set registry key DeleteLocalProfileWhenVHDShouldApply to 0. 2) Verify DNS resolution to the FSLogix profile container file share. 3) Reference: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/fslogix/profile-container-configuration-reference
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 3 - 8th Nov 2021/[Ning] 2110080060000382 _ fslogix issue.md

symptom: AVD session host domain join fails via domainjoin extension with VMExtensionProvisioningError. Manual domain join also fails. Netsetup log shows INSUFF_ACCESS_RIGHTS (0x5) when setting unicodePwd via LDAP modify.
rootCause: Stale/conflicting computer objects with the same name already exist in Active Directory. The domain join service account cannot modify the existing computer object attributes due to insufficient permissions on the pre-existing object.
solution: 1) Delete conflicting stale computer objects from AD. 2) Or change the AVD VM name prefix to avoid naming conflicts. 3) Ensure domain join service account has Reset Password and Write All Properties permissions on the target OU.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 3 - 8th Nov 2021/[Philip] 2109240060001498 _ failed to add desktop.md

symptom: AVD session connects but immediately logs off. User sees dialog box disappear after clicking Submit. RDP trace shows Force logoff because critical failure in session with LogoffReason=12. RDP to VM directly also fails.
rootCause: Network Level Authentication (NLA) state corruption on the session host VM, possibly caused by transient VM performance issues. NLA rejects the authentication handshake causing immediate session termination.
solution: 1) Disable NLA via Azure Portal Run Command or registry. 2) Restart the VM to apply changes. 3) Re-enable NLA after restart - this resets the NLA state. 4) If recurrent, monitor VM performance via Azure Monitor VM Insights.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 3 - 8th Nov 2021/[Philip] 2109240060001498 _ failed to add desktop.md

symptom: FSLogix profile fails to attach with error 'The user profile failed to attach. Please contact support'. Status 0x00000014 (Cannot import registry information), Error 0x00000001 (Incorrect function). FSLogix log shows SHSetKnownFolderPath failed with access denied (0x80070005).
rootCause: GPO 'Prohibit User from manually redirecting Profile Folders' (User Configuration > Administrative Templates > Desktop) was enabled, setting DisablePersonalDirChange=1, which prevents FSLogix from changing INetCache location. Additionally, customer had configured CleanupInvalidSessions registry key under Software slogixpps which conflicted with their environment.
solution: 1. Disable GPO 'Prohibit User from manually redirecting Profile Folders'. 2. Remove the CleanupInvalidSessions registry key (HKLM\SOFTWARE\FSLogix\Apps\CleanupInvalidSessions). Note: CleanupInvalidSessions should not be used with Cloud Cache when concurrent sessions are in use.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 3 - 8th Nov 2021/[Steve] 2105030040000722  _ 'The user profile fail.md

symptom: FSLogix profile on WVD shows CORRUPT_xxx_Profile_username.VHDX and user receives 'ctfmon.exe Unknown Hard Error' when logging in. Deleting existing profile and re-login does not fix the issue; new profile also becomes corrupt.
rootCause: NTUSER.DAT was missing inside the FSLogix profile container VHDX file. When mounted via Disk Management, the profile folder was completely empty. Missing NTUSER.DAT causes registry settings and IME (ctfmon.exe) settings to be unavailable.
solution: Mount the VHDX via Disk Management to verify if NTUSER.DAT exists. If VHDX is empty/corrupt: delete the corrupt VHDX profile from Azure Files storage, then have user log in again to create a fresh profile. If NTUSER.DAT exists, engage PFE for further ctfmon.exe investigation.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 3 - 8th Nov 2021/[Susan] 2108100040000307 _ Failed to load Fslogix.md

symptom: All users in all host pools fail to connect to AVD session hosts. RD Client shows TlsHandshake to CredSSPState then disconnects with ConnectionBroken(8). Session host Schannel Event ID 36874: 'An TLS 1.2 connection request was received but none of the cipher suites supported by the client are supported by the server.'
rootCause: Required TLS 1.2 cipher AES128-SHA256 (required by Azure Front Door for AVD connections) was disabled or removed from the session host via SSL Cipher Suite Order GPO. All AVD connections go through Azure Front Door, which requires specific TLS 1.2 ciphers.
solution: 1. Check SSL Cipher Suite Order GPO via gpresult. 2. Verify registry HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Cryptography includes AES128-SHA256. 3. Ensure all AVD-required ciphers (matching Azure Front Door cipher suite) are enabled. Ref: ADO Wiki - TLS or Cipher Mismatch.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 3 - 8th Nov 2021/[Xuzhou] 2104260010000402 _ Users got below errors.md

symptom: AVD session host provisioning fails to join domain with error 1355 when deploying host pool with AD DS. Error: 'Failed to join domain, option=NetSetupJoinDomain, NetSetupAcctCreate. Error code 1355'.
rootCause: Domain join failure error 1355 (ERROR_NO_SUCH_DOMAIN) typically indicates DNS resolution failure or network connectivity issue to domain controller during AVD host pool provisioning.
solution: Manually join the VM to the domain as workaround. Verify DNS resolution to DC, network connectivity, and domain credentials. For MSIX app attach with AD DS: ensure computer objects sync to AAD, create security group in AD DS, assign RBAC, configure NTFS permissions.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 4 - 22th Nov 2021/##[Lab3]MSIX+ADDS.md

symptom: When installing language packs for Windows 10 multi-session AVD images, certain Feature on Demand (FoD) cab packages cannot be found in the ISO: MSPaint, Notepad, PowerShell ISE, Printing-WFS, StepsRecorder, WordPad FoD packages.
rootCause: The FoD cab packages listed in Microsoft documentation are not included in the standard language pack ISO. These are separate Features on Demand from a different source.
solution: Download the missing FoD packages separately from the Features on Demand ISO (not the language pack ISO). The FoD ISO can be obtained from VLSC or Visual Studio Subscriptions. Alternatively, skip these optional FoD packages if not required.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 4 - 22th Nov 2021/[Bruce] Lab Verification - Install language packag.md

symptom: AVD user gets corrupt FSLogix profile (CORRUPT_xxx_Profile_username.VHDX) and ctfmon.exe 'Unknown Hard Error' dialog on login. Deleting profile and recreating does not initially help
rootCause: NTUSER.DAT is missing inside the FSLogix profile VHD container. Without NTUSER.DAT, registry settings and IME settings (used by ctfmon.exe) are not found, causing the Unknown Hard Error
solution: 1. Mount the VHDX via Disk Management (Action > Attach VHD) on a session host with local admin. 2. Check if NTUSER.DAT exists in the profile folder. 3. If NTUSER.DAT is missing or folder is empty, delete the corrupt profile VHDX entirely. 4. Have user login again to create a fresh profile. If issue recurs, investigate what corrupted NTUSER.DAT (e.g. ungraceful session termination)
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 3 - 8th Nov 2021/[Susan] 2108100040000307 _ Failed to load Fslogix.md

symptom: Azure Monitor workbook deployment for AVD fails with 'Deployment validation failed: Template parameter JToken type is not valid. Expected String, Uri. Actual Object.'
rootCause: Azure Monitor configuration workbook passes object-type parameter instead of string when setting up session host data (performance counters/event logs).
solution: If workbook deployment fails, go to Log Analytics workspace > Agents configuration to manually configure performance counters and Windows event logs for AVD session hosts.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 4 - 22th Nov 2021/[Philip] Lab verification - Use Azure Monitor for.md

symptom: AVD auto scaling setup script CreateOrUpdateAzAutoAccount.ps1 fails with 'Unable to acquire token for tenant' and 'Set-AzContext: Please provide a valid tenant or a valid subscription' error.
rootCause: AADTenantId parameter contains placeholder value instead of actual Azure AD tenant GUID.
solution: Replace the AADTenantId placeholder with actual Azure AD tenant ID in GUID format. Find tenant ID via Azure Portal > Azure Active Directory > Properties.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 4 - 22th Nov 2021/[Steve] Lab Verification - AVD auto scaling.md

symptom: AVD auto scaling script fails with 'The remote name could not be resolved' for *.ods.opinsights.azure.com endpoint when creating Log Analytics custom log table.
rootCause: Log Analytics workspace ODS ingestion endpoint DNS not resolvable. Workspace may not be fully provisioned yet, or network/DNS configuration blocking the endpoint.
solution: Re-run the script after Log Analytics workspace is fully provisioned. Verify DNS resolution for the ODS endpoint. For Mooncake, ensure endpoint uses .azure.cn domain.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 4 - 22th Nov 2021/[Steve] Lab Verification - AVD auto scaling.md

symptom: ADFS SSO for AVD does not work in Azure AD DS (AADDS) environment. SSO only available with federated authentication on Windows Desktop/Web client.
rootCause: AVD ADFS SSO has three limitations: (1) Not supported with Azure AD DS, (2) Requires AD FS federated authentication, (3) Only supported on Windows Desktop client and Web client.
solution: Use on-premises AD FS with federated authentication. Deploy AD FS server (not AADDS). Only Windows Desktop/Web client supports ADFS SSO. For AADDS, consider Azure AD SSO (Entra ID SSO) instead.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 4 - 22th Nov 2021/[Xuzhou] Lab verification - Configure AVD ADFS SSO.md

symptom: Azure Monitor configuration workbook for AVD fails with 'Deployment template validation failed: Template parameter JToken type is not valid. Expected String, Uri. Actual Object'
rootCause: The Azure Monitor configuration workbook for AVD may pass incorrect parameter types (Object instead of String) when deploying performance counter and event log settings via ARM template, particularly in Mooncake environment.
solution: Instead of using the configuration workbook, manually configure performance counters and Windows event logs in the Log Analytics workspace Agents configuration section. Reference: https://docs.azure.cn/zh-cn/azure-monitor/agents/data-sources-performance-counters
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 4 - 22th Nov 2021/[Philip] Lab verification - Use Azure Monitor for.md

symptom: AVD auto scaling script (CreateOrUpdateAzAutoAccount.ps1) fails with 'Invoke-WebRequest: The remote name could not be resolved: <workspace-id>.ods.opinsights.azure.com' when setting up Log Analytics integration in Mooncake
rootCause: The AVD auto scaling setup script references the Global Azure ODS endpoint (.ods.opinsights.azure.com) instead of the Mooncake-specific endpoint (.ods.opinsights.azure.cn). DNS resolution fails because the global domain is not reachable from China.
solution: 1. Verify the Log Analytics workspace endpoint uses .azure.cn suffix for Mooncake. 2. Modify the script to use the correct Mooncake ODS endpoint: <workspace-id>.ods.opinsights.azure.cn. 3. Alternatively, skip the Log Analytics integration if not required and configure scaling without OMS logging.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 4 - 22th Nov 2021/[Steve] Lab Verification - AVD auto scaling.md

symptom: AVD users see ADFS login prompt for session host RDP sign-in even though AAD gateway sign-in was silent (no prompt). Expected SSO behavior is not fully seamless across both gateway and session host.
rootCause: AAD cookie is still valid allowing silent sign-in to AVD gateway, but ADFS cookie has expired. Session host RDP authentication requires a separate ADFS token (via oauth2/authorize with scope=logon_cert), which triggers an ADFS login prompt when the ADFS cookie is missing or expired.
solution: This is expected behavior when ADFS cookie expires before AAD cookie. After user authenticates to ADFS once, the ADFS cookie is set and subsequent connections within the cookie lifetime will be fully silent. Adjust ADFS cookie lifetime (TokenLifetime) to match or exceed the AAD session lifetime. Note: ADFS SSO not supported with Azure AD DS, must use federated identity, Windows client only.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 4 - 22th Nov 2021/[Xuzhou] Lab verification - Configure AVD ADFS SSO.md

symptom: FSLogix profile container VHD/VHDX files grow continuously but never shrink, consuming excessive storage. Freed/deleted blocks remain physically present in dynamically expanding virtual disks.
rootCause: VHD dynamic expanding disks do not reclaim freed blocks when files are deleted within the virtual disk during user sessions. This is by-design behavior of thin-provisioned VHDs.
solution: Use Invoke-FslShrinkDisk PowerShell script (https://github.com/FSLogix/Invoke-FslShrinkDisk) to compact VHD files offline. Do NOT use frx.exe for shrinking — it corrupts the VHD causing 'Cannot open virtual disk' error 0x0000003b. Must run on VHDs of logged-off users only. Newer FSLogix versions have built-in VHD Disk Compaction feature (see learn.microsoft.com/fslogix/concepts-vhd-disk-compaction).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## How To/Fslogix profile container vhd shrinking.md
