symptom: When publishing Edge browser as RemoteApp in AVD, users can access session host local drives and run cmd/PowerShell through the browser address bar (file:// protocol), violating security policies.
rootCause: Browser RemoteApp runs on session host with full file system access. No default restriction prevents browser from navigating to local file paths via file:// protocol.
solution: Configure Group Policy URLBlocklist to block file:// URLs. For Edge: Microsoft.Policies.Edge::URLBlocklist (admx.help). For Chrome: Google.Policies.Chrome::URLBlocklist. For Firefox: Mozilla.Policies.Firefox::B_WebsiteFilter_Block. Apply via GPO to session hosts.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## How To/Restrict Edge (RemoteApp) from accessing local dri.md

symptom: Sysprep fails with error on AVD session host image after installing language pack on Windows 10 multi-session. Language pack provisioning breaks sysprep generalization.
rootCause: Windows Update patches for the installed language pack are not applied before running sysprep. Language pack requires corresponding cumulative updates to be installed first.
solution: After installing language pack, MUST run Windows Update and install ALL patches before sysprep. For golden image building, use dism with /Region='All' flag: Dism /Online /Add-ProvisionedAppxPackage /PackagePath:<path> /LicensePath:<path> /Region='All' (run as admin). Critical: check and apply Windows Update BEFORE AND AFTER language pack installation, then restart before sysprep.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## How To/Install language pack.md

symptom: AVD users fail to connect frequently with error 'Reverse Connect to rdgateway-*.wvd.azure.cn failed with error 0x80072F7D. Make sure it is reachable from your network.' Connection works when VPN is on but fails when VPN is off or after idle period.
rootCause: Network connectivity issue between client and AVD rdgateway endpoints. VPN routes traffic differently; without VPN, client cannot reliably reach rdgateway. Packet loss observed to gateway endpoints from client network. ADFS endpoint accessibility may also be a factor.
solution: 1. Use Kusto DiagActivity+DiagError join to identify failed connections and error codes. 2. Query RDInfraTrace to find which rdgateway handles successful vs failed connections. 3. Verify network path to all rdgateway endpoints (rdgateway-c100/c101-cne2/cnn2-r1.wvd.azure.cn). 4. Check VPN split-tunneling configuration. 5. Ensure ADFS endpoint reachable if federated auth is used.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Mooncake Case Study/2109010040000865 - Connection Failure.md

symptom: AVD connection failure with error ConnectionFailedAdTrustedRelationshipFailure after applying NSG outbound restrictions on session hosts. Error: session host cannot add user to Remote Desktop Users group because it lacks AD credentials.
rootCause: NSG rules blocked network communication between session host VNET and domain controller VNET (peered VNETs). The WindowsVirtualDesktop service tag only covers AVD service IPs, not intra-VNET traffic needed for AD authentication.
solution: Add service tag VirtualNetwork to NSG inbound/outbound rules to allow traffic between peered VNETs (session host to domain controller). Verify session host can reach DC on required AD ports (LDAP 389, Kerberos 88, etc.).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Mooncake Case Study/2109280010000131 - Connection Failure.md

symptom: AVD VM deployment stuck at OOBE screen when using custom image. Panther logs show Didnt find unattend file for this phase of Setup. CD-ROM device disabled in image.
rootCause: CD-ROM device was disabled in the custom image. During AVD VM deployment, an ISO is mounted via CD-ROM to provide the unattend.xml answer file. With CD-ROM disabled, the answer file cannot be read, causing OOBE to hang.
solution: Re-enable the CD-ROM device in Device Manager before capturing the custom image. Check C:/Windows/Panther/setupact.log to confirm unattend file detection.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Mooncake Case Study/2110180040003853 - Custom Image.md

symptom: AVD session host VM (v4 series without temp disk) shows error at first logon. Pagefile configured to D: drive in unattend.xml but D: drive does not exist on v4 VM SKU.
rootCause: Mooncake AVD custom image unattend.xml hardcodes pagefile to D:/pagefile.sys. VM SKUs without temp disk (Dv4/Ev4) have no D: drive, causing pagefile initialization failure.
solution: Run ConfigurePagefile.ps1 via Custom Script Extension: REG ADD PagingFiles to ?:/pagefile.sys 0 0 (auto-detect). Or deploy via ARM template for batch update. Registry: HKLM/SYSTEM/CurrentControlSet/Control/Session Manager/Memory Management/PagingFiles.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Mooncake Case Study/2203030050001117 - AVD21H2镜像创建sysprep报错.md

symptom: User cannot login to AVD session host. Session connects showing Windows is preparing, FSLogix is loading, user profile is loading, then user is signing out automatically. FSLogix log shows error 00000002/80070002. Procmon shows FSLogix unloaded the hive file. User Profile Service event log shows logon/logoff events without successful ntuser.dat and UsrClass.dat loading.
rootCause: FSLogix profile container UsrClass.dat failed to load during user logon. The hive file became corrupted or inaccessible, causing FSLogix to unload the profile and trigger automatic sign-out.
solution: 1. Check FSLogix logs for error 00000002/80070002. 2. Check Procmon for FSLogix hive unload events. 3. Check User Profile Service events (Event IDs 1-4) to verify if ntuser.dat/UsrClass.dat loaded successfully. 4. Compare with working users - successful logons show profile load events between logon and logoff. 5. If hive is corrupted, delete or rename the affected user profile VHD to force FSLogix to create a new one (backup first).
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/======VM&SCIM======/20.11 AVD  _ FSLogix 00000002 _ 80070002.md

symptom: FSLogix users in AVD can access other users profile folders in Azure File share. After mounting the file share path (obtained from registry), group members can browse to other users profile VHD containers.
rootCause: Incorrect NTFS permissions on the Azure File share. The share-level and NTFS permissions were not configured according to the recommended FSLogix profile container setup, allowing cross-user profile access.
solution: Follow the official documentation for FSLogix profile container file share setup: https://docs.azure.cn/en-us/virtual-desktop/create-file-share. Critical step: ensure proper NTFS permissions are applied so users can only access their own profile folders. Each user should have Modify permission only on their own profile folder, not the root share.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Mooncake Case Study/2204280060000964 - fslogix permission issue.md

symptom: AVD session host cannot be activated (Windows activation failure). Windows Application Event 12288 shows activation request failed. Network outbound IP is not an Azure IP address, so it is blocked by KMS proxy server.
rootCause: The outbound IP from the AVD session host is not recognized as an Azure IP by the KMS proxy server. This can happen when traffic is routed through a non-Azure egress point (e.g., on-premises firewall/proxy). KMS proxy requires the source IP to be an Azure IP to forward activation requests.
solution: 1. Check outbound IP from session host - must be an Azure IP for KMS activation to work. 2. Verify KMS proxy connectivity: check Jarvis logs for pseudo-VIP connection records. 3. A successful activation shows socket exception 10054 (expected - KMS completed activation and closed connection). 4. Find the pseudo-VIP for KMS proxy verification. 5. If traffic routes through non-Azure egress, configure routing to ensure KMS traffic (port 1688) goes through Azure gateway. Reference: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/virtual-machines/troubleshoot-activation-problems
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Mooncake Case Study/2205190060000380 - avd session host cannot be acti.md

symptom: Azure File Share failed to join on-premise AD using AzFilesHybrid module (v0.2.4). Join-AzStorageAccount command fails with error: Cannot find an object with name in domain. Storage account was joined to sub-domain but script runs in root domain context.
rootCause: AzFilesHybrid module Get-ADDomainInternal extracts the DNS root domain of the running account. When the storage account computer object is created in a sub-domain (e.g., sccn.swirecocacola.com) but Join-AzStorageAccount is executed with a root domain account (swirecocacola.com), the Get-AzStorageAccountADObject command searches the wrong domain and cannot find the object.
solution: Run Join-AzStorageAccount using an account that belongs to the same sub-domain where the storage account computer object should be created. In this case, use a sccn.swirecocacola.com domain account instead of the root domain swirecocacola.com account.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Mooncake Case Study/2209230040000656 - Azure File failed to join on-pr.md

symptom: AVD connection failure with error ConnectionFailedAdTrustedRelationshipFailure - session host lost trust relationship with domain controller. Adding domain user to Remote Desktop Users group via net localgroup or PsExec under SYSTEM context fails with: The trust relationship between this workstation and the primary domain failed. High latency (~500-600ms) observed between session host and on-premise AD.
rootCause: Session host machine lost trust relationship with Active Directory domain controller. The machine account password or secure channel between the session host and DC became invalid. High network latency to DC may have contributed to the issue.
solution: 1. Diagnose using Kusto DiagActivity+DiagError join to find ConnectionFailedAdTrustedRelationshipFailure errors. 2. Follow TSG: check if domain user can be added to RDU group manually (via Computer Management). 3. Test with PsExec -s (SYSTEM context): net localgroup "Remote Desktop Users" <user> /add. 4. If trust relationship is broken (error 1789), the resolution is to remove the session host from the domain and rejoin it. 5. Check AD connectivity: nltest /dsgetdc: /force and nltest /sc_verify:<domain>.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Mooncake Case Study/2303080040000846 _ Connection failure _ConnectionF.md

symptom: Remote Desktop client cannot subscribe to 21v AVD. Sign-in fails with error: AADSTS1400002 Request nonce is malformed. ETL trace shows TLS/SSL error: The remote certificate is invalid according to the validation procedure when connecting to rdweb.wvd.azure.cn. CAPI2 event logs show WVD certificate re-signed by 3rd party issuer (Digital Guardian).
rootCause: Third-party security tool (Digital Guardian) performs SSL inspection/injection, re-signing the WVD service certificates with its own CA. Remote Desktop client does not support SSL injection and rejects the modified certificate as invalid.
solution: Disable SSL inspection in the third-party security tool (Digital Guardian or similar) for AVD-related endpoints. Key diagnostic steps: 1. Collect ETL trace (cd $env:TEMP\DiagOutputDir\RdClientAutoTrace, tracerpt to csv). 2. Check CAPI2 event logs for certificate chain - if cert issuer is not Microsoft, SSL injection is occurring. 3. Compare certificate fingerprint with expected Microsoft certificate. 4. Whitelist AVD endpoints (rdweb.wvd.azure.cn, login.partner.microsoftonline.cn) from SSL inspection.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Mooncake Case Study/2303210040002568_ Remote Desktop client cannot sub.md

symptom: Unable to add MSIX package to AVD host pool via Azure Portal. Page spins and fails with error: subscriptionrequestthrottled - the write request has exceeded 1200 during 1:00:00. Browser trace shows nextLink pagination looping thousands of times during expand operation, hitting ARM request limits. Same MSIX works on one host pool but fails on another.
rootCause: Two issues: 1) Portal bug causing infinite nextLink pagination loop during MSIX package expand operation, hitting ARM throttling limits (confirmed by PG as portal bug, ICM 401942040). 2) Underlying VHD corruption causing Mount-DiskImage to succeed but Get-Partition returning empty AccessPaths/DeviceId, preventing MSIX package expansion.
solution: 1. If portal fails, try PowerShell (New-AzWvdMsixPackage) or REST API invoke method. 2. If PS/API also fails, test VHD manually: Mount-DiskImage -ImagePath <UNC> -PassThru -NoDriveLetter -Access ReadOnly, then Get-Partition - verify DeviceId is not empty. 3. If DeviceId is empty, the VHD is corrupted - recreate the MSIX VHD image from scratch. 4. Workaround: use expand result from a working host pool as parameter to add MSIX to the failing pool.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Mooncake Case Study/2306290010000551 AVD ab unable to add MSIX package.md

symptom: MSIX app not appearing in Start Menu when logging into AVD full desktop session after RemoteApp use. Repro steps: assign both RemoteApp (MSIX) and Desktop to same user on single session host → login desktop (no app in start menu) → logoff → login RemoteApp (app opens) → logoff RemoteApp → login desktop (app appears) → logoff → login desktop again (app gone again).
rootCause: MSIX on-demand registration race condition in shared session host pool. When user first logs into full desktop, MSIX packages are registered on-demand but the AppxDeploymentServer registration/deregistration cycle between RemoteApp and Desktop sessions causes the package to not be properly staged. GetAppAttachPackagesToStage returns numberOfPackages:0 during health check after RemoteApp deregistration, preventing proper re-staging on next desktop login.
solution: Under investigation. Workaround: ensure MSIX packages are assigned to separate host pools for RemoteApp vs Desktop, or use dedicated session hosts. Check AppxDeploymentServer event log (Microsoft-Windows-AppXDeploymentServer%4Operational.evtx) and Kusto RDOperation table for RegisterMsixPackages/DeregisterMsixPackages events to trace the registration lifecycle.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Mooncake Case Study/2410150040000576 AVD ABinBev MSIX cannot add to st.md

symptom: MSIX app does not appear in Start Menu when user logs into full desktop in AVD shared session host pool (FSLogix + MSIX app attach). Repro: assign both RemoteApp (MSIX) and Desktop to same user on single session host → login full desktop → no MSIX app in Start Menu. After launching RemoteApp separately then logging back into full desktop, the app briefly appears, but disappears again on next logoff/logon cycle.
rootCause: MSIX app registration/deregistration timing issue in shared host pools. When user logs off, DeregisterMsixWithPackageManager runs. On next full desktop login, OnDemandRegisterMsixWithPackageManager fires for the app, but GetAppAttachPackagesToStage returns numberOfPackages:0, meaning the staging health check does not detect packages to stage. The registration happens on-demand only when the specific app is launched, not proactively for Start Menu population in full desktop mode.
solution: Diagnostic steps: 1) Check AppXDeploymentServer Operational event log (Microsoft-Windows-AppXDeploymentServer%4Operational.evtx) on session host, search by app name for registration/deregistration events. 2) Use Kusto: cluster("rdskmc.chinaeast2.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn").database("WVD").RDOperation | where HostPool contains "<hostpool>" | where Name contains "packages" or Name contains "register" or Name contains "msix" or Name contains "stag" to trace the staging/registration sequence. 3) This is a known behavior: MSIX apps in shared host pools with both desktop and RemoteApp assignments may not reliably appear in Start Menu. Workaround: use RemoteApp-only assignment, or launch apps via alternative means (not Start Menu).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Mooncake Case Study/2410150040000576 AVD ABinBev MSIX cannot add to st.md

symptom: Multiple AVD users fail to load FSLogix profile, get temp profile with error: We cant sign in to your account / The User Profile Service service failed the sign-in.
rootCause: Golden image had RegistrySizeLimit registry key set to a very low value (224 MB), causing registry space exhaustion. Default is 4 GB.
solution: Delete the RegistrySizeLimit registry key so Windows uses the default 4GB allocated size. Check User Profile Service Operational event logs for the affected user SID.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## TS Workflow/FSLogix/Scenario based TSG.md

symptom: AVD FSLogix Cloud Cache slow logon (10+ minutes) with black screen. CloudCacheProvider logs show StorageIoTimeout exception.
rootCause: On-premises firewall device causing SMB packet loss between SMB client and SMB server, leading to StorageIoTimeout.
solution: Collect SMB ETL traces to identify packet loss. Fix firewall rules for SMB traffic. Verify CCD providers in same geo region.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## TS Workflow/FSLogix/Scenario based TSG.md

symptom: AVD user logoff takes 30+ minutes showing Waiting for FSLogix Apps Service message.
rootCause: Third-party driver (cwNep.sys) hanging during logoff, blocking FSLogix profile detach process.
solution: Identify and remove/update the 3rd party driver. Collect ProcMon + FSLogix profile logs + liveKD dump during logoff.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## TS Workflow/FSLogix/Scenario based TSG.md

symptom: AVD session host deployment completed but no session host appears in host pool. Application logs show connection failure between AVD agent and control plane.
rootCause: Outbound network restricted - AVD agent cannot reach broker. Or PS DSC extension failed to install AVD agent properly (Script-AddRdshServer.ps1 failure).
solution: Check: 1) ARM deployment status for errors. 2) Application event logs for WVD-Agent/RDAgentBootLoader. 3) C:\Windows\Temp\ScriptLog.log for DSC errors. 4) C:\Packages\Plugins\Microsoft.Powershell.DSC\Status�.status. 5) C:\WindowsAzure\Logs\waappagent for extension failures. 6) Ensure required outbound URLs are accessible.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## TS Workflow/Scenario/Deployment issue.md

symptom: USB camera/imaging device cannot be redirected to AVD session host running Windows Server OS. RemoteFX USB redirection not working.
rootCause: On Windows Server OS, the RDSH (Remote Desktop Session Host) role must be installed for RemoteFX USB device redirection to work. This is by design.
solution: 1) Install RDSH role on server (requires reboot). 2) On client: enable GPO 'Allow RDP redirection of other supported RemoteFX USB devices'. 3) Add registry keys under UsbSelectDeviceByInterfaces: Camera GUID {ca3e7ab9-b4c3-4ae6-8251-579ef933890f}, Image GUID {6bdd1fc6-810f-11d0-bec7-08002be2092f}. 4) Install device drivers on server if needed.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## TS Workflow/Scenario/Device redirection issue.md

symptom: FSLogix profile disk (VHDX) corruption error - user cannot load profile, FSLogix log shows disk corruption.
rootCause: VHDX file on storage (Azure Files/NetApp) is corrupted, preventing FSLogix from mounting the profile container.
solution: 1) Download the corrupted VHDX to local machine. 2) Mount the VHD locally. 3) Run chkdsk/scandisk to repair. 4) Upload repaired VHD back to storage account. Alternative: use frx migrate-vhd to create a new disk from the corrupted one.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## TS Workflow/Scenario/FSlogix profile disks disk corruption error.md

symptom: AVD Teams/apps running very slow. AVD Insights shows high P95 input delay.
rootCause: Input delay measures the time between user input and screen update. High input delay can be caused by: 1) Session host performance issues (CPU/memory/disk). 2) Network performance issues. 3) IO throttling on underlying Azure host.
solution: Check AVD Insights metrics: Green = focus on network perf. Red = focus on session host/process perf. Use Perfmon + Host Analyzer for session host issues. ProcMon for process-level slow issues. Log Analytics query: Perf table with ObjectName 'User Input Delay per Process'.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## TS Workflow/Scenario/AVD Insights.md

symptom: AVD agent cannot connect to broker through proxy. Session host shows unavailable in host pool due to proxy configuration not applied to LocalSystem account.
rootCause: Proxy is configured for user accounts but not for the LocalSystem/NetworkService accounts that the AVD agent runs under.
solution: Configure proxy for LocalSystem and NetworkService using bitsadmin: 'bitsadmin /util /setieproxy localsystem MANUAL_PROXY proxy:80 "<local>"' and 'bitsadmin /util /setieproxy networkservice MANUAL_PROXY proxy:80 "<local>"'. Verify with: 'bitsadmin /util /getieproxy localsystem'. Ref: docs.azure.cn/en-us/virtual-desktop/proxy-server-support
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## TS Workflow/Scenario/Proxy issue.md

symptom: Storage account AD domain join appears to succeed via Join-AzStorageAccount script but FSLogix authentication/permission issues occur afterwards.
rootCause: Common parameter mistakes in Join-AzStorageAccount: 1) EncryptionType uses wrong format like '<AES256|RC4|AES256,RC4>' instead of just 'AES256'. 2) SamAccountName includes trailing '$' (should be 'domain\computerName' not 'domain\computerName$'). The script runs without errors despite wrong parameters.
solution: Re-run Join-AzStorageAccount with correct parameters: EncryptionType='AES256' (recommended), SamAccountName without trailing '$'. Verify AD object created correctly. Ref: docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/files/storage-files-identity-ad-ds-enable
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## TS Workflow/Scenario/Storage Account Join AD Domain Issue.md

symptom: AVD session host marked as unhealthy/unavailable. Agent heartbeat mechanism: agent sends heartbeat to broker every 30s. Stack heartbeat: sends packet to client every 1s through gateway.
rootCause: Agent heartbeat: If broker misses multiple heartbeats, VM is marked unhealthy and new connections stop. Stack heartbeat: client misses 8 packets = warning, misses 8 more = disconnect and reconnect. Registry path: HKLM\Software\Microsoft\RDInfraAgent.
solution: Check agent heartbeat via Kusto (RDInfraTrace table, Category contains 'Heartbeat'). Verify network connectivity from session host to broker URL in registry. Default stack heartbeat: interval=1000ms, warn threshold=8, disconnect threshold=16.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## TS Workflow/Scenario/Heartbeat.md

symptom: AVD preview API versions being deprecated (tracking ID: PKYM-DVG). Only preview API versions on the published list are impacted. Azure CLI 2.75.0 uses API version 2021-07-12 which is NOT impacted. Portal operations are NOT impacted.
rootCause: Microsoft retiring preview API versions for Azure Virtual Desktop. Only specific preview versions on the published deprecation list are affected.
solution: 1. Check the deprecation tracking page: portal.azure.cn → Azure Health → PKYM-DVG. 2. Verify your automation/scripts do not use the deprecated preview API versions. 3. Azure CLI (2.75.0+) uses 2021-07-12 and is safe. 4. Portal operations are unaffected. 5. Migrate any custom ARM/REST calls to a supported GA API version.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/##AVD notification.md
